1.title: Preparing for School
paras: Every morning, Cindy prepares for school. She gathers up all her homework and any books that she needs for the day. She then packs it all into her backpack. She also packs her pencil case, a snack and a jacket, in case she gets cold during the day. By the time she is finished packing, the backpack is quite heavy, but she has everything she needs for the day.

When packed and ready to go, she slings the backpack over her shoulder and heads for the door. She must get tired of carrying that heavy bag around all the time. At least she doesn’t have to worry about getting enough exercise.
questions: What do you carry in your school bag every day?
What type of school bag do you prefer, a backpack or a regular book bag?
Do you get tired of carrying your school bag around?

vocab.exzh: 每天晚上睡前，湯姆喜歡先準備好隔天早上要穿的衣服。

1.translation: 每天早上辛蒂準備上學，她收好所有的作業和當天需要的書，然後把它們裝進背包。她也把鉛筆盒、點心，以及一件外套放進去，以免白天的時候會冷。等她裝好之後，背包已經很重了，不過她已經有了當天所需要的每樣東西。 當她裝好書包準備上學時，她把背包掛在肩膀上，然後朝門的方向走去。對於老是得背那麼重的背包到處走，她一定感到很厭煩，不過，至少她不用擔心運動量不夠的問題。
answers: I usually carry a pencil case and any books I need for the day in my school bag.
I prefer a backpack because it is safer to carry when I ride my bike.
Yes, I’m tired of carrying my school bag around.
paras_zh: 每天早上，辛蒂都會為上學做準備。她把所有的家庭作業和當天需要的書都收集起來，然後把這些東西全部裝進她的背包裡。她也會裝進她的鉛筆盒、一份點心，還有一件外套，以免她在白天覺得冷。等她整理完畢時，背包已經相當重了，但她已經帶齊了當天所需要的一切。
整理好準備出門後，她把背包甩上肩膀，朝門口走去。她一定對整天背著這麼重的包包感到厭煩。至少她不用擔心運動量不夠。

quiz.q: According to the passage, what is one advantage of carrying such a heavy backpack every day?
options: It helps her make new friends
It saves her money on the bus
She does not have to worry about getting enough exercise
It makes her walk to school faster

1.title: Jason Wipes His Nose
paras: Jason has a runny nose. He reaches for a box of tissue on the table so that he can wipe it. His mother told him that he should use tissue and not the sleeve of his shirt because that is very disgusting. A single tissue is all he needs and he plucks one out of the box.

First he wipes his nose, but it still feels plugged so he covers his nose with the tissue and blows. It sounds like he is playing the trumpet. After doing this a couple of times, his nose is cleared and he can breathe much better.

Jason is a slob. 16  He leaves the germ-laden, 18 dirty tissue on the table.  This is not the most hygienic of practices as someone else could catch his cold by touching it, but Jason is lazy and doesn’t care. His mother is always picking up after him. Will he ever learn?
questions: Should you wipe your nose on your sleeve?
How does Jason clear his nose so that he can breathe better?
What should you do with a dirty tissue?

1.translation: 傑森流鼻涕了，他伸手去拿桌子上的面紙來擦鼻涕。他的媽媽以前就告訴他應該要用面紙擦鼻涕，而不是用襯衫的袖子，因為那樣是非常噁心的。他只需要一張面紙就夠了，他從盒子中抽出一張。 一開始，他先擦他的鼻子，但仍然感覺鼻塞，所以他用面紙掩住鼻子，並用力的吹氣，聲音聽起來很像在吹小喇叭。當他擤個兩三次之後，他的鼻子通了，而且呼吸也順暢多了。 傑森是個不愛乾淨的人，他把充滿細菌的面紙留在桌子上。這可不是個最衛生的習慣，因為其他的人若接觸到的話，可能會染上他的感冒。由於他太懶了，所以他並不在乎。他的媽媽總是幫他收拾善後。他以後會學乖嗎？
answers: No, because it’s disgusting.
He blows his nose with a tissue.
You should throw it in the garbage can.
paras_zh: 傑森流鼻涕了。他伸手去拿桌上的一盒衛生紙，好把鼻涕擦掉。他的媽媽告訴過他，應該用衛生紙，而不是用襯衫的袖子，因為那樣非常噁心。他只需要一張衛生紙就夠了，於是他從盒子裡抽出一張。
他先擦了擦鼻子，但鼻子還是覺得塞住，於是他用衛生紙蓋住鼻子，用力吹氣。聽起來就像他在吹小喇叭一樣。這樣做了幾次之後，他的鼻子通了，呼吸也順暢多了。
傑森是個邋遢鬼。他把充滿細菌、髒兮兮的衛生紙留在桌上。這樣做很不衛生，因為別人可能因為碰到它而被傳染感冒，但傑森很懶，根本不在乎。他的媽媽總是在他身後幫他收拾。他到底什麼時候才會學乖呢？

quiz.q: Why does Jason's mother tell him not to wipe his nose on his sleeve?

1.title: Wendell’s Big Bubble Trouble
paras: Wendell’s grandmother always brings a treat for him when she comes to visit. This time she has brought him a pack of gum. Wendell thanks his grandma and then unwraps a piece of the bubblegum and stuffs it into his mouth. It is grape flavor, which is his favorite.

As he chews his big wad of gum, his little sister, Lily, asks if she can have some, too. Wendell is a very selfish boy and doesn’t like to share. He continues to chew his gum and blow bubbles as Lily looks on with envy. She is very sad. Eventually, she gives up and goes crying to her mother.

Meanwhile, Wendell keeps blowing bubbles as if nothing had ever happened. He couldn’t care less about anyone’s feelings other than his own. Then just as he is blowing the biggest bubble of his life, it pops in his face. The sticky gum flies everywhere. It’s on the floor, on his clothes, on his face and even in his hair. Perhaps after treating his sister so poorly, karma has snuck up behind him and burst his bubble.
questions: What treat did Wendell’s grandmother bring for him?
Does Wendell share his treat with Lily?
Where does the gum go when the big bubble pops?

1.translation: 溫德爾的祖母來訪時，總會帶點零嘴點心給他。這回她帶給溫德爾的是一條口香糖。他向祖母謝謝後，隨即打開一塊泡泡糖，並且將它塞進嘴裡。這塊泡泡糖是葡萄口味的，這是他最愛的口味。 當他咀嚼著大塊的口香糖時，他的小妹莉莉問他可不可以給她一些。溫德爾是個非常自私的男孩，他不喜歡和別人分享。儘管莉莉在旁邊羨慕的看著，他仍繼續咀嚼他的口香糖，並且還吹著泡泡。她非常難過，最後她只好放棄，跑去向媽媽哭訴。 這時溫德爾若無其事地繼續吹著泡泡。他只在乎自己，完全不理會別人的感受。正當他吹著有生以來最大的泡泡時，泡泡砰的一聲在他臉上破掉了。黏黏的口香糖飛得到處都是，沾在地板、衣服、臉上，甚至沾在他的頭髮上。也許這是因為他對妹妹很不好，所以招致惡果偷偷溜到他的背後將泡泡戳破。(註：此處「惡果」採擬人化用法。)
answers: She brought a pack of gum for him.
No, because he is a very selfish boy.
It’s on the floor, on his clothes, on his face and even in his hair.
paras_zh: 溫德爾的奶奶每次來看他，總會帶一份好吃的東西給他。這一次，她帶來了一包口香糖。溫德爾向奶奶道謝，接著打開一片泡泡糖，把它塞進嘴裡。是葡萄口味的，正是他最喜歡的口味。
當他嚼著那一大團口香糖時，他的小妹妹莉莉也問她是否可以吃一點。溫德爾是個非常自私的男孩，不喜歡分享。他繼續嚼著口香糖、吹著泡泡，莉莉在一旁羨慕地看著。她非常難過。最後，她放棄了，跑去向媽媽哭訴。
與此同時，溫德爾繼續吹著泡泡，彷彿什麼事都沒發生過一樣。除了自己的感受之外，他一點也不在乎任何人的感受。就在他吹出他這輩子最大的一個泡泡時，泡泡在他臉上砰的一聲爆裂了。黏黏的口香糖噴得到處都是，地板上、衣服上、臉上，甚至頭髮裡都是。也許是因為他這麼惡劣地對待妹妹，因果報應悄悄地找上了他，弄破了他的泡泡。

1.title: Who’s at the Door?
paras: Buzz! The doorbell is ringing. Myles wonders who it is as he races for the intercom.
“Who is it?” he inquires.
“Pizza delivery,” a strange voice replies.
“I didn’t order any pizza,” Myles claims.  “Are you sure you have the right address?”
“Is this number 63 Julin Street?” he asks.
“Uh, yeah.”
“Then I have ten pepperoni pizzas for you,” the voice insists.
“There must be a mistake! I didn’t order any pizza!” Myles argues.
The voice on the other end breaks into a roar of laughter.
“Gotcha!” Carl shouts.
“Carl, you idiot!” Myles says, breathing a sigh of relief.  “You really had me going.”
“Yeah, I know,” Carl snorts. “Buzz me up so we can watch TV.”
Myles presses a button on the intercom and the front door unlocks. He then runs to hide so that he can pay back Carl by scaring him.
questions: Does Myles know who is at the door?
Who does his friend claim to be?
Why does Myles run and hide after he unlocks the door?

vocab.exzh: 我們點了一份大披薩,上面放了義大利辣味香腸和起司。

vocab.ex: He continued to insist that he had ordered the pizza himself.
vocab.exzh: 他仍然堅持說是自己訂的披薩。

vocab.ex: The two brothers began to argue about whose turn it was.
vocab.exzh: 兩兄弟開始爭論輪到誰了。

vocab.ex: There was silence on the other end of the phone line.
vocab.exzh: 電話線的另一端一片寂靜。

vocab.ex: The whole class suddenly broke into laughter.
vocab.exzh: 全班同學突然大笑了起來。

vocab.ex: A roar of laughter filled the room after the joke.
vocab.exzh: 笑話講完後,房間裡充滿了大笑聲。

vocab.ex: The sound of laughter came from the kitchen.
vocab.exzh: 廚房傳來笑聲。

vocab.ex: He paused to breathe deeply before answering the door.
vocab.exzh: 他在應門之前停下來深呼吸。

vocab.ex: She let out a sigh when she realized it was just a joke.
vocab.exzh: 當她發現這只是個玩笑時,她發出了一聲嘆息。

vocab.ex: He felt great relief once he knew it was only a prank.
vocab.exzh: 當他知道這只是惡作劇時,他感到非常輕鬆。

vocab.ex: He began to snort when he saw his friend's shocked face.
vocab.exzh: 當他看到朋友驚訝的表情時,他嘲諷地大笑了起來。

vocab.ex: Please press the button to open the gate.
vocab.exzh: 請按下按鈕以打開大門。

vocab.ex: She used her key to unlock the front door.
vocab.exzh: 她用鑰匙打開了前門。

vocab.ex: Myles wanted to pay back Carl for the scare he gave him.
vocab.exzh: 麥爾斯想要報復卡爾嚇他的那一次。

1.translation: 鈴...！門鈴響起。麥爾斯跑去接對講機時，心裡在想到底會是誰呢？ 「請問是哪位？」他問。 「送披薩的，」一個陌生的聲音回答他。 麥爾斯說：「我沒有訂任何的披薩，你確定住址正確嗎？」 「這是竹林街63號嗎？」他問。 「嗯，是的！」 「那我有十個義大利辣味香腸披薩要給你，」這個人堅持地說。 「一定是出了差錯，我沒有訂任何的披薩啊！」麥爾斯辯稱。 對講機的另一頭傳出了一陣爆笑聲。 「上當了！」卡爾大叫。 「卡爾你這個白癡，」麥爾斯鬆了一口氣說：「我真的被你騙了。」 「是啊，我知道，」卡爾嘲諷地大笑。「幫我開門吧，我們一起看電視。」 麥爾斯按了對講機的按鈕，前門就打開了。接著他跑去躲起來，以便要嚇嚇卡爾來報復他。
answers: No, he doesn’t know who is at the door.
His friend claims to be a pizza delivery boy.
He runs to hide because he wants to scare Carl.
paras_zh: 叮咚!門鈴響了。麥爾斯一邊納悶是誰,一邊衝去接對講機。
「是誰呀?」他問道。
「披薩外送。」一個陌生的聲音回答。
「我沒有訂披薩啊,」麥爾斯說。「你確定地址沒錯嗎?」
「這裡是朱林街63號嗎?」他問。
「那我這裡有十份義大利辣味香腸披薩要給你。」那個聲音堅持說。
「一定是搞錯了!我沒有訂披薩!」麥爾斯爭辯道。
電話另一端的聲音突然爆出一陣大笑。
「騙到你了!」卡爾大喊。
「卡爾,你這個白痴!」麥爾斯鬆了一口氣說。「你真的把我唬得一愣一愣的。」
「對啊,我知道,」卡爾嗤笑著說。「幫我開門,我們好一起看電視。」
麥爾斯按下對講機上的按鈕,前門的鎖打開了。接著他跑去躲起來,好嚇卡爾一跳作為報復。

quiz.q: Why does the caller say he has ten pepperoni pizzas?
options: He delivered to the wrong apartment
Myles secretly ordered them earlier
The pizza shop made a real mistake
He is playing a trick on Myles

quiz.q: How does Myles feel after he finds out who is really at the door?
options: Angry and confused
Frightened and worried
Relieved and amused
Bored and tired

quiz.q: What does Myles plan to do after he unlocks the door for Carl?
options: Order real pizza for both of them
Hide so he can scare Carl back
Call the police about the prank
Refuse to let Carl into the house

1.title: Naptime at School
paras: The teacher in this classroom must be extremely boring, as all the children have fallen asleep. Actually, it is part of the daily routine for most Taiwanese school children. From 12:00 till 12:30 they eat their lunch. This is followed by a nap that lasts until about 1:20. Some teachers believe that by having the children take a short nap after lunch they will be refreshed and ready to learn for the second half of the day.  　　The standard position seems to be hunched over with your head resting on folded arms. Some students say that this is a very uncomfortable position and claim that it gives them sore arms. Others say that being hunched over while having a full belly makes them feel a bit sick. Regardless of 22 whether or not they like it, it is the policy of some teachers. I hope those students who don’t like it get used to it, as there is no room in the classroom or the school budget for dozens of 28 beds.
questions: Why are all the children sleeping?
What is the problem with sleeping hunched over?
Why can’t the school buy beds?

vocab.ex: After lunch, it is naptime for the children in the classroom.
vocab.exzh: 吃完午餐後，就是教室裡孩子們的午休時間。

vocab.ex: The math test was extremely difficult for the students.
vocab.exzh: 這次數學考試對學生來說極度困難。

vocab.ex: She left early, as she had a headache.
vocab.exzh: 由於頭痛，她提早離開了。

vocab.ex: It did not take long for the tired boy to fall asleep.
vocab.exzh: 這個疲累的男孩沒過多久就睡著了。

vocab.ex: He looks stern, but he is actually a very kind teacher.
vocab.exzh: 他看起來很嚴肅，但事實上是個很和善的老師。

vocab.ex: Brushing your teeth should be part of your daily routine.
vocab.exzh: 刷牙應該是你每日例行公事的一部分。

vocab.ex: Waking up at six every morning is part of his routine.
vocab.exzh: 每天早上六點起床是他例行公事的一部分。

vocab.ex: The students studied till nine o'clock at night.
vocab.exzh: 學生們一直讀書到晚上九點。

vocab.ex: A short rest will follow the lunch break.
vocab.exzh: 午休之後會接著一段短暫的休息時間。

vocab.ex: The nap usually lasts about forty minutes.
vocab.exzh: 午睡通常會持續大約四十分鐘。

vocab.ex: A short nap can refresh tired students.
vocab.exzh: 短暫的午睡能讓疲累的學生重新提起精神。

vocab.ex: Wearing a uniform is the standard practice at most schools.
vocab.exzh: 穿制服是大多數學校的標準做法。

vocab.ex: He fell asleep in an odd sitting position.
vocab.exzh: 他以一個奇怪的坐姿睡著了。

vocab.ex: The student sat hunched over his desk during the nap.
vocab.exzh: 這個學生午睡時弓著身體趴在書桌上。

vocab.ex: She let her head rest on her folded arms.
vocab.exzh: 她讓頭靠在交叉的手臂上。

vocab.ex: He slept with his head on his folded arms.
vocab.exzh: 他把頭靠在交叉的手臂上睡覺。

vocab.ex: Sleeping in a chair can be quite uncomfortable.
vocab.exzh: 坐在椅子上睡覺可能相當不舒服。

vocab.ex: Some students claim that naptime gives them a sore neck.
vocab.exzh: 有些學生宣稱午休時間讓他們脖子痠痛。

vocab.ex: My arms feel sore after resting my head on them for so long.
vocab.exzh: 我的手臂靠著頭這麼久後感覺很痠。

vocab.ex: It is hard to bend over with a full belly.
vocab.exzh: 肚子吃得很飽時很難彎腰。

vocab.ex: The teacher expects everyone to nap, regardless of whether they are tired.
vocab.exzh: 不管累不累，老師都要求每個人午睡。

vocab.ex: I am not sure whether the students like naptime or not.
vocab.exzh: 我不確定學生們是否喜歡午休時間。

vocab.ex: It is the school's policy that all students take a nap after lunch.
vocab.exzh: 所有學生午餐後都要午睡是這所學校的政策。

vocab.ex: It took her a while to get used to sleeping at her desk.
vocab.exzh: 她花了一段時間才習慣趴在書桌上睡覺。

vocab.ex: There is not enough room in the classroom for beds.
vocab.exzh: 教室裡沒有足夠的空間放床。

vocab.ex: The school does not have the budget to buy new beds.
vocab.exzh: 學校沒有預算可以買新床。

vocab.ex: There are dozens of students in this school who take a nap every day.
vocab.exzh: 這所學校裡有幾十個學生每天都要午睡。

1.translation: 這間教室的老師上課一定極為無聊，因為所有的小朋友都睡著了。事實上，這是大多數台灣學校小朋友每天例行公事的一部份。十二點到十二點半他們吃午餐，之後的休息持續到大約一點二十分。一些老師相信，讓這些學生吃完午餐後稍微小睡一下，可以讓他們恢復精神，然後準備好學習下午的課程。 午睡的標準姿勢似乎是趴著，然後頭靠在交叉的手臂上面。有些學生說這是非常不舒服的姿勢，並宣稱這個姿勢會讓手臂痠痛。其他學生則說肚子飽飽的時候趴著睡，會讓他們覺得有一點不舒服。不管他們喜不喜歡，這是一些老師的規定。我希望那些不喜歡的學生能夠適應它，因為教室沒有空間，或者學校也沒有預算能夠買幾十張床放在教室。
answers: Because it’s a part of their daily routine.
It’s a very uncomfortable position.
Because there’s no budget. Besides, there is no room in the classroom.
paras_zh: 這間教室的老師一定無聊透頂，因為所有的孩子都睡著了。事實上，這是大多數台灣學童每日例行公事的一部分。從十二點到十二點半，他們吃午餐。接著是一段一直持續到大約一點二十分的午休時間。有些老師認為，讓孩子午餐後小睡一下，能讓他們重新提起精神，準備好迎接下半天的學習。標準的午休姿勢似乎是弓著身體，把頭靠在交叉的手臂上。有些學生說這個姿勢非常不舒服，還說會讓他們的手臂痠痛。也有人說，吃飽後又弓著身體，會讓他們覺得有點噁心。不管他們喜不喜歡，這都是某些老師的政策。我希望那些不喜歡這樣的學生能漸漸習慣，因為教室或學校的預算都沒有空間可以擺放幾十張床。

quiz.q: According to the passage, what happens right before naptime?
options: The children eat their lunch
The children go outside to play
The children take a test
The children clean the classroom

quiz.q: Why do some teachers have children take a nap after lunch?
options: So the teacher can also rest
Because it is required by law
Because the classroom is too noisy otherwise
So the children will be refreshed for the second half of the day

quiz.q: Why does the writer say beds are not an option for naptime?
options: The students prefer sleeping at their desks
Beds are not comfortable enough
There is no room or budget for beds
The school already has enough beds

1.title: The School Library
paras: Students borrow a lot of books from libraries. Whether you are researching a topic for your homework or just need a book to relax with for the weekend, you can just check out a book from your school’s library.  You don’t even have to pay for it.

These students are browsing the shelves for a book, but there are so many to choose from that finding a book can be difficult.  All the books are organized into categories or alphabetized by author or title.  Still there are times when you just can’t find that specific book you’re looking for. In these instances, try using the library’s database to speed up your search.  It could save you a lot of time by locating the book for you.

If you decide to take a book home for a while, you will need to check it out. Simply take the book to the librarian. They will scan the book and your library card into the computer. This will give them a record of who has the book and when it is due back. Make sure your book comes back on time. In public libraries you can be charged a fee for any overdue books. If it happens too often, they may revoke your library card.
questions: What are some of the reasons students borrow books?
Why is a database useful for finding a book?
How do you check a book out?

vocab.ex: Whether you like it or not, you still have to finish your homework.
vocab.exzh: 不管你喜不喜歡，你還是得完成你的作業。

vocab.ex: She spent the whole afternoon at the library to research her science project.
vocab.exzh: 她花了整個下午在圖書館研究她的科學專題。

vocab.ex: Our teacher let us choose our own topic for the essay.
vocab.exzh: 我們的老師讓我們自己選擇作文的主題。

vocab.ex: After a long week of tests, I like to relax by reading a good book.
vocab.exzh: 經過漫長的一週考試後，我喜歡靠讀一本好書來放鬆。

vocab.ex: I want to check out this mystery novel for the weekend.
vocab.exzh: 我想借出這本推理小說在週末看。

vocab.ex: We like to browse the shelves whenever we visit the bookstore.
vocab.exzh: 我們每次去書店都喜歡瀏覽書架。

vocab.ex: The library’s shelves are full of books on every subject.
vocab.exzh: 圖書館的架子上擺滿了各種主題的書。

vocab.ex: It is hard to choose just one book from so many great options.
vocab.exzh: 要從這麼多不錯的選項中只選一本書真的很難。

vocab.ex: The librarian worked hard to organize all the new books on the cart.
vocab.exzh: 圖書館員很努力把手推車上所有的新書整理好。

vocab.ex: Mystery novels belong to a very popular category at our school library.
vocab.exzh: 推理小說在我們學校圖書館裡是很受歡迎的一個種類。

vocab.ex: The librarian asked us to alphabetize the returned books by author.
vocab.exzh: 圖書館員要我們把歸還的書按作者姓氏字母順序排列。

vocab.ex: My favorite author just published a new adventure story.
vocab.exzh: 我最喜歡的作者剛出版了一本新的冒險故事。

vocab.ex: I forgot the title of the book, so I asked the librarian for help.
vocab.exzh: 我忘記那本書的書名了，所以我請圖書館員幫忙。

vocab.ex: I am looking for one specific book about ancient Egypt.
vocab.exzh: 我正在找一本關於古埃及的特定書籍。

vocab.ex: In this instance, the database can help you find the book faster.
vocab.exzh: 在這種情況下，資料庫可以幫你更快找到那本書。

vocab.ex: You can search the library’s database to see if a book is available.
vocab.exzh: 你可以搜尋圖書館的資料庫來看某本書是否可借閱。

vocab.ex: Using the computer system can speed up your search for a book.
vocab.exzh: 使用電腦系統可以加速你找書的過程。

vocab.ex: My search for the missing book took almost an hour.
vocab.exzh: 我尋找那本遺失的書花了將近一個小時。

vocab.ex: The database helped me locate the book on the third shelf.
vocab.exzh: 資料庫幫我找出了那本書在第三層架子上。

vocab.ex: I plan to keep this novel for a while before returning it.
vocab.exzh: 我打算把這本小說留一段時間之後再歸還。

vocab.ex: To borrow a book, simply bring it to the librarian with your card.
vocab.exzh: 要借書，只要把書和你的借書證拿給圖書館員就好了。

vocab.ex: The librarian scanned my book and my library card together.
vocab.exzh: 圖書館員把我的書和借書證一起掃描了。

vocab.ex: The machine will scan the barcode on the back of the book.
vocab.exzh: 這台機器會掃描書背後的條碼。

vocab.ex: The library keeps a record of every book you have borrowed.
vocab.exzh: 圖書館會保留你借過每一本書的記錄。

vocab.ex: This book is due back at the library next Monday.
vocab.exzh: 這本書下週一到期，必須還給圖書館。

vocab.ex: The public library will charge you a small fee for a lost book.
vocab.exzh: 公共圖書館會針對遺失的書向你收取一筆小額費用。

vocab.ex: I had to pay a fee because I returned the book late.
vocab.exzh: 因為我還書遲了，所以必須付一筆費用。

vocab.ex: My book is three days overdue, so I need to pay a fine.
vocab.exzh: 我的書已經過期三天了，所以我需要繳罰款。

vocab.ex: The library may revoke your card if you have too many overdue books.
vocab.exzh: 如果你有太多過期未還的書，圖書館可能會取消你的借書證。

1.translation: 學生從圖書館借很多書，不管你是為了寫作業而研究一個主題，或者只是想利用週末看書來放鬆心情，你都可以到學校的圖書館借書，甚至不用花你任何錢呢！ 這些學生正在瀏覽書架找書，但是因為有太多的書可供選擇，所以要找到一本書可能很困難。所有的書都有分類編排，或依照作者的姓名或書名的字母順序排列。然而，有時候你還是會找不到某本你要的書。遇到這種情況，你可以試著使用圖書館的資料庫來加快查詢速度；它可以找出書的位置，為你節省很多時間。 如果你決定要把書帶回家看一陣子，你必須要辦理借閱手續。只要把書交給圖書館員，他們就會把這本書以及你借書證的資料掃描到電腦中。這樣一來，他們便會有這本書被誰借走以及何時到期的記錄。請務必準時歸還書籍，在公立圖書館，如果你逾期還書可能會被罰錢。如果這種情形經常發生的話，他們也許會取消你的借書證。
answers: They borrow books to do their homework or to research things for papers.
It is useful because it can speed up your search.
You check it out by taking it to the librarian.
paras_zh: 學生們從圖書館借了很多書。不論你是在為作業研究某個主題，還是只是想找一本書在週末放鬆一下，你都可以直接向學校的圖書館借書。你甚至不需要為此付費。
這些學生正在書架間瀏覽尋找一本書，但是可以選擇的書實在太多了，要找到一本書可能很困難。所有的書都依照種類分類，或是依作者姓名或書名按字母順序排列。即便如此，有時候你還是找不到你要找的那本特定的書。在這種情況下，可以試著使用圖書館的資料庫來加快你的搜尋速度。這可以幫你省下很多找書的時間。
如果你決定把一本書帶回家一段時間，你就需要辦理借閱手續。只要把書拿給圖書館員就行了。他們會把書和你的借書證一起掃描進電腦裡。這樣就會留下記錄，顯示誰借了這本書，以及什麼時候該還書。務必要準時把書還回去。在公共圖書館，如果書逾期未還，你可能會被收取費用。如果這種情況發生得太頻繁，他們可能會取消你的借書證。

quiz.q: According to the passage, what are two reasons students might borrow a book from the library?
options: To sell it later or to give it as a gift
To research a homework topic or to relax with over the weekend
To decorate their room or to practice handwriting
To use as a doorstop or to press flowers

quiz.q: Why might a student have trouble finding a book even though the books are organized by category and alphabetized?
options: Because there are so many books to choose from
Because the library does not allow students to touch the shelves
Because the library only has one copy of each book
Because the librarian hides certain books

quiz.q: What happens after the librarian scans a book and the student's library card?
options: The student must immediately read the book in the library
The book is removed from the library's collection
The student receives a new library card
A record is created showing who has the book and when it is due

1.title: Grocery Shopping
paras: On her way home from school, Jenny decided she needed to stop at the local grocery store. As she entered, she looked around and found a shopping basket. She placed the basket on a cart to make a mini-shopping cart. It was easier for her to push the cart rather than carry the basket. The aisles were organized neatly and Jenny pushed the cart down each one to find what she needed. Each item she wanted she took off the shelf and placed in the shopping cart. Jenny, being a bargain hunter, preferred the items that were on sale. Her thriftiness allows her to save a lot of money whenever she shops.

After filling the basket, she lined up at the checkout. When it was her turn, she emptied the contents of the basket onto the counter. The cashier scanned each item. A couple items wouldn’t scan correctly so the cashier had to key in the bar code manually. When the cashier had finished scanning everything, she hit the total button. Jenny handed her a five hundred dollar bill. The cashier checked the bill to make sure it wasn’t counterfeit and then slipped it into the cash register. She then counted out the change and handed it back to Jenny. Jenny thanked her, took her bag of groceries and headed for home.
questions: Where was Jenny going when she decided to stop and buy groceries?
Why does Jenny prefer items that are on sale?
What was the purpose for checking the bill?

1.translation: 在從學校返家的途中，珍妮決定必須去一趟當地的雜貨店。當她一進門時，四周看了一下，並找到了一個購物籃。她把它放在推車上，就變成了一台迷你購物車。對她來說，推購物車比提籃子還要輕鬆。走道的東西排放地非常整齊。珍妮推著購物車，一排一排地尋找她需要的東西。她把想買的每樣東西從架上拿下來，然後放入購物車中。由於珍妮是一個喜歡購買便宜貨的人，所以她偏好特價商品。由於她的節儉，讓她每次購物時都能節省很多的錢。 當籃子裝滿東西後，她便到收銀台排隊結帳。輪到她的時候，她把籃子的東西全部拿到櫃台上面。收銀員將每件商品一一掃描，但是其中有一兩樣無法正確地掃描，所以收銀員必須用手輸入條碼。當她把每樣東西都掃描過後，便按了合計的按鍵。珍妮拿了一張五百元的鈔票給她，收銀員便檢查這張鈔票，以確認是否為偽鈔，接著她就把它塞入收銀機。她算出要找的零錢，然後把它交給了珍妮。珍妮對她說聲謝謝，便提著一包雜貨回家。
answers: She was going home.
Because she is a bargain hunter.
The purpose was to make sure it wasn’t counterfeit.
paras_zh: 珍妮放學回家的路上，決定要去附近的食品雜貨店買點東西。她一進門，就四處張望，找到了一個購物籃。她把籃子放到推車上，做成一台迷你購物車。這樣推著車比提著籃子要輕鬆多了。走道排列得整整齊齊，珍妮沿著每一條走道推車尋找她需要的東西。每次看到想要的商品，她就把它從架上拿下來放進購物車裡。珍妮是個喜歡購買便宜貨的人，她偏好那些正在特價的商品。她的節儉讓她每次購物都能省下不少錢。
裝滿購物籃後，她排隊到結帳櫃檯。輪到她時，她把籃子裡的東西全部倒在櫃檯上。收銀員一項一項地掃描商品。有幾樣商品掃不出來，收銀員只好手動輸入條碼。等收銀員把所有商品都掃描完後，她按下了合計鍵。珍妮遞給她一張五百美元的鈔票。收銀員檢查了這張鈔票，確認不是偽鈔，然後把它放進收銀機裡。接著她點好零錢，交還給珍妮。珍妮向她道謝，拿起她的雜貨袋，往家的方向走去。

She used two baskets at the same time
She placed the basket on a cart to make a mini-shopping cart
She left her basket at the entrance and carried items by hand

She gave the bill back to Jenny
She called the store manager
She asked Jenny for a different bill

1.title: Dad’s New Car
paras: My dad bought a brand-new car yesterday. It is the coolest car ever. It has everything you could want, like nice soft seats, air-conditioning, power windows, and even a DVD player in the back seat. Silver wouldn’t have been my first choice for a color, but then again I didn’t have to pay for it, so I guess it’s ok.

I like the new car so much that I am going to ask my parents if I can live in it. That would be so cool. It has everything I could possibly want—except for maybe a bathroom. I can see myself sitting in it during a hot summer day, watching a movie, sipping a drink and cooling off with the air-conditioning blasting away. Life couldn’t get much better than that.

My little sister really likes the new car, too, but not as much as me. Her favorite part is the sunroof. Dad says we aren’t allowed to stick out our heads while the car is moving or they’ll be chopped off, but we still like to do it when the car is parked.

I am actually looking forward to long trips now. I used to hate them, but then again our old car was a piece of junk.  Whenever we went on a trip, the car would overheat and we weren’t allowed to use the air-con because that only made it heat up faster. We shouldn’t have to worry about that anymore;23 plus, we can watch movies. It’s like a dream come true.
questions: What is in the back seat of the car?
What doesn’t the car have that the boy might like?
Why couldn’t they use the air-con in the old car?

vocab.ex: She was so proud of her brand-new bicycle.
vocab.exzh: 她對自己那台全新的腳踏車感到非常驕傲。

vocab.ex: We turned on the air-conditioning because the room was too hot.

vocab.ex: The classroom was empty except for one student cleaning the board.

vocab.ex: I did not think I would like the movie, but I actually loved it.
vocab.exzh: 我原本以為自己不會喜歡這部電影，但我其實真的很喜歡。

vocab.ex: We look forward to visiting our grandparents every summer.
vocab.exzh: 我們每年夏天都期待去拜訪祖父母。

vocab.ex: I used to be afraid of the dark when I was little.

vocab.exzh: 我們清理了車庫，丟掉了一堆舊雜物。

vocab.exzh: 這台舊電腦如果運轉太久就會過熱。

vocab.ex: The engine started to heat up after the long drive uphill.

vocab.ex: You do not need to worry about the test; you studied hard.
vocab.exzh: 你不需要擔心這場考試，你已經很認真讀書了。

vocab.ex: The new bike is faster; plus, it is much lighter than my old one.
vocab.exzh: 這台新腳踏車比較快；此外，它也比我的舊腳踏車輕很多。

1.translation: 昨天我爸買了一台全新的車子。它是有史以來最酷的車子。任何你可能想要的配備它都有了，像是精巧又柔軟的座椅、空調、電動車窗，甚至後座還有DVD播放器。就顏色而言，銀色並不是我的第一選擇，但是話說回來，買車的錢並不是我出的，所以我想這是可以接受的。 我非常喜歡這台新車，喜歡到我想問我的父母是否可以住在這台車子裡面，那將會是很棒的。任何我可能想要的配備都在裡面了，也許除了廁所之外吧！我可以想像夏天的時候，我坐在裡面看電影、喝飲料，還吹著涼爽的冷氣。人生中沒有比這樣更美好的了！ 我的小妹也很喜歡這台車，但是沒有像我這麼喜歡。她最喜歡的部份是天窗。 爸爸說：當車子開動的時候，不准我們把頭伸出車外，不然頭會被切斷。但是，當車子停好時，我們仍然喜歡把頭伸出去。 我真的很期待去長途旅行。以前我很討厭長途旅行，話說回來，這是因為我們的舊車就像垃圾一樣爛，坐起來很不舒服。每次我們去旅行，車子都會過熱，而且我們不能使用空調，因為這會使溫度上升的更快。我們應該再也不必擔心這個問題了。此外，我們還可以看電影。這就像是美夢成真啊！
answers: A DVD player is in the back seat of the car.
It doesn’t have a bathroom.
Because the old car would overheat.
paras_zh: 我爸爸昨天買了一輛全新的車。這是有史以來最酷的車。它擁有你想要的一切，像是柔軟舒適的座椅、空調、電動車窗，後座甚至還有一台 DVD 播放器。銀色本來不會是我第一個選擇的顏色，但話說回來，反正錢也不是我付的，所以我想這樣也還好。
我太喜歡這輛新車了，我打算問問我爸媽，我可不可以住在裡面。那該有多酷啊。它幾乎擁有我想要的一切——除了可能少了一間浴室。我可以想像自己在炎熱的夏日坐在車裡，看著電影，啜飲著飲料，同時讓空調強力地吹送涼風降溫。人生大概沒有比這更美好的了。
我妹妹也非常喜歡這輛新車，不過沒有我那麼喜歡。她最喜歡的部分是天窗。爸爸說，車子行駛時我們不准把頭伸出去，不然頭會被削掉，但只要車子停著，我們還是喜歡把頭伸出去玩。
現在我其實還滿期待長途旅行的。我以前很討厭這種旅行，不過話說回來，我們的舊車根本是一堆破銅爛鐵。每次我們出去旅行，車子就會過熱，而我們又不能開空調，因為那樣只會讓車子更快發燙。我們現在應該不用再擔心這種事了；此外，我們還可以看電影。這簡直就像美夢成真一樣。

quiz.q: Why does the narrator say silver is fine as the car's color even though it was not his first choice?
options: Because silver is his favorite color
Because his sister chose the color

Because it would scratch the car's paint

quiz.q: Why is the narrator now looking forward to long trips, when he used to hate them?

The new car will not overheat and they can watch movies
He no longer gets carsick in the new car
The trips will be much shorter from now on

1.title: My Dog Rex
paras: This is my dog Rex. He is small with short legs and floppy ears. His coat is light brown with a few black patches. I don’t know what breed he is, but he looks like a beagle crossed with a typical Taiwanese mutt.

He was just a puppy when Dad found him on the street and was in very bad condition. It looked like he hadn’t eaten for days or longer, and was covered with ticks. My father took pity on him and brought him home.

Mom immediately took Rex to the vet to have him checked out and to make sure that he was ok. The vet gave him a couple of shots, a flea bath and treated a couple of sores on his legs. It cost quite a bit to have him taken care of, but it didn’t matter because our family couldn’t stand to let an animal suffer like that.
Once at home, he was a bit nervous, but settled down shortly after. He could tell that he was in a safe place.
The next day, Dad went out and bought him a doghouse. It was small, white, and had a peaked roof 20 just like a real house. Rex seemed to like it too and went in to sniff 21 around right away.

Dad also bought a collar and a leash. Most of the family is gone during the daytime, so we needed something to tie him up in the yard with. It restricted him a bit, but I don’t think he minded. He was happy to have a good home.

My family has had Rex for almost seven years now and I can’t imagine life without him. He is so happy to see us whenever we get home and always greets us with a big sloppy kiss. He is also great at cheering us up when we are feeling down. He is the best dog ever, and I hope he lives a long, long time.
questions: What kind of condition was the dog in when it was found?
Why didn’t it matter that it cost so much to take the dog to the vet?
What does Rex do to greet its family?
