vocab.exzh: 我們一放牠出去，狗就開始在院子裡到處嗅聞。

vocab.ex: We put a new collar on Rex with a tag that shows our phone number.
vocab.exzh: 我們幫Rex戴上一個新頸圈，上面掛著寫有我們電話號碼的名牌。

vocab.ex: Please keep your dog on a leash when you walk him in the park.
vocab.exzh: 在公園遛狗時，請用牽繩牽好你的狗。

vocab.ex: The fence was built to restrict the dog to the backyard.
vocab.exzh: 這道圍籬是為了把狗限制在後院裡而建的。

vocab.ex: Rex gave me a big sloppy kiss the moment I walked through the door.
vocab.exzh: 我一走進門，Rex就給了我一個又濕又黏的親吻。

vocab.ex: She always calls a friend when she is feeling down.
vocab.exzh: 她心情低落時總是會打電話給朋友。

1.translation: 這是我的狗－雷克斯。他是一隻腿很短而且耳朵下垂的小狗。他的毛是淺棕色的，並且有一些黑色的斑點。我不知道他的品種，但他看起來像是小獵犬和傳統台灣土狗的混種。   當我爸在街上發現他時，他還很小，而且狀況非常不好。那時他看起來似乎有好幾天沒吃東西了，或甚至更久一點，而且全身都是蝨子。我爸看他很可憐，於是將他帶回家。   媽媽立刻帶他去獸醫那兒檢查，以確認他的狀況無虞。獸醫給他打了幾針、幫他洗澡清除跳蚤，並且治療他腳上的幾處傷口。照顧他花了我們不少錢，但這不重要，因為我們家人無法忍受讓一隻動物受那種苦。   剛到我家時，他有點緊張，但很快心就安定下來了。他知道他待的是一個安全的地方。 隔天，老爸外出幫他買了個狗屋，這個小狗屋顏色是白的，屋頂尖尖的，看起來像是真的房子。雷克斯似乎也很喜歡他的窩，並且馬上走進去到處嗅嗅。   爸爸也買了一個項圈和鍊條。白天時，大部分的家人都不在，所以我們需要東西來把他拴在院子裡。這有點限制了他的活動範圍，但我認為他不會介意，他很高興能有一個好的住所。   雷克斯來我們家已經快七年了，我無法想像沒有他的生活。每當我們回家時，他都很高興看到我們，而且總是用溼溼的舌頭熱情地舔著我們以示歡迎。當我們心情沮喪時，他也很會逗我們開心。他是有史以來最棒的狗，我希望他能活很久很久。
answers: It looked like he hadn’t eaten for days or longer, and was covered with ticks.
Because they couldn’t stand to let an animal suffer like that.
He always greets them with a big sloppy kiss.
paras_zh: 這是我的狗Rex。牠體型小小的，有一雙短短的腿和軟軟垂下的耳朵。牠的毛色是淺棕色，帶著幾塊黑色斑點。我不知道牠是什麼品種，但牠看起來像是米格魯和台灣土狗混種的。
爸爸在街上發現牠的時候，牠還只是一隻小狗，當時狀況非常糟糕。牠看起來好像已經好幾天，甚至更久沒吃東西了，身上還爬滿了壁虱。我爸爸心生憐憫，把牠帶回了家。
媽媽立刻帶Rex去看獸醫，做檢查確保牠沒事。獸醫幫牠打了幾針疫苗、洗了除蚤澡，還治療了牠腿上的幾處潰瘍。雖然花了不少錢照顧牠，但這都不重要，因為我們家沒辦法忍受看著一隻動物這樣受苦。
到家以後，牠一開始有點緊張，但不久就安定下來了。牠感覺得出來自己身處一個安全的地方。
隔天，爸爸出門幫牠買了一間狗屋。狗屋小小白白的，還有一個尖尖的屋頂，就像真正的房子一樣。Rex似乎也很喜歡，馬上就鑽進去到處嗅聞。
爸爸還買了頸圈和牽繩。家裡大部分的人白天都不在家，所以我們需要一些東西把牠拴在院子裡。這樣做多少限制了牠的自由，但我想牠並不介意。牠很高興能有一個溫暖的家。
我家養Rex已經快七年了，我無法想像沒有牠的生活會是什麼樣子。每次我們回到家，牠總是非常開心地見到我們，還會用又濕又黏的親吻迎接我們。牠也很擅長在我們心情低落時逗我們開心。牠是有史以來最棒的狗，我希望牠能活得長長久久。

quiz.q: Where did Dad find Rex?
options: On the street
At a pet store
At the vet's office
In the backyard

quiz.q: Why did Dad buy Rex a doghouse?
options: Because Rex was afraid of the vet
Because the family was moving to a new house
Because Rex refused to sleep indoors
So that Rex would have his own small house to sniff around in and stay in

quiz.q: Why did the family need a collar and a leash for Rex?
options: Because Rex kept running away from the vet
Because the doghouse was too small for him
Because most of the family is gone during the daytime and they needed to tie him up in the yard
Because Rex did not like wearing a collar at first

1.title: Belinda Has a Flat
paras: Belinda’s back tire seemed a little flat as she rode to school this morning, so she is checking the air pressure in it. As she squeezes it with her hands, it feels noticeably soft. If she were to ride on it any further, it may damage the tire or tube.
To fill the tire up, she uses a small air pump. First, she attaches the air hose to the valve stem located between the spokes and locks it in place.

Next, she begins to fill the tube with air by pumping up and down on the handle. It takes only a few minutes before the tire is hard again, but there is a problem. Belinda hears a hissing sound coming from somewhere on the tire. Examining the tire more closely reveals a small nail that has punctured it. Before she can ride again, she will need to buy a new tube or patch the old one. For now, she will have to be content with walking.
questions: How does Belinda check the tire’s air pressure?
What would happen if Belinda rode the bike with a flat tire?
How does Belinda know that there is something wrong with the tire?

vocab.ex: On her way home, Jenny got a flat and had to walk her bike the rest of the way.
vocab.exzh: 珍妮回家的路上輪胎爆了，剩下的路只好牽著腳踏車走。

vocab.ex: The mechanic checked the tire before letting her drive off.
vocab.exzh: 技師在讓她開走之前先檢查了輪胎。

vocab.ex: You should check the air pressure in your tires every month.
vocab.exzh: 你應該每個月檢查一次輪胎的胎壓。

vocab.ex: He would squeeze the orange to get the last drop of juice out.
vocab.exzh: 他把柳橙擠壓出最後一滴果汁。

vocab.ex: The room felt noticeably colder after the window was left open all night.
vocab.exzh: 窗戶開了一整晚後，房間明顯變冷了。

vocab.ex: The trail continues further into the forest than most hikers realize.
vocab.exzh: 這條步道比大多數健行者以為的更深入森林。

vocab.ex: Riding on a flat tire can damage the wheel rim.
vocab.exzh: 騎著洩氣的輪胎可能會損害輪框。

vocab.ex: The mechanic replaced the punctured tube inside the tire.
vocab.exzh: 技師更換了輪胎裡被刺破的內胎。

vocab.ex: He kept a small air pump in his backpack in case he got a flat tire.
vocab.exzh: 他在背包裡放了一個小打氣筒，以防輪胎沒氣。

vocab.ex: She used a strong cord to attach the trailer to the bicycle.
vocab.exzh: 她用一條堅固的繩子把拖車裝到腳踏車上。

vocab.ex: He connected the hose to the pump before filling the tire with air.
vocab.exzh: 他先把軟管接上打氣筒，才開始為輪胎打氣。

vocab.ex: Air escapes quickly once the valve is opened.
vocab.exzh: 閥一打開，空氣就會很快地洩出。

vocab.ex: The valve stem sticks out between two spokes on the wheel.
vocab.exzh: 氣門桿從輪子的兩根輪輻之間伸出來。

vocab.ex: The valve is located between the spokes near the rim.
vocab.exzh: 氣門位於輪輻之間、靠近輪框的地方。

vocab.ex: A small pebble got stuck between two of the bicycle's spokes.
vocab.exzh: 一顆小石子卡在腳踏車的兩根輪輻之間。

vocab.ex: She pumped up and down on the handle until the tire felt hard.
vocab.exzh: 她上下按壓把手，直到輪胎變硬為止。

vocab.ex: Air began to hiss out of the small hole in the tire.
vocab.exzh: 空氣開始從輪胎的小洞裡嘶嘶地漏出來。

vocab.ex: The doctor took a moment to examine the injured ankle.
vocab.exzh: 醫生花了一點時間檢查受傷的腳踝。

vocab.ex: She looked closely at the tire and finally spotted the tiny nail.
vocab.exzh: 她仔細地查看輪胎，終於發現了那根小釘子。

vocab.ex: A closer look at the tire began to reveal the source of the leak.
vocab.exzh: 仔細一看，輪胎逐漸顯露出漏氣的原因。

vocab.ex: A rusty nail was lying on the road right where she rode over it.
vocab.exzh: 一根生鏽的釘子正好躺在她騎過的路上。

vocab.ex: A sharp piece of glass can easily puncture a bicycle tire.
vocab.exzh: 一小片尖銳的玻璃很容易刺穿腳踏車輪胎。

vocab.ex: She used a rubber patch to patch the hole in the old tube.
vocab.exzh: 她用一塊橡膠補丁修補了舊內胎上的破洞。

vocab.ex: Unable to ride her bike, she was content with walking to school instead.
vocab.exzh: 無法騎腳踏車，她只好滿足於改用走路上學。

1.translation: 今天早上，布蘭達騎腳踏車上學時，她的後輪似乎有點洩氣了，所以她現在正在檢查胎壓。當她用手擠壓後輪時，輪胎感覺明顯變軟了。如果她再騎下去，腳踏車的外胎或內胎可能會受損。 她使用一個小型的打氣筒幫輪胎打氣。首先，她將送氣的軟管接到輪輻間的閥桿，並且把它固定好。 接著，她開始用把手上下施力為輪胎打氣。只花了幾分鐘的時間，輪胎就變硬了。但是有一個問題，那就是布蘭達聽到輪胎的某處發出嘶嘶聲。在更加仔細地檢查輪胎之後，發現有一根小鐵釘刺穿了輪胎。她將必須換新的內胎，或是把舊胎修補一下，才可以繼續騎。現在，她就只能安步當車了。
answers: She squeezes it with her hands.
It may damage the tire or tube.
She hears a hissing sound coming from somewhere on the tire.
paras_zh: 貝琳達今天早上騎車上學時，發現後輪胎好像有點洩氣，於是她正在檢查胎壓。當她用手擠壓輪胎時，明顯感覺到它變軟了。如果她繼續騎下去，可能會損壞輪胎或內胎。
為了把輪胎打飽氣，她用了一個小型打氣筒。首先，她把打氣的軟管接到位於輪輻之間的氣門桿上，並將它鎖緊固定。
接著，她開始上下按壓把手，將空氣打進內胎裡。輪胎很快就變硬了，只花了幾分鐘，但問題來了。貝琳達聽到輪胎上某處傳來嘶嘶的漏氣聲。她仔細檢查輪胎後，發現有一根小釘子刺穿了它。在能再次騎車之前，她必須買一條新的內胎，或是修補舊的那條。眼下，她只能滿足於用走的了。

quiz.q: Why does Belinda decide to check her tire in the morning?
options: She heard a loud bang from the wheel.
It seemed a little flat when she rode to school.
Her friend told her the tire looked new.
She wanted to clean the bicycle before school.

quiz.q: What does Belinda do first when she starts to fill the tire with air?
options: She attaches the air hose to the valve stem and locks it in place.
She pumps the handle up and down right away.
She removes the tube from the tire.
She buys a new tube at the store.

quiz.q: What does Belinda plan to do since she cannot ride her bike right away?
options: She will ask someone to drive her to school.
She will ride on the flat tire anyway.
She will leave the bicycle at school overnight.
She will walk instead until she gets a new tube or patches the old one.

2.title: Running Laps
paras: Our junior high school really believes in promoting physical exercise for the benefit of the students. Every morning after assembly, students are required to run three laps around the track. Not only does this boost their metabolism throughout the day, but it also helps them to wake up and be more alert.

Keeping the students alert is a major battle that teachers have to face every day. It is hard to keep students motivated for learning, especially in the morning when everyone has just crawled out of bed. Most teachers believe that running laps every day has a definite effect on students’ alertness, focus and also helps to improve their grades greatly. Additionally, doing laps also improves the overall health of students.
questions: How many laps are they required to run?
What are the benefits of exercising before classes start?
What effect does exercise seem to have on grades?

vocab.ex: She ran three laps around the track before breakfast.
vocab.exzh: 她在早餐前繞著跑道跑了三圈。

vocab.ex: The school hopes to promote healthy habits among students.
vocab.exzh: 學校希望在學生之間提倡健康的習慣。

vocab.ex: Regular exercise has many benefits for both the body and the mind.
vocab.exzh: 規律運動對身心都有許多好處。

vocab.ex: All the students gathered in the gym for the morning assembly.
vocab.exzh: 所有學生都到體育館參加早晨的集會。

vocab.ex: The teacher will require every student to hand in the report on time.
vocab.exzh: 老師會要求每位學生準時繳交報告。

vocab.ex: The runners lined up at the start of the track.
vocab.exzh: 跑者們在跑道起點排好隊。

vocab.ex: A good breakfast can boost your energy for the whole morning.
vocab.exzh: 豐盛的早餐可以提高你整個早上的活力。

vocab.ex: Exercising in the morning helps speed up your metabolism.
vocab.exzh: 早上運動有助於加快你的新陳代謝。

vocab.ex: She felt energetic throughout the entire day.
vocab.exzh: 她整天都感到精力充沛。

vocab.ex: Drinking water in the morning can make you feel more alert.
vocab.exzh: 早上喝水能讓你感覺更清醒機靈。

vocab.ex: Getting enough sleep is a major factor in student performance.
vocab.exzh: 睡眠充足是影響學生表現的重大因素。

vocab.ex: Waking up early is a daily battle for many students.
vocab.exzh: 對許多學生來說，早起是每天的一場掙扎。

vocab.ex: The coach praised the team for staying motivated until the final match.
vocab.exzh: 教練稱讚球隊一直保持動力直到最後一場比賽。

vocab.ex: The baby began to crawl across the living room floor.
vocab.exzh: 嬰兒開始在客廳地板上爬行。

vocab.ex: There is a definite improvement in her grades this semester.
vocab.exzh: 她這學期的成績有明確的進步。

vocab.ex: Regular exercise has a positive effect on concentration.
vocab.exzh: 規律運動對專注力有正面的效果。

vocab.ex: Morning exercise can greatly improve a student's alertness in class.
vocab.exzh: 晨間運動能大幅提升學生上課時的敏捷度。

vocab.ex: It is hard to keep your focus when you are tired.
vocab.exzh: 疲倦的時候很難保持專注。

vocab.ex: Practicing every day will improve your English quickly.
vocab.exzh: 每天練習會讓你的英文進步得很快。

vocab.ex: Additionally, the program helps students build good habits.
vocab.exzh: 此外，這個計畫也有助於學生養成好習慣。

vocab.ex: Daily exercise improves the overall health of students.
vocab.exzh: 每天運動能改善學生的整體健康。

2.translation: 我們的國中十分相信提倡體能運動對學生有益。在每天早上的朝會過後，學生必須跑操場三圈。這不僅會促進他們一天的新陳代謝，也能幫助他們精神振作，並且變得更加靈敏。 讓學生保持靈敏是老師每天必須面對的重大挑戰。特別是早上學生剛從床上爬起來，要讓他們維持學習的動機是很困難的。大多數的老師相信每天跑操場對學生的靈活度與專注力有顯著的效果，並且能夠大大地提昇他們的成績。此外，跑操場也能改善學生整體的健康。
answers: They are required to run 3 laps around the track.
It improves focus, alertness, and helps students to wake up. It also boosts metabolism.
It seems to improve grades greatly.
paras_zh: 我們的國中非常重視促進學生的體育鍛鍊，對他們大有好處。每天朝會結束後，學生都被要求繞著跑道跑三圈。這不僅能提升他們一整天的新陳代謝，還有助於喚醒身體、讓精神更加靈活機靈。
讓學生保持清醒機靈，是老師每天都要面對的一場硬仗。要讓學生對學習保持動力並不容易，尤其是在大家剛從床上爬起來的早晨。大多數老師都認為，每天跑步對學生的機靈度、專注力有明確的效果，也大大有助於提升他們的成績。此外，跑步也能改善學生的整體健康。

quiz.q: When are students required to run laps according to the passage?
options: Every morning after assembly
Right after lunch
Before school starts each week
Only on physical education days

quiz.q: According to the passage, why is keeping students alert considered a major battle for teachers?
options: Students refuse to attend assembly
The track is too far from the classrooms
Teachers do not believe in the benefits of exercise
It is hard to keep students motivated for learning, especially in the morning

quiz.q: Besides alertness and focus, what other benefit of running laps is mentioned in the passage?
options: It saves money for the school
It shortens the school day
It improves students' overall health
It reduces the number of assemblies

2.title: Peter and the Drinking Fountain
paras: Peter has taken a break from class. His classroom is always very hot during the summer months and he needs to drink lots of water so that he doesn’t get dehydrated.  He has also been diligently studying all morning in the hot classroom and has worked up a thirst.

The thirsty boy wanders down the long hallway to the nearest water fountain. As he turns the knob on the top of the stainless steel fountain, water begins to stream out. He lets it run for a few seconds to cool it off. He then bends over and takes a sip. Ah, it refreshes him instantly and he continues slurping away.
The cool water quickly quenches his thirst and he wipes his mouth on his sleeve.  Satisfied now, he slowly returns to class, trying to waste as much time as possible.
questions: What had Peter been doing that made him thirsty?
Why does Peter let the water run for a few seconds before he takes a drink?
What does Peter wipe his mouth on?

vocab.ex: During recess, the children lined up at the drinking fountain to get a cool drink of water.
vocab.exzh: 下課時間，孩子們在飲水機前排隊，喝一口涼水。

vocab.ex: After two hours of studying, Mary decided to take a break and stretch her legs.
vocab.exzh: 讀了兩個小時的書之後，瑪麗決定休息一下，伸展一下腿。

vocab.ex: If you exercise in the hot sun without drinking water, you can quickly dehydrate.
vocab.exzh: 如果在炎熱的陽光下運動卻不喝水，你可能很快就會脫水。

vocab.ex: She worked diligently on her homework every night after dinner.
vocab.exzh: 她每天晚飯後都很用功地做功課。

vocab.ex: Running around the playground all afternoon can work up a big appetite.
vocab.exzh: 整個下午在操場上跑來跑去，會讓人激起很大的食欲。

vocab.ex: A cold glass of water can quickly satisfy your thirst on a hot day.
vocab.exzh: 在炎熱的日子裡，一杯冰水可以很快解除你的口渴。

vocab.ex: During lunch break, some students like to wander around the schoolyard.
vocab.exzh: 午休時間，有些學生喜歡在校園裡漫步。

vocab.ex: The lockers are lined up neatly along the school hallway.
vocab.exzh: 置物櫃整齊地排列在學校走廊兩側。

vocab.ex: He turned the knob on the fountain and watched the water come out.
vocab.exzh: 他轉動飲水機上的旋鈕，看著水流出來。

vocab.ex: The kitchen sink is made of shiny stainless steel.
vocab.exzh: 廚房的水槽是用閃亮的不鏽鋼做的。

vocab.ex: The bridge was built with strong steel beams.
vocab.exzh: 這座橋是用堅固的鋼樑建造的。

vocab.ex: Water began to stream out of the fountain as soon as he turned the knob.
vocab.exzh: 他一轉動旋鈕，水就開始從飲水機流出。

vocab.ex: He let the tap run for a moment before filling his cup.
vocab.exzh: 他讓水龍頭流了一會兒，才裝滿他的杯子。

vocab.ex: He waited a few seconds for the water to cool off before drinking it.
vocab.exzh: 他等了幾秒鐘讓水變涼，才喝下去。

vocab.ex: She had to bend down low to reach the water from the short fountain.
vocab.exzh: 她必須彎下腰才能喝到那台矮飲水機的水。

vocab.ex: He took a small sip of the cold water and smiled.
vocab.exzh: 他啜飲了一小口冰水，露出微笑。

vocab.ex: A cold drink of water can refresh you after a long walk in the sun.
vocab.exzh: 在陽光下走了很久之後，喝一杯冰水能讓你精神振奮。

vocab.ex: The cool water made him feel better instantly.
vocab.exzh: 那冰涼的水讓他立刻感覺好多了。

vocab.ex: The thirsty dog began to slurp water noisily from its bowl.
vocab.exzh: 那隻口渴的狗開始從碗裡咕嚕咕嚕地喝水。

vocab.ex: Nothing can quench your thirst better than a cold glass of water.
vocab.exzh: 沒有什麼比一杯冰水更能解渴了。

vocab.ex: He wiped the water from his mouth with his sleeve.
vocab.exzh: 他用袖子擦掉嘴邊的水。

vocab.ex: After drinking the cool water, he felt completely satisfied.
vocab.exzh: 喝了那杯冰水之後，他感到非常滿足。

2.translation: 彼得下課了。他的教室在夏天的這幾個月份都很熱，所以他需要補充很多水分才不至於脫水。他整個早上都在很熱的教室裡用功讀書，使得他很口渴。這個口渴的小男孩緩慢的沿著長廊走到了最近的飲水機。當他轉動不鏽鋼飲水機上面的轉鈕時，水就慢慢的流出來。他讓它流了幾秒，讓水涼一點才喝。然後他彎下腰去喝一小口。啊！這一小口使他立刻恢復精神，接著他繼續咕嚕咕嚕地喝。 那涼爽的水很快地解除他的口渴，然後他用袖子擦擦他的嘴巴。此刻，彼得感到很滿足。他緩慢的走回教室，試著要儘量拖延一些時間。
answers: He had been diligently studying all morning.
He lets the water run to cool it off.
He wipes his mouth on his sleeve.
paras_zh: 彼得暫時離開了教室休息一下。他的教室在夏天總是非常悶熱，所以他需要喝很多水，以免脫水。他整個早上都在悶熱的教室裡用功讀書，因此感到口渴難耐。
這個口渴的男孩沿著長長的走廊漫步走向最近的飲水機。當他轉動不鏽鋼飲水機頂部的旋鈕時，水開始流了出來。他讓水流了幾秒鐘讓它變涼。接著他彎下腰，啜飲了一口。啊，這讓他立刻精神一振，於是他繼續咕嚕咕嚕地喝著。
冰涼的水很快解了他的渴，他用袖子擦了擦嘴。現在心滿意足的他，慢慢地走回教室，儘量拖延時間。

quiz.q: Why is Peter's classroom uncomfortable during the summer?
options: It is too cold
It is very hot
It is too noisy
It has no windows

quiz.q: What is the drinking fountain made of?
options: Stainless steel
Plastic
Wood
Glass

quiz.q: How does Peter behave on his way back to class?
options: He runs quickly so he will not be late
He stops to talk to his friends
He goes back to the fountain for more water
He walks slowly to waste as much time as possible

2.title: Bilingual Signs
paras: Taiwanese schools are trying to become more and more bilingual all the time. A good way to do this is to make all existing signs English and Chinese like exit, bathroom and other common signs. Another way is to put signs in high traffic areas to increase exposure to English.

This particular school has done this by placing old adages on the stairs. It works quite well as students cannot go up the stairs without having to look at the sayings. They may choose not to read them for a while, but chances are after going up and down the steps repeatedly they will eventually read them all out of curiosity.
The signs have English on the left and Chinese on the right. They are also brightly colored to catch your eye.
questions: What is a good way to make schools bilingual?
Where should schools put the signs to increase exposure?
Why are the old adages brightly colored?

vocab.ex: Our school offers a bilingual program in English and Chinese.
vocab.exzh: 我們學校提供英語和中文的雙語課程。

vocab.ex: The city decided to improve its existing road signs instead of building new ones.
vocab.exzh: 市政府決定改善現有的路標，而不是重新建造。

vocab.ex: There is a sign on the door that says the shop is closed.
vocab.exzh: 門上有一個標誌寫著商店已關門。

vocab.ex: Please use the exit at the back of the building during a fire drill.
vocab.exzh: 消防演習期間請使用大樓後方的出口。

vocab.ex: It is quite common for students in Taiwan to study English from a young age.
vocab.exzh: 台灣學生從小就開始學英文是很常見的。

vocab.ex: The hallway outside the cafeteria always has heavy traffic during lunchtime.
vocab.exzh: 午餐時間，餐廳外的走廊總是有大量的人潮。

vocab.ex: Students are asked to stay quiet in the reading area of the library.
vocab.exzh: 學生們被要求在圖書館的閱讀區保持安靜。

vocab.ex: Reading English books every day can increase your vocabulary quickly.
vocab.exzh: 每天閱讀英文書可以快速增加你的字彙量。

vocab.ex: Watching English cartoons gives children more exposure to the language.
vocab.exzh: 看英語卡通能讓孩子們更多接觸這個語言。

vocab.ex: This particular classroom is decorated with English posters on every wall.
vocab.exzh: 這間特定的教室每面牆上都貼滿了英文海報。

vocab.ex: The old adage "practice makes perfect" is often used to encourage students.
vocab.exzh: 「熟能生巧」這句古老的格言常被用來鼓勵學生。

vocab.ex: She climbed each stair slowly while carrying a heavy bag of books.
vocab.exzh: 她提著一袋沉重的書，慢慢地爬上每一級樓梯。

vocab.ex: My grandmother often shares an old Chinese saying with the whole family.
vocab.exzh: 我的祖母常常和全家人分享一句古老的中國諺語。

vocab.ex: Students can choose not to read the signs at first, but curiosity often wins.
vocab.exzh: 學生一開始可以選擇不去讀那些標語，但好奇心通常會佔上風。

vocab.ex: There is a good chance that students will notice the signs after walking past them many times.
vocab.exzh: 學生在多次經過那些標語後，很有機會注意到它們。

vocab.ex: He paused on the last step to look at the poster on the wall.
vocab.exzh: 他在最後一級臺階上停下來看牆上的海報。

vocab.ex: The teacher repeatedly reminded the class to review the new vocabulary.
vocab.exzh: 老師一再提醒班上同學要複習新的單字。

vocab.ex: If students see the same sign every day, they will eventually remember its meaning.
vocab.exzh: 如果學生每天都看到同一個標誌，他們最終會記住它的意思。

vocab.ex: Out of pure curiosity, the boy stopped to read every sign on the stairs.
vocab.exzh: 出於純粹的好奇心，那個男孩停下來閱讀樓梯上的每一個標語。

vocab.ex: The posters were painted brightly so that students would notice them right away.
vocab.exzh: 海報被漆得色彩鮮明，這樣學生就能立刻注意到它們。

vocab.ex: The colorful sign on the stairs really caught my eye this morning.
vocab.exzh: 今天早上樓梯上那個色彩繽紛的標誌真的吸引了我的目光。

2.translation: 台灣的學校一直試著要變得更為雙語化，而將目前所有的標誌做成中英對照是個好方法，例如：出口、洗手間，以及其它常見的標誌。另一個方式是把標誌放在人來人往的地方，以增加學生接觸英文的機會。 這所特定的學校把一些古老的諺語放在階梯來達成雙語化的目的。由於學生上樓梯時一定會看到這些諺語，所以它的效果很好。也許會有一段時間他們選擇不去看這些諺語，但是很可能因為要不斷的上下樓梯，最後只因出於好奇心而去閱讀它們。 這些標誌左邊是英文，右邊是中文。它們的色彩也很鮮明，以便能吸引你的目光。
answers: A good way to do this is by making all existing signs English and Chinese.
They should put them in high traffic areas to increase exposure.
They are brightly colored to catch your eye.
paras_zh: 台灣的學校正努力變得越來越雙語化。一個好方法是把所有現有的標誌都改成英文和中文並列，像是出口、廁所和其他常見的標誌。另一個方法是在人潮眾多的區域張貼標語，以增加學生接觸英文的機會。
這所特定的學校就是這麼做的，他們把古老的格言貼在樓梯上。這個做法效果相當好，因為學生上下樓梯時不得不看到這些標語。他們一開始或許會選擇不去閱讀，但在反覆上下樓梯之後，很有可能出於好奇心最終會把它們全部讀完。
這些標語左邊是英文，右邊是中文，而且顏色鮮明，非常吸引目光。

quiz.q: According to the passage, why do students at this school eventually read the adages on the stairs?
options: Because a teacher requires them to read the signs aloud
Because the adages are printed in their textbooks
Because they cannot go up and down the stairs without seeing them repeatedly
Because the principal tests them on the sayings every week

quiz.q: What is one purpose of placing bilingual signs in high traffic areas?
options: To decorate the school for special events
To increase students' exposure to English
To replace the need for English teachers
To help visitors find the exit more quickly

quiz.q: How are the bilingual signs on the stairs arranged and designed?
options: English on the left and Chinese on the right, brightly colored
Chinese on the left and English on the right, in plain black and white
Only in English, with small black text
Chinese on top and English below, in dull gray tones

2.title: Eli Makes Instant Noodles
paras: Picture 1 Eli peels back the lid of the Styrofoam container and finds a package of seasoning sitting on top of the instant noodles.
Picture 2 He tears open the seasoning packet for the broth and sprinkles it over the top of the noodles.
Picture 3 Eli then pours boiling water from the kettle over the noodles, filling the container almost to the top.
Picture 4 Next, he re-covers the container with the foil lid, and weighs it down with his chopsticks.  He needs to let it stand until the noodles are nice and soft—about 3 minutes or so.
Picture 5 Now that the noodles are cooked, Eli slowly peels back the foil, careful not to burn himself with the steam.
Picture 6 He lets the noodles sit for an additional minute to cool off before eating them. He certainly seems to be enjoying his Cup O’ Noodles.
questions: What does Eli do with the seasoning?
How hot does the water have to be to make noodles?
Why is Eli careful when he takes the lid off?

vocab.ex: For a quick lunch, Tom decided to make some instant noodles.
vocab.exzh: 為了快速解決午餐，湯姆決定煮一些速食麵。

vocab.ex: She carefully peeled the lid off the yogurt cup.
vocab.exzh: 她小心翼翼地把優格杯的蓋子剝開。

vocab.ex: The hot soup was served in a Styrofoam cup.
vocab.exzh: 熱湯是用保麗龍杯裝的。

vocab.ex: He stored the leftover rice in a plastic container.
vocab.exzh: 他把剩下的飯放進一個塑膠容器裡。

vocab.ex: She opened a small package of crackers for a snack.
vocab.exzh: 她打開一小包餅乾當作點心。

vocab.ex: He added some seasoning to make the soup tastier.
vocab.exzh: 他加了一些調味料讓湯更好喝。

vocab.ex: She had to tear the packet open with her teeth.
vocab.exzh: 她必須用牙齒把包裝撕開。

vocab.ex: He added a packet of sugar to his coffee.
vocab.exzh: 他在咖啡裡加了一包糖。

vocab.ex: The warm broth smelled delicious as it simmered on the stove.
vocab.exzh: 溫熱的湯在爐子上熬煮時聞起來很香。

vocab.ex: She likes to sprinkle cheese on top of her pasta.
vocab.exzh: 她喜歡在義大利麵上灑一些起司。

vocab.ex: Please pour the milk into the glass slowly.
vocab.exzh: 請把牛奶慢慢倒進杯子裡。

vocab.ex: The kettle whistled loudly when the water started to boil.
vocab.exzh: 水一開始滾，茶壺就發出響亮的鳴叫聲。

vocab.ex: He wrapped the sandwich in foil before putting it in his bag.
vocab.exzh: 他把三明治用鋁箔紙包起來，再放進包包裡。

vocab.ex: He used a rock to weigh down the paper on the windy day.
vocab.exzh: 在起風的日子，他用一顆石頭壓住紙張。

vocab.ex: She picked up the noodles easily with her chopsticks.
vocab.exzh: 她用筷子輕鬆地夾起麵條。

vocab.ex: Let the tea stand for five minutes before drinking it.
vocab.exzh: 讓茶靜置五分鐘後再喝。

vocab.ex: Be careful not to burn your hand on the hot pan.
vocab.exzh: 小心不要被熱鍋燙傷你的手。

vocab.ex: Hot steam rose from the bowl of noodles.
vocab.exzh: 熱蒸氣從那碗麵中升起。

vocab.ex: The teacher gave the students an additional five minutes to finish the test.
vocab.exzh: 老師多給了學生額外五分鐘來完成測驗。

vocab.ex: This is certainly the best bowl of noodles I have ever had.
vocab.exzh: 這無疑是我吃過最好吃的一碗麵。

2.translation: 照片一伊萊把保麗龍碗的蓋子往後掀開，在泡麵上面看到一包湯頭調味料。 照片二他撕開湯頭調味包，並且將它倒在泡麵上面。 照片三接著，伊萊將茶壺中的沸水倒在泡麵上，水量差不多到碗的最上方。 照片四接下來，他再把錫箔紙蓋蓋上，並且用他的筷子壓住蓋子，他必須靜置大約三分鐘，直到麵條變得Q軟好吃。 照片五現在泡麵已經泡好了，伊萊慢慢地把保麗龍碗的蓋子往後掀開，他小心翼翼地，以免自己被熱氣燙傷。 照片六食用前，他再多等一分鐘，讓泡麵稍微變涼。 毫無疑問的，他似乎很享受他的杯麵。
answers: He sprinkles it over the top of the noodles.
It has to be boiling hot.
He is careful so that he doesn’t burn himself with the steam.
paras_zh: 圖片1 伊萊掀開保麗龍容器的蓋子，發現速食麵上放著一包調味料。
圖片2 他撕開湯底調味包，把它灑在麵條上面。
圖片3 接著，伊萊把熱水壺裡的滾水倒在麵上，幾乎把容器倒滿到接近頂端。
圖片4 接下來，他把鋁箔蓋重新蓋上，並用筷子壓住蓋子。他需要讓麵靜置到變得軟嫩——大約3分鐘左右。
圖片5 現在麵已經泡好了，伊萊慢慢掀開鋁箔蓋，小心不要被蒸氣燙傷。
圖片6 他讓麵再多放一分鐘冷卻後才開始吃。他看起來非常享受他的杯麵。

quiz.q: What does Eli use to keep the foil lid down while the noodles cook?
options: A spoon
A book
A plate
His chopsticks

quiz.q: About how long does Eli let the noodles stand before opening the lid?
options: About 30 seconds
About 1 minute
About 3 minutes
About 10 minutes

quiz.q: Why does Eli wait an additional minute after peeling back the foil?
options: To add more seasoning
To let the noodles cool off before eating
To pour in more water
To let the broth thicken

2.title: No Cafeteria is No Problem
paras: Most schools in Taiwan do not have cafeterias. Instead, they hire caterers to do all the cooking and bring it to the schools. The contracted caterers do not prepare the food on-site; they do it in their own kitchens. Once the food is ready, it is packed into containers and dropped off 7at the schools. Each container holds the same thing so that there is no worry of a mix-up as to which one goes where. Representatives from each class then pick up the food at the drop-off point and bring it back to their classrooms.

At our school, the lunch containers are put on chairs or tables at the front of the classroom near the chalkboard. Volunteers then distribute the food evenly amongst the students. If there are any leftovers, students are allowed to have second helpings. As you can see in the second picture, the students here are very hungry and can’t wait to dish up.

Every student carries a small lunch bag that contains utensils and a stainless steel bowl. They pack their lunch bag with them every day, but if they happen to forget, there are disposable bowls and utensils available so that no one has to go hungry. After lunch, each student is responsible for cleaning his or her own dish.
questions: Do most schools in Taiwan have cafeterias?
What is the food packed into so that it can be brought to the schools?
What do the students have to pack every day so that they can eat?

vocab.ex: Students can buy lunch at the school cafeteria every day.
vocab.exzh: 學生每天都可以在學校的自助餐廳買午餐。

vocab.ex: She did not want rice, so she ordered noodles instead.
vocab.exzh: 她不想吃飯，所以改點了麵。

vocab.ex: The school hired a caterer to prepare lunch for all the students.
vocab.exzh: 學校聘請了一位供餐業者為所有學生準備午餐。

vocab.ex: The contracted company delivers fresh food to the school every morning.
vocab.exzh: 這家簽約的公司每天早上都會把新鮮的食物送到學校。

vocab.ex: The food is not cooked on-site; it is made in another kitchen.
vocab.exzh: 食物不是在現場烹煮的，而是在另一個廚房製作的。

vocab.ex: Workers pack the hot meals into containers before sending them out.
vocab.exzh: 工作人員在把熱食送出去之前，先把它們裝進容器裡。

vocab.ex: The driver will drop off the lunch boxes at the school gate.
vocab.exzh: 司機會把便當放在學校門口。

vocab.ex: Each container has a label so there is no mix-up about which class it belongs to.
vocab.exzh: 每個容器都有標籤，所以不會弄混是哪一班的。

vocab.ex: A class representative picks up the food and brings it back to the room.
vocab.exzh: 班級代表會去領取食物並帶回教室。

vocab.ex: Students gather at the drop-off point to collect their class lunch.
vocab.exzh: 學生們在放置點集合，領取班上的午餐。

vocab.ex: A volunteer helps serve the food to every student in the class.
vocab.exzh: 一位志願者幫忙把食物分給班上每位學生。

vocab.ex: The teacher asked the students to distribute the bowls before lunch.
vocab.exzh: 老師請學生們在午餐前把碗分發下去。

vocab.ex: The volunteers try to share the food evenly among all the students.
vocab.exzh: 志願者們盡量把食物平均分給所有學生。

vocab.ex: There was some leftover soup, so a few students asked for more.
vocab.exzh: 還剩了一些湯，所以有幾個學生要求再添一些。

vocab.ex: He was still hungry, so he asked for a second helping of rice.
vocab.exzh: 他還是很餓，所以又要了一份飯。

vocab.ex: The hungry students could not wait to dish up their lunch.
vocab.exzh: 飢腸轆轆的學生們等不及要把午餐盛出來吃。

vocab.ex: Every student brings a spoon and other utensils for lunch.
vocab.exzh: 每個學生都會帶湯匙和其他餐具來吃午餐。

vocab.ex: If a student should happen to forget his bowl, he can use a disposable one.
vocab.exzh: 如果學生碰巧忘了帶碗，他可以使用免洗的。

vocab.ex: The school keeps disposable bowls on hand for students who forget theirs.
vocab.exzh: 學校備有免洗碗，給忘記帶碗的學生使用。

vocab.ex: Each student is responsible for washing his or her own dish after lunch.
vocab.exzh: 每個學生都要負責在午餐後清洗自己的餐具。

2.translation: 在台灣，大部分的學校都沒有自助餐廳，而是僱用辦理外食的廠商來幫他們料理飯菜，然後再送到學校來。和學校簽約的廠商並非在現場料理食材，而是在他們自己的廚房做。一旦食物做好後，就會被裝在餐桶送到學校。每一個餐桶裡都裝著相同的菜色，所以不必擔心餐桶擺放的位置會搞混。各班接著派代表到放置餐桶的地方，將食物抬回他們的教室。 在我們學校，餐桶放在靠近教室前方黑板的椅子或是桌子上。然後，志願幫忙盛飯菜的學生，便會把食物平均分配給每一位同學。如果分完後還有剩下的話，同學們就可以再取用一次飯菜。就如同您在第二張照片中所看到的，這群學生們都已是飢腸轆轆，迫不及待地要開動了!     每一個學生都會帶一個裝餐具和不鏽鋼碗的小餐袋。他們每天都帶餐袋來學校。不過要是他們忘了帶的話，學校裡也有免洗餐具，所以就用不著擔心會餓肚子了。吃完午餐後，每一位學生都要負責清洗他們自己的餐具。
answers: No, most schools in Taiwan do not have cafeterias.
It is packed into containers.
They have to pack utensils and a bowl.
paras_zh: 台灣大部分的學校都沒有自助餐廳。相反地，他們聘請供餐業者負責烹煮，再把食物送到學校。簽約的供餐業者不會在學校現場準備食物，而是在自己的廚房裡完成烹調。食物一旦準備好，就會被裝進容器裡，送到學校去。每個容器裝的東西都一樣，這樣就不用擔心搞混哪一份是哪一班的。接著，各班代表會到卸貨地點領取食物，再帶回自己的教室。
在我們學校，午餐的容器會被放在教室前方、靠近黑板的椅子或桌子上。接著志願者會把食物平均分給學生們。如果還有剩，學生們可以再添一份。從第二張照片中可以看到，這裡的學生都非常餓，迫不及待想要把飯菜盛出來吃。
每個學生都會攜帶一個小餐袋，裡面裝著餐具和一個不鏽鋼碗。他們每天都會帶著自己的餐袋，但如果碰巧忘記帶，學校也準備了免洗碗和餐具，讓大家都不會餓肚子。午餐過後，每個學生都要負責清洗自己的餐具。

quiz.q: Why do most schools in Taiwan hire caterers instead of cooking food themselves?
options: Because most schools do not have cafeterias
Because students prefer food from outside
Because the government requires it
Because caterers are cheaper than teachers

quiz.q: How does the school make sure the correct food goes to the correct class?
options: Teachers call the caterer to check
Students choose their own food at the cafeteria
The principal delivers each container personally
Each container holds the same thing, so there is no mix-up

quiz.q: What happens if a student forgets to bring a bowl and utensils?
options: The student cannot eat lunch that day
The student must borrow from a classmate
Disposable bowls and utensils are available
The student goes home for lunch

2.title: Jack Eats Out
paras: Jack and his family are eating out this evening. The place they have chosen is a buffet-style restaurant. These restaurants offer a wide range of foods prepared in many ways.

After taking a plate to put the food on, Jack heads to the steam table. The steam table keeps the food warm even after sitting for some time. Each tray on the table contains a different dish. Jack eyes the tray heaped with stewed tofu. He thinks it looks very good, so he digs right in.

After putting a few vegetables on his plate, he takes the food to the cashier. In this restaurant you are charged by weight. This means you have to pay so much per gram of food. At some buffet-style restaurants you are charged by the number of items and not by weight, so if you want to sample many dishes it may be a lot more expensive.
Included with the price of Jack’s meal are soup and black tea. He takes advantage by repeatedly filling up his bowl with steaming hot soup. He sure loves soup, doesn't he?
Finally, Jack sits down to enjoy his meal. His family is still dishing up, but they will join him soon. By that time Jack will probably be ready for a second helping. Bon appetit, Jack!
questions: Where have Jack and his family chosen to eat at?
What does Jack think looks very good?
Do you have to pay by item at this restaurant?

vocab.ex: We decided to eat out tonight instead of cooking at home.
vocab.exzh: 我們決定今晚在外用餐，不在家煮飯了。

vocab.ex: The hotel offers a buffet-style breakfast every morning.
vocab.exzh: 這間飯店每天早上都提供自助餐式的早餐。

vocab.ex: The store sells a wide range of products, from food to clothing.
vocab.exzh: 這家店販售種類廣泛的商品，從食物到衣服都有。

vocab.ex: After class, the students head straight to the cafeteria.
vocab.exzh: 下課後，學生們直接走向自助餐廳。

vocab.ex: Steam rose from the hot bowl of noodles.
vocab.exzh: 熱騰騰的麵碗裡冒出蒸氣。

vocab.ex: She carried a tray full of dishes to the table.
vocab.exzh: 她端著裝滿菜餚的托盤走到桌邊。

vocab.ex: He decided to heap his plate with fried rice.
vocab.exzh: 他決定把盤子裝滿炒飯。

vocab.ex: The stewed beef was so tender it fell apart with a fork.
vocab.exzh: 這道燉牛肉非常軟嫩，用叉子一碰就散開了。

vocab.ex: The children grabbed their spoons and began to dig into the cake.
vocab.exzh: 孩子們拿起湯匙開始挖起蛋糕大吃特吃。

vocab.ex: The cashier weighed my plate and told me the price.
vocab.exzh: 收銀員秤了我的盤子並告訴我價錢。

vocab.ex: This restaurant will charge you extra for a second drink.
vocab.exzh: 這間餐廳如果你要續第二杯飲料會額外收費。

vocab.ex: The recipe calls for two hundred grams of sugar.
vocab.exzh: 這份食譜需要兩百公克的糖。

vocab.ex: Each item on the menu comes with a small salad.
vocab.exzh: 菜單上的每一項餐點都附一份小沙拉。

vocab.ex: Visitors can sample several local dishes at the food festival.
vocab.exzh: 遊客可以在美食節上品嚐好幾道當地菜餚。

vocab.ex: The set meal will include a drink and a dessert.
vocab.exzh: 這份套餐會包含一杯飲料和一份甜點。

vocab.ex: She decided to take advantage of the free refills and drink more soup.
vocab.exzh: 她決定利用免費續碗的機會多喝點湯。

vocab.ex: He poured himself a bowl of steaming hot soup.
vocab.exzh: 他為自己盛了一碗熱騰騰的湯。

vocab.ex: If he keeps eating like that, he will probably want a second plate soon.
vocab.exzh: 如果他繼續這樣吃下去，很可能很快就會想再來一盤。

vocab.ex: The waiter set down the plates and said, "Bon appetit!"
vocab.exzh: 服務生放下盤子後說：「祝你有好的食慾！」

2.translation: 今天晚上，傑克和他的家人到外面去吃飯。他們所選擇的地點是一間自助式餐廳。這種餐廳提供了很多種以不同方式烹調的食物。 在拿了一個裝食物的盤子後，傑克走向了蒸氣保溫餐桌。這個餐桌可以保持食物的熱度，即使食物已經放了一段時間。桌上每個盤子都裝著不同的菜餚。傑克看著一個裝滿滷豆腐的托盤，他認為這道菜看起來很好吃，所以他立即挖了一勺。 在裝了一些青菜到他的餐盤之後，他端著盤子走到結帳處。這間餐廳是根據食物的重量來算錢的，這意謂著你必須按食物每公克的價格來付費。某些自助式餐廳是根據你吃幾種菜餚來算錢，而不是重量。所以，如果你想要品嚐很多樣的菜餚，那費用可能就會貴很多。 傑克這餐的費用包含了湯和紅茶。他善用這項優惠，將熱湯一碗一碗地盛到碗裡。他真的很喜歡喝湯，不是嗎? 最後，傑克坐下來享用他的餐點。他的家人們還在挑選菜餚，但他們很快就會加入他的行列。到那時候，傑克將已將準備好要吃第二盤了。傑克，祝你胃口大開!
answers: They have chosen to eat at a buffet-style restaurant.
He thinks the tray heaped with stewed tofu looks very good.
No, you have to pay by weight.
paras_zh: 傑克和他的家人今晚要外出用餐。他們選擇的地方是一間自助餐式的餐廳。這類餐廳提供種類豐富、烹調方式多樣的食物。
傑克拿了一個盤子準備裝食物後，便走向保溫台。保溫台能讓食物即使放了一段時間仍保持溫熱。台上的每個托盤都裝著不同的菜餚。傑克盯著那盤堆得滿滿的燉豆腐，覺得看起來非常美味，於是立刻動手夾了起來。
在盤子裡加了幾樣蔬菜後，他把食物拿去給收銀員結帳。在這間餐廳，是依重量計費的，也就是說，你要依每公克的食物付費。在有些自助餐廳則是依項目數量計費而非重量，所以如果你想多樣品嚐，可能會貴上許多。
傑克這餐的價錢已經包含了湯和紅茶。他便善加利用，一次又一次地把碗盛滿熱騰騰的湯。他真的很愛喝湯，不是嗎？
最後，傑克坐下來享用他的這一餐。他的家人還在夾菜，但很快就會加入他。到那個時候，傑克很可能已經準備要再來一份了。傑克，祝你好胃口！

quiz.q: Why does the steam table keep the food useful for a buffet restaurant?
options: It allows customers to cook their own food
It keeps the food warm even after it has been sitting for a while
It weighs the food automatically
It changes the price of each dish

quiz.q: According to the passage, why might sampling many dishes be more expensive at some buffet restaurants?
options: Because those restaurants charge by the number of items rather than by weight
Because those restaurants only serve imported food
Because those restaurants do not allow refills
Because those restaurants charge extra for using a tray

quiz.q: What is included in the price of Jack's meal besides the food he selects?
options: Dessert and coffee
A free appetizer
Unlimited fried rice
Soup and black tea

2.title: Document Photos
paras: In Taiwan there are a lot of forms that require photos to be attached. The standard size photo is 4 * 5 cm for most forms. They are especially important for passports, visas and hospital records. On such forms there is a box at the top specifically for this purpose.

Picture This girl is removing a photo from a small reddish envelope. She is filling out an application to take a GEPT test. The form requires her picture so that the test administrator can verify her identity when she takes the test. This prevents the girl from having another person take the test for her.

Picture 2 She applies glue to the back of the photo and then sticks it to the application. It is now ready to be mailed and, with any luck, she will be accepted to take the upcoming test; otherwise, she has to wait for the next test.
questions: What do you have to attach to a lot of forms in Taiwan?
What is the girl filling out an application for?
Why does the GEPT form require a photo?

vocab.ex: You need to bring an important document to the bank when you open an account.
vocab.exzh: 開戶時你需要帶一份重要的文件到銀行。

vocab.ex: Please fill out this form before you see the doctor.
vocab.exzh: 看醫生之前請先填寫這張表格。

vocab.ex: Remember to attach a photo to your application.
vocab.exzh: 記得在申請表上貼一張照片。

vocab.ex: This is the standard size for a passport photo.
vocab.exzh: 這是護照照片的標準尺寸。

vocab.ex: She showed her passport to the officer at the airport.
vocab.exzh: 她在機場向官員出示了護照。

vocab.ex: He applied for a visa before traveling abroad.
vocab.exzh: 他在出國前申請了簽證。

vocab.ex: The hospital keeps a record of every patient's history.
vocab.exzh: 醫院會保存每位病人的病歷記錄。

vocab.ex: Write your name in the box at the top of the form.
vocab.exzh: 請在表格上方的格子裡寫上你的名字。

vocab.ex: This section is specifically designed for photo attachments.
vocab.exzh: 這個部分是特別設計來貼照片用的。

vocab.ex: The purpose of this box is to hold your photo.
vocab.exzh: 這個格子的用途是放你的照片。

vocab.ex: She had to remove the photo from the envelope carefully.
vocab.exzh: 她必須小心地把照片從信封裡拿出來。

vocab.ex: The envelope had a reddish color that was easy to notice.
vocab.exzh: 這個信封帶著紅色，很容易注意到。

vocab.ex: You must fill out this application before the deadline.
vocab.exzh: 你必須在截止日期前填寫這份申請表。

vocab.ex: She submitted her application for the test online.
vocab.exzh: 她在網路上提交了考試的申請表。

vocab.ex: The test administrator checked every student's identity card.
vocab.exzh: 監考人員檢查了每位學生的身分證。

vocab.ex: The staff will verify your identity before you enter the exam room.
vocab.exzh: 工作人員會在你進入考場前驗證你的身分。

vocab.ex: You need an ID card to prove your identity.
vocab.exzh: 你需要一張身分證來證明你的身分。

vocab.ex: A photo on the form helps prevent people from cheating.
vocab.exzh: 表格上的照片有助於防止有人作弊。

vocab.ex: She will apply a thin layer of glue to the back of the photo.
vocab.exzh: 她會在照片背面塗上一層薄薄的膠水。

vocab.ex: Please stick the photo firmly onto the application form.
vocab.exzh: 請把照片牢牢地黏在申請表上。

vocab.ex: She is nervous about the upcoming GEPT test.
vocab.exzh: 她對即將到來的全民英檢感到緊張。

vocab.ex: Send the application on time; otherwise, you will miss the test date.
vocab.exzh: 請準時寄出申請表，否則你會錯過考試日期。

2.translation: 在台灣有很多表格都要求貼上照片。在大多數的表格中，照片的標準尺寸為4*5公分。照片在護照、簽證及醫院記錄上格外重要。在這一類表格的上方會有一個小格子專門用來貼照片。照片一這女孩正從紅色小袋子裡拿出一張照片。她正在填寫參加全民英檢的報名表。這份表格需要貼上她的照片，以便監考人員在考試時能確認她的身份。這是為了防止她請別人代替她考試。照片二她在照片背後塗上膠水，然後把照片黏在報名表上。現在報名表準備要寄出去了。若一切順利，她的報名即將被受理，然後她便能參加最近的這次考試。不然，她就得等到下一梯次的測驗了。
answers: You have to attach a photo.
She is filling out an application for a GEPT test.
So that the test administrator can verify the girl’s identity when she takes the test.
paras_zh: 在台灣，有許多表格都需要附上照片。大部分表格規定的標準照片尺寸是 4 x 5 公分。這在護照、簽證和醫院病歷上尤其重要。這類表格上方都會有一個專門用來貼照片的格子。
圖片一 這個女孩正從一個帶紅色的小信封裡拿出一張照片。她正在填寫一份全民英檢（GEPT）的報名表。表格上需要她的照片，這樣監考人員在她應試時就能核對她的身分。這可以防止有人代替她去考試。
圖片二 她在照片背面塗上膠水，然後把照片貼到申請表上。現在申請表已經準備好可以寄出了，如果順利的話，她將會被核准參加即將到來的考試；否則，她就得等下一次考試了。

quiz.q: According to the passage, what is the standard size for photos on most forms in Taiwan?
options: 3 x 5 cm
5 x 7 cm
4 x 5 cm
2 x 3 cm

quiz.q: What does the girl do right after applying glue to the back of the photo?
options: She mails the application immediately.
She sticks it to the application.
She removes the photo from the envelope.
She waits for the next test.

quiz.q: What will happen if the girl is not accepted for the upcoming test?
options: She has to wait for the next test.
She will lose her passport.
She will get a refund.
She must reapply for a visa.

2.title: Henry’s Vanity
paras: Henry is quite fond of himself. He would stand in front of the mirror all day if his mother would allow it. He likes to try on many different outfits in his wardrobe and model them in front of the mirror, too. Sometimes, he even pretends he is on the catwalk modeling the latest fashions for all the designers to see.

Whatever his motivations are, his mother is disturbed by his vain performances and has removed all the mirrors and reflective surfaces from the house—even the toaster had to go. Now, if he wants to see himself, he has to fill the bathtub with water and stare at his reflection.
questions: What does Henry like to stand in front of?
How does Henry’s mother feel about him doing this?
What has she done to prevent him from staring at himself?

vocab.ex: His constant need for compliments comes from pure vanity.
vocab.exzh: 他不斷需要別人的讚美，完全出於虛榮心。

vocab.ex: She is very fond of chocolate cake.
vocab.exzh: 她非常喜歡巧克力蛋糕。

vocab.ex: He checked his hair in the mirror before leaving the house.
vocab.exzh: 他出門前在鏡子裡檢查了一下頭髮。

vocab.ex: Her parents do not allow her to stay out late.
vocab.exzh: 她的父母不允許她太晚回家。

vocab.ex: She wanted to try on the red dress before buying it.
vocab.exzh: 她想在買下那件紅色洋裝前先試穿看看。

vocab.ex: He picked out a new outfit for the party.
vocab.exzh: 他為派對挑選了一套新服裝。

vocab.ex: She keeps all her dresses in a large wardrobe.
vocab.exzh: 她把所有的洋裝都放在一個大衣櫃裡。

vocab.ex: The students took turns to model the costumes they had made.
vocab.exzh: 學生們輪流展示他們自己做的服裝。

vocab.ex: The children like to pretend they are superheroes.
vocab.exzh: 孩子們喜歡假裝自己是超級英雄。

vocab.ex: The models walked confidently down the catwalk.
vocab.exzh: 模特兒們自信地走在伸展台上。

vocab.ex: He always wants to buy the latest smartphone.
vocab.exzh: 他總是想買最新的智慧型手機。

vocab.ex: She is always interested in the newest fashion trends.
vocab.exzh: 她一直對最新的時尚潮流很感興趣。

vocab.ex: The famous designer created a beautiful new dress collection.
vocab.exzh: 那位知名設計師推出了一系列美麗的新洋裝。

vocab.ex: Whatever happens, we will support each other.
vocab.exzh: 無論發生什麼事，我們都會互相支持。

vocab.ex: Her main motivation for studying hard is to become a doctor.
vocab.exzh: 她努力讀書的主要動機是想成為一名醫生。

vocab.ex: The loud noise outside began to disturb the sleeping baby.
vocab.exzh: 外面的巨大噪音開始打擾到熟睡的嬰兒。

vocab.ex: He is so vain that he checks his reflection in every shop window.
vocab.exzh: 他非常自負，每經過一家店的櫥窗都要看看自己的倒影。

vocab.ex: The audience clapped loudly after the dancer's amazing performance.
vocab.exzh: 舞者精彩的演出結束後，觀眾們熱烈鼓掌。

vocab.ex: The lake's reflective surface looked like a mirror in the morning light.
vocab.exzh: 清晨的光線下，湖面反射的表面看起來就像一面鏡子。

vocab.ex: Water droplets covered the smooth surface of the table.
vocab.exzh: 水珠布滿了桌子光滑的表面。

vocab.ex: He put two slices of bread into the toaster for breakfast.
vocab.exzh: 他把兩片吐司放進烤麵包機準備當早餐。

vocab.ex: She filled the bathtub with warm water and bubbles.
vocab.exzh: 她在浴缸裡裝滿了溫水和泡泡。

vocab.ex: It is rude to stare at strangers on the street.
vocab.exzh: 盯著街上的陌生人看是不禮貌的。

vocab.ex: He saw his reflection in the still water of the pond.
vocab.exzh: 他在池塘平靜的水面上看見了自己的倒影。

2.translation: 亨利有點自戀。假如他的媽媽允許的話，他可能會一整天都站在鏡子前。他喜歡試穿衣櫥裡各種不同的服飾，然後在鏡子前展示一番。有時候，他甚至假裝自己正站在伸展台上，為所有的設計師展現最新流行的服飾。 不管他的動機為何，他如此愛慕虛榮的行徑，令他的媽媽困擾不已。她已經把家中所有表面可以反射的物品通通拿走，甚至連烤麵包機也一樣。現在，如果他想看到自己，他必須先將浴缸裝滿水，好凝視自己水中的倒影。
answers: He likes to stand in front of the mirror.
She is disturbed by it.
She has removed all the mirrors and reflective surfaces from the house.
paras_zh: 亨利相當自戀。如果媽媽允許的話，他可以整天站在鏡子前面。他也喜歡在衣櫃裡試穿各式各樣不同的服裝，然後站在鏡子前展示。有時候，他甚至會假裝自己走在伸展台上，向所有設計師展示最新的流行時裝。
不管他的動機是什麼，他媽媽對他這種愛慕虛榮的表演感到很困擾，於是把家裡所有的鏡子和反光表面都拿走了——就連烤麵包機也不例外。現在，如果他想看看自己的樣子，就得把浴缸裝滿水，然後盯著水中的倒影看。

quiz.q: Why does Henry pretend he is on the catwalk?
options: He is practicing for a real fashion show.
His mother told him to do it.
He wants to make his friends laugh.
He enjoys imagining himself modeling clothes for designers.

quiz.q: Why did Henry's mother remove the toaster from the house?
options: It was broken and unsafe to use.
She wanted to buy a new one.
It had a reflective surface Henry could see himself in.
Henry accidentally damaged it while posing.

quiz.q: How does Henry manage to see his reflection after the mirrors are removed?
options: He uses his phone camera.
He fills the bathtub with water and looks at the water's surface.
He asks his mother to describe him.
He looks at a window at night.

2.title: Bulletin Boards
paras: Schools often use main entrances to post announcements for students and parents to read. This school is no exception as they have put up a number of bulletin boards along the main hallway. To prevent important notices from being removed or vandalized, they have covered the bulletin boards with protective sheets of glass.

Some things you may find on the announcement boards are class schedules, upcoming events, student awards, or notices to remind students of important test dates. Diligent students always check the board to make sure that they know what's going on at their school. The school staff is responsible for periodically updating the boards with current information and removing anything that is outdated, so that the information posted is always current and accurate.
questions: Where do schools usually put bulletin boards?
Why are the bulletin boards covered with glass?
Who is responsible for updating the bulletin boards?

vocab.ex: The teacher pinned the new schedule onto the bulletin board in the hallway.
vocab.exzh: 老師把新的課程表釘在走廊的佈告欄上。

vocab.ex: Students gather near the main entrance before class begins.
vocab.exzh: 上課前，學生們會聚集在主要入口附近。

vocab.ex: The library is located on the main floor of the building.
vocab.exzh: 圖書館位於這棟建築物的主要樓層。

vocab.ex: The office will post the exam results tomorrow morning.
vocab.exzh: 辦公室明天早上會張貼考試成績。

vocab.ex: The principal made an important announcement during the morning assembly.
vocab.exzh: 校長在朝會時發布了一項重要通告。

vocab.ex: Every student must wear a uniform, and there are no exceptions to this rule, with the exception of physical education days.
vocab.exzh: 每位學生都必須穿制服，這項規定沒有例外，唯獨體育課那天是個例外。

vocab.ex: A notice about the field trip was sent home with every student.
vocab.exzh: 一份關於校外教學的通知已發給每位學生帶回家。

vocab.ex: Someone tried to vandalize the school wall with spray paint last night.
vocab.exzh: 昨晚有人試圖用噴漆任意破壞學校的牆壁。

vocab.ex: Workers wear protective gloves when handling chemicals in the lab.
vocab.exzh: 工作人員在實驗室處理化學物質時會戴上保護手套。

vocab.ex: A thin sheet of glass covers the wooden frame to protect the photo.
vocab.exzh: 一片薄薄的玻璃覆蓋在木框上，用來保護照片。

vocab.ex: You can check your class schedule on the bulletin board outside the office.
vocab.exzh: 你可以在辦公室外的佈告欄查看你的課程表。

vocab.ex: The sports day is the biggest event of the school year.
vocab.exzh: 運動會是這個學年最大的活動。

vocab.ex: She received an award for perfect attendance this semester.
vocab.exzh: 她這學期因全勤而獲得一個獎項。

vocab.ex: The sign on the board is meant to remind students about the upcoming test.
vocab.exzh: 佈告欄上的標示是用來提醒學生即將到來的考試。

vocab.ex: The school staff met early in the morning to plan the new schedule.
vocab.exzh: 學校全體工作人員一大早開會討論新的課程表。

vocab.ex: The fire alarm system is checked periodically to make sure it works properly.
vocab.exzh: 火災警報系統會定期檢查，以確保運作正常。

vocab.ex: The staff will update the bulletin board with new event dates every month.
vocab.exzh: 工作人員每個月都會用新的活動日期更新佈告欄。

vocab.ex: Please make sure the information on the board is current before the parents arrive.
vocab.exzh: 在家長到來之前，請確保佈告欄上的資訊是最新的。

vocab.ex: The bulletin board provides useful information about school events.
vocab.exzh: 佈告欄提供了關於學校活動的實用資訊。

vocab.ex: The staff removed the outdated notice and replaced it with a new one.
vocab.exzh: 工作人員撤下了過期的公告，換上了一則新的。

vocab.ex: It is important that the class schedule posted is completely accurate.
vocab.exzh: 張貼出來的課程表必須完全正確，這一點非常重要。

2.translation: 學校經常利用主要的入口張貼公告給學生和家長閱讀。這所學校也不例外，因為他們沿著川堂擺設一些佈告欄。為了防止重要的告示被移除或破壞，他們用防護的玻璃窗來遮住佈告欄。 一些你可以在佈告欄看到的訊息有班級課表、即將舉辦的活動、學生獎勵，或提醒學生重要考試日期的告示。用功的學生總會去查看佈告欄，以確認他們知道學校發生的事情。學校的職員負責定期更新佈告欄，他們張貼當前的資訊，並移除過期的資料，如此一來，張貼在上面的資訊就會一直保持是最新而且是正確的。
answers: They usually put bulletin boards in the main entrance.
They are covered with glass to prevent important notices from being removed or vandalized.
The school staff is responsible for updating the bulletin boards.
paras_zh: 學校經常利用主要入口張貼公告，供學生與家長閱讀。這所學校也不例外，他們在主要走廊上設置了好幾面佈告欄。為了防止重要的公告被撕除或任意破壞，他們在佈告欄上覆蓋了一層保護性的玻璃。
你可能會在公告欄上看到的內容包括課程表、即將舉行的活動、學生獎項，或是提醒學生重要考試日期的通知。認真的學生總是會查看佈告欄，以確保自己了解學校的最新動態。學校工作人員負責定期更新佈告欄上的資訊，並撤除任何過期的內容，以確保張貼的資訊始終保持最新且正確。

quiz.q: Why does the school cover its bulletin boards with sheets of glass?
options: To protect notices from being removed or vandalized
To make the announcements easier to read
To keep the boards clean from dust
To make the hallway look more decorative

quiz.q: According to the passage, what kind of students always check the bulletin board?
options: New students
Students who are late
Students who forgot their books
Diligent students

quiz.q: What is the school staff's responsibility regarding the bulletin boards?
options: To design colorful posters for the boards
To lock the boards so no one can touch them
To periodically update them and remove outdated information
To take photos of the boards for the yearbook

2.title: Bottled Water
paras: When you are on the go, the best option to re-hydrate yourself is bottled water. It is readily available at most stores and there are countless brands and types of water to choose from. The number of choices is almost mind-boggling. You have distilled water, spring water, carbonated water and flavored water—just to name a few. Whatever their name, they all serve the same purpose: quenching your thirst.

The lady pictured here has just unscrewed the lid on her bottle of water. When buying bottled water, make sure the safety seal is not broken, so that you can be sure that the water hasn’t been tampered with and is fresh. If the seal is broken, it is best not to drink it just to be on the safe side.

In the second picture, our beautiful young model is taking a drink. She tips back the bottle and starts guzzling it down like there’s no tomorrow. She seems to be very thirsty. Perhaps she was lost in the desert and was unable to find water to quench her thirst. After finishing, she tosses the empty bottle into the recycling bin.
questions: How can you be sure that your bottle of water is safe to drink
Do you have a favorite kind of water that you like to drink?
What does the lady do with the empty bottle?

vocab.ex: When you are always on the go, it is important to carry water with you.
vocab.exzh: 當你總是外出忙碌時，隨身攜帶水是很重要的。

vocab.ex: Buying a reusable bottle is a better option than buying plastic ones every day.
vocab.exzh: 買一個可重複使用的水瓶，比每天買塑膠瓶裝水是更好的選擇。

vocab.ex: After the long run, she drank plenty of water to re-hydrate her body.
vocab.exzh: 長跑之後，她喝了很多水來補充身體的水分。

vocab.ex: We bought several bottles of bottled water for the road trip.
vocab.exzh: 我們為了公路旅行買了好幾瓶瓶裝水。

vocab.ex: Bottled water is readily available at almost every convenience store.
vocab.exzh: 幾乎每一家便利商店都能輕易買到瓶裝水。

vocab.ex: There are countless brands of water lined up on the shelf.
vocab.exzh: 架上擺著數不清的水品牌。

vocab.ex: She always buys the same brand of spring water.
vocab.exzh: 她總是買同一個品牌的泉水。

vocab.ex: The number of flavors available was truly mind-boggling.
vocab.exzh: 可供選擇的口味數量實在多得驚人。

vocab.ex: The technician used distilled water to clean the machine parts.
vocab.exzh: 技師用蒸餾水清潔機器零件。

vocab.ex: This bottle contains natural spring water from the mountains.
vocab.exzh: 這瓶裝的是來自山區的天然泉水。

vocab.ex: He prefers carbonated water over still water because of the fizz.
vocab.exzh: 他喜歡含氣泡的碳酸水勝過無氣泡的水，因為喜歡那股氣泡感。

vocab.ex: Lemon-flavored water is a refreshing choice on a hot day.
vocab.exzh: 檸檬口味的水是炎熱天氣裡令人清爽的選擇。

vocab.ex: The store sells apples, oranges, and bananas, just to name a few.
vocab.exzh: 這家店賣蘋果、橘子和香蕉，這裡僅列出幾樣。

vocab.ex: All these different waters serve the same basic purpose.
vocab.exzh: 這些不同種類的水都適用於同一個基本目的。

vocab.ex: She had to unscrew the cap before she could take a sip.
vocab.exzh: 她必須先轉開瓶蓋才能喝一口水。

vocab.ex: Make sure the lid is tightly closed so the water does not spill.
vocab.exzh: 務必把蓋子蓋緊，以免水灑出來。

vocab.ex: For your own safety, always check the seal before drinking bottled water.
vocab.exzh: 為了你自身的安全，喝瓶裝水前務必先檢查封口。

vocab.ex: Do not buy the bottle if the seal is already broken.
vocab.exzh: 如果封口已經破損，就不要買這瓶水。

vocab.ex: It looked like someone had tried to tamper with the bottle cap.
vocab.exzh: 看起來似乎有人動過手腳破壞了瓶蓋。

vocab.w: to be on the safe side
vocab.ex: I threw the bottle away just to be on the safe side.
vocab.exzh: 為了安全起見，我把那瓶水丟掉了。

vocab.ex: He had to tip the bottle all the way back to get the last drop.
vocab.exzh: 他必須把瓶子往後傾斜到底，才能喝到最後一滴水。

vocab.ex: After the race, the runner began to guzzle down a whole bottle of water.
vocab.exzh: 比賽結束後，那位跑者開始牛飲整瓶水。

vocab.ex: Please do not toss the empty bottle on the ground.
vocab.exzh: 請不要把空瓶隨意丟在地上。

vocab.ex: She put the empty bottle into the recycling bin instead of the trash can.
vocab.exzh: 她把空瓶放進資源回收桶，而不是垃圾桶。

2.translation: 當你外出時，補充水分最好的選擇就是瓶裝水。你可以輕易地在大部分商店買到瓶裝水，而且有非常多的品牌及種類可以買，可供選擇的數量多到幾乎令人吃驚。你可以選擇蒸餾水、礦泉水、汽水，及加味水等等，這只是其中的一些種類而已。不管它們的名稱是什麼，他們要達成的目的都是一樣的：解渴。 照片中的女士，剛剛把她的瓶裝水的瓶蓋打開。當購買瓶裝水時，要確定安全封口是完好無缺的，如此一來，你才有把握這瓶水沒有被蓄意毀損，而且是全新的。如果安全封口有破損，為了安全著想，最好不要去喝它。 在第二張圖當中，我們這位年輕貌美的模特兒正在喝水。她把瓶子拿斜並且開始狂飲，好像沒有明天似的。她似乎感到非常口渴。也許她在沙漠中迷路，無法找到水來解渴。喝完水後，她將空瓶丟到資源回收桶裡。
answers: By checking to see if the safety seal is broken.
Yes, I like lemon-flavored water.
She puts it into the recycling bin. She recycles it.
paras_zh: 當你外出忙碌時，補充水分最好的選擇就是瓶裝水。幾乎每家商店都能輕易買到瓶裝水，而且品牌與種類多得數不清。可選擇的數量之多，簡直令人驚訝。你可以選擇蒸餾水、泉水、碳酸水，還有各種口味的水——這裡僅列出幾種。無論名稱為何，它們的用途都相同：解渴。
圖中這位女士剛轉開她那瓶水的瓶蓋。購買瓶裝水時，務必確認安全封口沒有被破壞，這樣你才能確定這瓶水沒有被動過手腳，而且是新鮮的。如果封口已經破損，為了安全起見，最好不要喝。
在第二張照片中，我們美麗的年輕模特兒正在喝水。她把瓶子往後傾斜，開始像沒有明天似地牛飲起來。她看起來非常口渴。也許她曾經迷失在沙漠中，找不到水來解渴。喝完之後，她把空瓶丟進資源回收桶。

quiz.q: According to the passage, why should you check the safety seal before drinking bottled water?
options: To find out which brand of water it is
To make sure the water has not been tampered with and is fresh
To check whether the water is carbonated
To see how much water is left in the bottle

quiz.q: What might explain why the young model in the second picture is guzzling the water so eagerly?
options: She may have been lost in the desert and very thirsty
She just bought a new brand of water
She was checking if the seal was broken
She was pouring the water into a recycling bin

quiz.q: Based on the passage, what is true about all the different types of water mentioned, such as distilled, spring, carbonated, and flavored water?
options: They all must be re-hydrated before drinking
They are all sold in the same brand
They all have broken safety seals
They all serve the same purpose of quenching thirst

3.title: How to Make a Scarecrow
paras: This is a scarecrow. It is standing alone in a field. Scarecrows are used to scare away birds that may eat crops. They are supposed to look like people standing in the field. If the birds think the field is inhabited, they will probably not stop there.

To make a scarecrow all you need are a couple of poles, some straw and an old shirt. First, you make a cross out of the poles and tie them together. Then you put a shirt on the cross and stuff it with straw. Next you need something that looks like a head to put on the top. Finally, put a hat on the head and you’re finished. Now all you have to do is to stick it in the ground in your field or garden.
Without scarecrows, some farmers may resort to shooting or poisoning birds. In reality, scarecrows save crops and birds. Everyone is a winner.
questions: Why do farmers put scarecrows up?
What does a scarecrow look like to a bird?
What things do you need to make a scarecrow?

vocab.ex: The farmer put a scarecrow in the field to keep the birds away.
vocab.exzh: 農夫在田裡放了一個稻草人來趕走鳥類。

vocab.ex: She likes to walk home alone after school.
vocab.exzh: 她喜歡放學後自己一個人走路回家。

vocab.ex: The cows were eating grass in the field.
vocab.exzh: 牛群正在田野上吃草。

vocab.ex: Farmers work hard all year to grow a good crop.
vocab.exzh: 農夫整年辛勤工作以種出好的農作物。

vocab.ex: You are supposed to finish your homework before dinner.
vocab.exzh: 你應該要在晚餐前完成作業。

vocab.ex: Many kinds of animals inhabit the forest.
vocab.exzh: 許多種類的動物居住在這片森林裡。

vocab.ex: It is probably going to rain later this afternoon.
vocab.exzh: 今天下午稍晚也許會下雨。

vocab.ex: He used a long pole to knock the fruit down from the tree.
vocab.exzh: 他用一根長竿把水果從樹上打下來。

vocab.ex: The farmer filled the barn with fresh straw.
vocab.exzh: 農夫在穀倉裡裝滿了新鮮的乾草。

vocab.ex: They tied two sticks together to form a cross.
vocab.exzh: 他們把兩根木棍綁在一起做成一個十字架。

vocab.ex: Please tie the rope tightly around the box.
vocab.exzh: 請把繩子緊緊地綁在箱子上。

vocab.ex: She likes to stuff the pillow with soft cotton.
vocab.exzh: 她喜歡把枕頭塞滿柔軟的棉花。

vocab.ex: He decided to stick the flag firmly into the ground.
vocab.exzh: 他決定把旗子牢牢地插進地裡。

vocab.ex: When nothing else worked, they had to resort to asking for help.
vocab.exzh: 當其他方法都行不通時，他們只好求助於他人。

vocab.ex: The hunter did not want to shoot the deer in the field.
vocab.exzh: 獵人不想在田野裡射擊那隻鹿。

vocab.ex: Farmers should be careful not to poison the birds by accident.
vocab.exzh: 農夫應該小心，不要意外毒害到鳥類。

vocab.ex: The plan sounded difficult, but in reality it was quite simple.
vocab.exzh: 這個計畫聽起來很難，但實際上相當簡單。

vocab.ex: Everyone cheered when the winner was announced.
vocab.exzh: 當贏家被宣布時，大家都歡呼了起來。

3.translation: 這是一個稻草人，它獨自地站在稻田裡。稻草人被用來嚇走可能會吃農作物的鳥類。它們看起來應該要像是有人站在田裡的樣子。如果鳥類以為田裡有人居住，它們可能就不會在那裡停留。 做一個稻草人只需要兩根竿子、一些乾草和一件舊襯衫。首先，用竿子做成一個十字架，並且把它們綁起來。然後把襯衫穿在十字架上面，並在裡面塞進乾草。接下來，你需要將一個看起來像頭部的東西放在頂端。最後，放一頂帽子在頭上就大功告成了。現在，你只需將它插在你田裡或菜園的土裡即可。 不用稻草人的話，有些農夫可能會用射殺或毒害小鳥的方式。事實上，稻草人拯救了農作物和鳥類。每個人都是贏家。
answers: They put them up to scare away birds.
It looks like a person standing in the field.
You need a couple of poles, some straw, an old shirt, a hat and something that looks like a head.
paras_zh: 這是一個稻草人。牠獨自站在田野裡。稻草人是用來嚇走可能會吃掉農作物的鳥類。牠們應該要看起來像站在田裡的人。如果鳥類以為這片田野裡有人居住，牠們也許就不會停留在那裡。
要做一個稻草人，你只需要兩根竿子、一些乾草和一件舊襯衫。首先，你用竿子做成一個十字架並把它們綁在一起。接著把襯衫套在十字架上，並用乾草把它塞滿。然後你需要一個看起來像頭的東西放在頂端。最後，在頭上戴一頂帽子，這樣就完成了。現在你只需要把它插牢在你的田地或花園的地上。
如果沒有稻草人，有些農夫可能會採取射殺或毒害鳥類的手段。實際上，稻草人既能保護農作物，也能保護鳥類。每個人都是贏家。

quiz.q: According to the passage, why might scarecrows prevent birds from stopping in a field?
options: Birds think the field is inhabited by a person
Birds are afraid of loud noises
Birds cannot see the crops clearly
Birds do not like the smell of straw

quiz.q: What is the correct order for making a scarecrow, based on the passage?
options: Put on a hat, make a cross, stuff with straw, add a shirt
Stuff straw into the ground, then add poles and a shirt
Add a head first, then build the cross, then add straw
Make a cross with poles, put a shirt on it and stuff with straw, add a head, then a hat

quiz.q: Why does the passage say that scarecrows make 'everyone a winner'?
options: They are cheap and easy to sell
They help farmers win awards for their fields
They save both the crops and the birds from harm
They attract more birds to the farm

3.title: The Claw Game
paras: Luke is trying to win a stuffed toy for his daughter. To operate the claw vending machine, 3 he first needs to insert a coin. When he hears the music start, it's time for action. He uses the joystick to maneuver the claw forward, backward, left and right. When the claw is positioned over top of the toy, he hits the button on the right. The open claw then drops down and attempts to grab a prize. At this point the claw, and Luke’s prize, begin to return to the top of the machine. Usually when the grapple hook gets to the top, it drops the item and ends your turn, but not this time. The claw has a firm hold on the stuffed toy, it then moves toward the hole on the left side. Luke is excited because he has never had a toy make it this far. The claw slowly opens and drops the prize into the hole. Luke reaches in and proudly claims his prize. More often than not, people walk away without a toy and without any change left in their pockets, but on this day, Luke was one of the lucky ones.
questions: How does Luke know when his turn starts?
What does he use to move the claw around?
What usually happens when the claw gets to the top?

vocab.ex: The claw slowly closed around the small stuffed animal.
vocab.exzh: 那個爪形器具緩緩地夾住了那隻小玩偶。

vocab.ex: It takes some practice to operate the claw machine well.
vocab.exzh: 要熟練地操作夾娃娃機需要一些練習。

vocab.ex: She bought a cold drink from the vending machine near the entrance.
vocab.exzh: 她在入口附近的販賣機買了一瓶冷飲。

vocab.ex: You need to insert a coin before the machine will start.
vocab.exzh: 你必須先投入一枚硬幣，機器才會啟動。

vocab.ex: He pushed the joystick to the right to move the claw.
vocab.exzh: 他把控制桿往右推，讓爪子移動。

vocab.ex: She tried to maneuver the claw carefully over the toy.
vocab.exzh: 她試著小心地移動爪子到玩偶上方。

vocab.ex: He pushed the joystick forward to move the claw closer.
vocab.exzh: 他把控制桿向前推，讓爪子靠得更近。

vocab.ex: The claw moved backward when he pulled the joystick toward himself.
vocab.exzh: 當他把控制桿往自己方向拉時，爪子就向後移動。

vocab.ex: He needed to position the claw exactly above the prize.
vocab.exzh: 他需要把爪子準確地放在獎品的正上方。

vocab.ex: The claw will attempt to grab the toy when the button is pressed.
vocab.exzh: 按下按鈕後，爪子就會嘗試抓取玩偶。

vocab.ex: The claw opened wide, ready to grab the stuffed toy.
vocab.exzh: 爪子張得大大的，準備要抓取那個玩偶。

vocab.ex: Luke was thrilled to finally win a prize from the machine.
vocab.exzh: 路克終於從機器裡贏得獎品，感到非常興奮。

vocab.ex: At this point, the claw began to rise toward the top of the machine.
vocab.exzh: 在這個時間點，爪子開始往機器頂部升起。

vocab.ex: The metal grapple tightened around the toy as it lifted.
vocab.exzh: 金屬抓具在提起玩偶時緊緊夾住它。

vocab.ex: A small hook on the claw helps it hold on to the prize.
vocab.exzh: 爪子上的一個小鉤子能幫助它抓住獎品。

vocab.ex: The claw usually drops the item before it reaches the top.
vocab.exzh: 爪子通常在到達頂部之前就會把物品掉落。

vocab.ex: The claw kept a firm hold on the toy all the way to the hole.
vocab.exzh: 爪子一路牢牢抓住玩偶，直到抵達洞口。

vocab.ex: Luke had to reach into the machine to get his prize.
vocab.exzh: 路克必須把手伸進機器裡拿他的獎品。

vocab.ex: He proudly showed his new stuffed toy to his daughter.
vocab.exzh: 他驕傲地把新得到的玩偶秀給女兒看。

vocab.ex: Winners can claim their prize from the slot at the bottom.
vocab.exzh: 獲勝者可以從底部的取物口取走獎品。

vocab.ex: He checked his pocket for extra change to try again.
vocab.exzh: 他檢查口袋裡是否還有多的零錢可以再玩一次。

vocab.ex: He put the last coin from his pocket into the machine.
vocab.exzh: 他把口袋裡最後一枚硬幣投進機器裡。

3.translation: 路克正試著為他的女兒贏得一個填充玩具。要操作夾娃娃機，首先他必須投入一枚硬幣。當他聽到音樂啟動時，就是開始行動的時候了。他用搖桿操作機器夾，讓它前、後、左、右的移動。當機器夾移動到玩具上方的位置時，他按下右邊的按鈕。這時，張開的機器夾會下降，並且試著抓住獎品。在這個時候，機器夾和路克的獎品開始回到機器的上方。通常當抓鉤到達頂端時，抓住的東西會掉下來，遊戲就結束了，但路克這次並沒有這樣，機器夾牢牢地抓住了填充玩具，並且移動到左邊的洞口。他非常的興奮，因為他從未把玩具夾到這麼遠。機器夾慢慢地張開，把獎品投到洞口。路克把手伸進去，驕傲地取出他的獎品。人們多半在離開時沒有夾到玩具，而且口袋裡的零錢也花光了。但是今天路克是屬於幸運的一員。
answers: He knows because he hears music.
He uses a joystick to move the claw around.
It usually drops your prize.
paras_zh: 路克正努力想幫女兒贏得一隻絨毛玩偶。要操作這台夾娃娃機，他得先投入一枚硬幣。當他聽到音樂響起時，就知道該行動了。他利用控制桿操縱爪子前後左右移動。當爪子被移到玩偶正上方時，他按下右邊的按鈕。張開的爪子隨即往下降，試圖抓住獎品。這時，爪子連同路克的獎品，開始返回機器頂端。通常抓具一到達頂端，就會把東西掉下去，這一局就結束了，但這次不一樣。爪子牢牢抓住絨毛玩偶，接著朝左側的洞口移動。路克非常興奮，因為他從來沒有讓玩偶撐到這麼後面過。爪子慢慢張開，把獎品掉進洞口。路克把手伸進去，驕傲地拿走他的獎品。大多數時候，人們會空手而歸，口袋裡的零錢也所剩無幾，但這一天，路克是幸運的其中一人。

quiz.q: Why did Luke need to insert a coin into the machine?
options: To buy a drink
To operate the claw vending machine
To turn on the lights
To print a ticket

quiz.q: What was unusual about this particular turn on the claw machine?
options: The claw kept a firm hold on the toy all the way to the hole instead of dropping it
The claw broke down halfway
Luke needed two coins instead of one
The music did not start

quiz.q: According to the passage, what usually happens to most people who play the claw machine?
options: They always win a prize on the first try
They get their coin back automatically
They are given a free replay
They walk away without a toy and with little or no change left

3.title: Correcting Your Mistakes
paras: When making study notes, it is a good idea to keep them neat and legible. Sloppy notes will only lead to confusion and lost time when you study. Try to use pencil as much as possible because it is easier to correct mistakes and then when you do mess up, you can simply erase the mistake and write it again. This will help to keep your work nice and neat.

If you must work with a pen, or are working with photocopied materials, using an eraser is not an option. In these cases you must use either correction fluid or correction tape for your mistakes. I find correction tape the tidiest and you can write over it almost as soon as it is applied, too. Correction fluid is okay as well, but it will require drying time and doesn’t look quite as nice. Sometimes dried bits of liquid paper end up on your work, making it difficult to write neatly. But in a pinch, I suppose, it is better than nothing.
questions: How should your study notes look?
What should you do with your mistakes?
Which is tidier, correction tape or correction fluid?

vocab.ex: You should correct your spelling mistakes before handing in the essay.
vocab.exzh: 你應該在交出這篇文章之前修正拼字錯誤。

vocab.ex: Everyone makes a mistake from time to time when they are learning something new.
vocab.exzh: 每個人在學習新事物時，偶爾都會犯錯。

vocab.ex: She always keeps her desk clean and neat.
vocab.exzh: 她總是把書桌整理得乾淨整齊。

vocab.ex: Please make sure your handwriting is legible so the teacher can read it.
vocab.exzh: 請確保你的字跡清楚易讀，這樣老師才看得懂。

vocab.ex: His sloppy handwriting made it hard for anyone to read his notes.
vocab.exzh: 他潦草的字跡讓大家都很難讀懂他的筆記。

vocab.ex: Not studying regularly can lead to poor grades.
vocab.exzh: 不規律讀書可能會導致成績不佳。

vocab.ex: The teacher's unclear instructions caused a lot of confusion among the students.
vocab.exzh: 老師含糊不清的指示讓學生們感到十分困惑。

vocab.ex: Do not mess up your notebook with random doodles.
vocab.exzh: 不要在筆記本上亂塗亂畫，把它弄得亂七八糟。

vocab.ex: If you make a mistake in pencil, you can simply erase it.
vocab.exzh: 如果你用鉛筆寫錯了，你只需要把它擦掉就好。

vocab.ex: He used an eraser to erase the wrong answer.
vocab.exzh: 他用橡皮擦把錯誤的答案擦掉。

vocab.ex: The teacher handed out photocopied pages of the story for everyone to read.
vocab.exzh: 老師發下影印的故事講義給大家閱讀。

vocab.ex: Please bring all your study material to class tomorrow.
vocab.exzh: 請明天把你所有的學習材料帶來上課。

vocab.ex: Using a pencil gives you the option of erasing your mistakes.
vocab.exzh: 使用鉛筆讓你有機會可以把錯誤擦掉。

vocab.ex: She dabbed some correction fluid over the wrong word.
vocab.exzh: 她在寫錯的字上塗了一些修正液。

vocab.ex: I prefer correction tape because it dries instantly.
vocab.exzh: 我比較喜歡用修正帶，因為它馬上就乾了。

vocab.ex: You need to apply the correction fluid carefully so it does not smear.
vocab.exzh: 你需要小心塗上修正液，才不會弄髒暈開。

vocab.ex: Correction fluid works fine, but correction tape is convenient as well.
vocab.exzh: 修正液用起來還不錯，但修正帶用起來也很方便。

vocab.ex: Correction fluid will require some time to dry before you can write over it.
vocab.exzh: 修正液需要一些時間乾燥，之後才能在上面書寫。

vocab.ex: If you are not careful, dried bits of correction fluid can end up on your paper.
vocab.exzh: 如果不小心，乾掉的修正液碎屑最後可能會掉在你的紙上。

vocab.ex: Correction fluid is a thick, white liquid substance.
vocab.exzh: 修正液是一種濃稠的白色液態物質。

vocab.ex: In a pinch, correction fluid is better than nothing at all.
vocab.exzh: 在緊要關頭，修正液總比什麼都沒有好。

vocab.ex: I suppose correction tape is the neater choice of the two.
vocab.exzh: 我猜想修正帶是這兩者之中比較整潔的選擇。

3.translation: 當你在做筆記時，保持字跡工整與易讀是很好的。當你在讀書時，筆記的字跡潦草只會導致困惑，而且浪費時間。儘量使用鉛筆，因為錯誤比較容易修正，而且當你的筆記弄亂時，你可以簡單地擦掉錯誤並且重寫。這將有助於使你的筆記非常整齊。 假如你必須用原子筆，或是處理影印的資料，你是不能選擇使用橡皮擦的。在這種情形下，你必須使用修正液或修正帶來處理你的錯誤。我覺得使用修正帶是最整潔的方式，而且你還可以在用了修正帶後，馬上寫上去。修正液也可以使用，但是它需要變乾的時間，而且看起來不像用修正帶那樣地好看。有時候修正液乾掉後形成的小紙塊讓你更難寫得整齊。但我想在緊急情況時，這總比什麼都沒有要來得好。
answers: They should look neat and legible.
You should correct/erase/change them.
Correction tape is tidier.
paras_zh: 做讀書筆記時，把筆記寫得整齊清楚是個好主意。潦草的筆記只會在你複習時造成困惑，浪費時間。盡量使用鉛筆，因為這樣比較容易修正錯誤，一旦你寫錯了，只要把錯誤擦掉再重寫一次就好。這樣有助於讓你的筆記保持整潔美觀。
如果你必須用原子筆寫字，或是使用影印的資料，那麼用橡皮擦就不是一個可行的辦法了。在這些情況下，你必須使用修正液或修正帶來處理錯誤。我覺得修正帶是最整潔的方式，而且幾乎塗上去之後馬上就可以在上面書寫。修正液也還不錯，但它需要一些乾燥的時間，看起來也不會那麼美觀。有時候，乾掉的修正液碎屑會殘留在你的作業上，讓你難以寫得整齊。不過在緊要關頭，我想它總比什麼都沒有好。

quiz.q: Why does the passage recommend using pencil for study notes?
options: Pencils are cheaper than pens
Pencils make your handwriting more legible automatically
It is easier to correct mistakes with a pencil
Teachers require students to use pencils

quiz.q: According to the passage, why can you not use an eraser on photocopied materials?
options: Photocopied materials are too expensive to risk erasing
Erasers do not work on photocopied paper the way they do with pencil
Erasing is against school rules
Photocopied paper is too thin and will tear

quiz.q: What downside of correction fluid does the passage mention?
options: It needs time to dry and dried bits can end up on your work
It is more expensive than correction tape
It cannot be used on photocopied materials
It only comes in one color

3.title: A Pocketful of Change
paras: Paul’s pocket was heavy with change after a day of shopping. He reached his hand into his pocket and grabbed handful after handful of change, placing it on the coffee table. Sitting on the comfy armchair, he began to stack the change. He put the one dollar coins in stacks of 10 to make them easier to count. After he sorted them all out, he added them up. He had nearly 200 dollars in change. It was no wonder his pocket felt so heavy. He took the change and put it in his piggybank to save it for a day when he would want to buy something.
questions: Why was Paul’s pocket so heavy?
Why did he put them in stacks of 10?
Where did he put the change after he counted it?

vocab.ex: She carried a pocketful of coins to the store.
vocab.exzh: 她帶著一袋子的硬幣去商店。

vocab.ex: He took a handful of candy from the jar.
vocab.exzh: 他從罐子裡抓了一把糖果。

vocab.ex: Please place the books on the shelf.
vocab.exzh: 請把書放在書架上。

vocab.ex: This sofa is very comfy to sit on.
vocab.exzh: 這張沙發坐起來非常舒適。

vocab.ex: Grandpa likes to read the newspaper in his armchair.
vocab.exzh: 爺爺喜歡坐在扶手椅上看報紙。

vocab.ex: She likes to stack the plates neatly in the cupboard.
vocab.exzh: 她喜歡把盤子整齊地堆疊在櫥櫃裡。

vocab.ex: It took an hour to sort out all the old photographs.
vocab.exzh: 花了一個小時才把所有的舊照片分類好。

vocab.ex: He used a calculator to add up the total cost of the groceries.
vocab.exzh: 他用計算機把買菜的總金額加起來。

vocab.ex: It is nearly midnight, so we should go to bed soon.
vocab.exzh: 現在將近午夜了，我們應該快點去睡覺。

vocab.ex: You skipped breakfast, so no wonder you are hungry now.
vocab.exzh: 你沒吃早餐，難怪你現在餓了。

vocab.ex: Every week, Amy puts some coins into her piggybank.
vocab.exzh: 艾咪每週都會把一些硬幣放進她的撲滿裡。

3.translation: 購物了一整天之後，保羅的口袋因為裝零錢變得很重。他把手伸進他的口袋，然後拿出一把一把的零錢放在咖啡桌上。他坐在舒適的扶手椅上，開始把硬幣疊起來。他把一元的硬幣十個十個疊成堆，這樣比較容易數。在他把全部的硬幣分類好之後，他計算了零錢的總數。他的零錢將近有兩百元，難怪他的口袋感覺這麼重。他把零錢放入撲滿存起來，以便改天想買東西時就可以拿來用。
answers: It was heavy because it was full of change.
He put them in stacks of 10 to make them easier to count.
He put it in his piggybank.
paras_zh: 保羅逛了一整天街，口袋裡裝滿了沉甸甸的零錢。他把手伸進口袋，一把接一把地抓出零錢，放在咖啡桌上。他坐在舒適的扶手椅上，開始把零錢堆疊起來。他把一元硬幣每十個疊成一堆，這樣比較方便計算。把所有的零錢都分類好之後，他把它們加總起來。他竟然有將近兩百元的零錢，難怪他的口袋會覺得這麼沉重。他把零錢收起來，放進他的撲滿裡存起來，留待將來想買東西的那一天使用。

quiz.q: What did Paul do first when he sat down in the armchair?
options: He counted his paper money.
He put the coins into his piggybank.
He went shopping for more items.
He began to stack the change.

quiz.q: Why did Paul arrange the one dollar coins in stacks of 10?
options: To make them look neat on the table.
Because the piggybank required it.
To make them easier to count.
To separate the different coin types.

quiz.q: What does Paul plan to do with the money in his piggybank?
options: Give it to his parents to keep.
Save it until he wants to buy something.
Exchange it for paper bills at a bank.
Count it again the next day.

3.title: The Dish Dryer
paras: Lester has just finished washing the dishes. After he washes the dishes, he places each of them into the dish-dryer above the sink. The wire rack in the dryer holds plates and bowls on their side. This allows air to flow freely between the dishes as they dry. It also helps to organize the dishes so that you can fit more of them into the dryer. There is a spot on the side for utensils, too. It holds them upright, which allows water to drip off of them.

When the machine is fully loaded, he can turn it on, but he must close the door first. The glass door slides out from above and then swings down to cover the front of the unit. To turn it on, he needs to select the proper cycle and push the button. The machine will then dry the dishes with hot air.
The whole process takes about ten minutes or so. For the sake of energy conservation, you should try to make sure the machine is full before you turn it on.
questions: How does Lester place the dishes in the dish rack?
Why are the dishes placed on their side?
How long does the whole process take?

vocab.ex: We put the wet plates into the dish dryer after dinner.
vocab.exzh: 晚餐後我們把濕的盤子放進烘碗機裡。

vocab.ex: She washed the vegetables in the sink before cooking.
vocab.exzh: 她煮飯前先在水槽裡把蔬菜洗乾淨。

vocab.ex: The fence is made of thin metal wire.
vocab.exzh: 那道籬笆是用細金屬線做成的。

vocab.ex: He hung his coat on the rack by the door.
vocab.exzh: 他把外套掛在門邊的架子上。

vocab.ex: The frame will hold the picture in place on the wall.
vocab.exzh: 這個相框可以把照片固定在牆上。

vocab.ex: He placed the book on its side to save space on the shelf.
vocab.exzh: 他把書側放以節省書架的空間。

vocab.ex: Fresh air can flow through the open window.
vocab.exzh: 新鮮空氣可以透過打開的窗戶流通進來。

3.translation: 列斯特剛把碗盤洗好。他洗完之後，把碗盤一一的放到洗滌槽上面的烘碗機裡。烘碗機裡的金屬架可以讓碗盤側放，這樣一來，在烘乾的時候，空氣可以不受阻礙地在碗盤之間流動。這種方式也能將碗盤排放整齊，讓你能放更多的碗盤到烘碗機裡。在烘碗機的邊緣還有個放餐具的位置，餐具放得直直的，好讓水可以滴下來。 當烘碗機裝滿碗盤之後，他就可以打開電源了。但是在啟動之前，他得先把門關上才行。玻璃門從上方滑出，然後往下擺動蓋住烘碗機的前面。要啟動時，他必須選擇適合的循環模式，並且按下按鈕，機器便會開始用熱空氣來烘乾碗盤了。整個過程大概會花十分鐘左右。為了節約能源起見，在啟動之前，你應該先確認烘碗機是否有擺滿碗盤。
answers: He places them in on their side/edge.
They are placed on their side to allow the air to flow freely through them.
It takes about 10 minutes or so.
paras_zh: 萊斯特剛洗完碗盤。洗完之後,他把每一件碗盤都放進水槽上方的烘碗機裡。烘碗機裡的金屬線架會把盤子和碗側放。這樣能讓空氣在碗盤之間自由流通,幫助它們乾得更快。這麼做也有助於把碗盤排放得整整齊齊,讓你能放進更多東西。烘碗機側邊還有一個放餐具的地方。它能讓餐具直立擺放,這樣水就能從上面滴落下來。
當機器裝滿之後,他就可以把它打開,但必須先把門關上。玻璃門會從上方滑出,然後向下擺動,蓋住機器的正面。要啟動機器,他需要選擇適當的行程,然後按下按鈕。接著機器就會用熱風把碗盤烘乾。
整個過程大約需要十分鐘左右。為了節約能源,你應該盡量確定機器裝滿了之後再打開它。

quiz.q: What is the purpose of placing plates and bowls on their side in the rack?

To make them look neater on the shelf
To keep them from breaking during washing
To make the dryer quieter while running

It prevents the dishes from breaking

3.title: Andre Makes a Scrapbook
paras: Hi, my name is Andre and I love collecting basketball memorabilia. I collect pins, hats, stickers, shirts, banners and anything I can get my hands on. In my spare time I have also made a scrapbook.

My favorite team has always been the Denver Nuggets, so the bulk of the book is of them. Every day I look through the sports sections of the local and national papers. If I find an interesting article about a Nuggets’ player, I cut it outand put it in my scrapbook. There are pages and pages of such newspaper clippings in my book.

As long as I can remember, Allen Iverson has been my favorite player. I actually have another scrapbook just for him. Inside this book there are pictures that I have collected from various magazines and papers. They span his entire career. I have pictures of his first pro game, his first all-star game, and even some of his personal life. As well, every milestone he has passed is documented in my book. I think I must be his biggest fan.

When I am old, I will be able to look back through my scrapbooks and remember my heroes in all their glory. Hopefully, my children will want to look at them, too. Maybe it will inspire them to start a scrapbook of their own.
questions: What kinds of things does Andre like to collect?
What does Andre do in his spare time?
Have you ever made a scrapbook about something or someone?

vocab.ex: She keeps a scrapbook filled with photos from her childhood.
vocab.exzh: 她保存了一本裝滿童年照片的剪貼簿。

3.translation: 嗨，我叫做安德烈。我喜歡收集一些籃球紀念品。我會收集胸針、帽子、貼紙、運動衫、旗子，以及任何我可以得到的東西。在我空閒的時候，我也製作了一本剪貼簿。 我最喜愛的球隊一直都是丹佛金塊隊，所以我剪貼簿的資料大部分都是關於他們的。我每天都會瀏覽地方與全國性報紙的體育版，如果我看到一篇關於金塊隊球員有趣的文章，我都會把它剪下來貼到我的剪貼簿上。我的本子裡有一頁頁像這樣子的新聞剪報。 自從我有記憶以來，艾倫‧艾弗森一直是我最喜愛的球員。事實上，我有另一本他專屬的剪貼簿。在這本簿子裡，有我從不同的雜誌和報紙收集來的照片。這些照片涵蓋了他整個籃球生涯。我有他初次在職籃比賽的照片，他第一場明星賽的照片，甚至一些個人的生活照。此外，他所經歷過的所有重要事件都記錄在我的本子裡。我想我一定是他的頭號粉絲。 當我老的時候，我會去回顧我的剪貼簿，並回想我那些處於光榮時期的英雄。但願我的小孩也會想要看看他們，這也許能激發他們開始製作自己的剪貼簿呢！
answers: He collects basketball pins, banners, hats, stickers and shirts (memoribilia).
He makes scrapbooks.
Yes, I have made a scrapbook about a professional baseball player.
paras_zh: 嗨，我叫安德烈，我熱愛蒐集籃球紀念品。我蒐集別針、帽子、貼紙、球衣、旗子，以及任何我能弄到手的東西。閒暇時，我還做了一本剪貼簿。
我最喜歡的球隊一直是丹佛金塊隊，所以這本剪貼簿裡大部分都是關於他們的內容。我每天都會翻閱地方報和全國性報紙的體育版。如果我發現一篇關於金塊隊球員的有趣文章，我就會把它剪下來，貼進我的剪貼簿裡。我的剪貼簿裡有好幾頁都是這樣的剪報。
從我有記憶以來，艾倫‧艾佛森一直是我最喜歡的球員。我甚至還有另一本專門為他做的剪貼簿。這本剪貼簿裡收藏著我從各種雜誌和報紙上蒐集來的照片，涵蓋了他整個球員生涯。我有他第一場職業比賽的照片、他第一次參加明星賽的照片，甚至還有一些他私生活的照片。此外，他所達成的每一個重要里程碑，我也都記錄在我的剪貼簿裡。我想我一定是他最忠實的頭號粉絲。
等我老了以後，就能翻閱我的剪貼簿，回味我心目中英雄們的光榮時刻。希望我的孩子們也會想看看這些剪貼簿。或許這也會激勵他們開始製作屬於自己的剪貼簿。

3.title: Posing for a Picture
paras: At one of the local markets, an artist has set up a shop. There are examples of his work hung on the walls for you to admire. If you want, he will draw your picture, too.

Posing for a picture will require that you sit very still. If you move around too much, it will be difficult for the artist to sketch your picture. As you sit, he will study the lines and shape of your face, and then capture them on paper. He will try to make you as beautiful as possible by enhancing your good features and maybe overlooking some of the bad ones. Don’t expect miracles, though, as there is only so much he can hide.

The whole process will take about 20 minutes or so. When he has finished, you will have immortalized yourself in a piece of art and can hang it on a wall in your house for all to see. Don’t buy too many pictures of yourself, though, as your family might get sick of looking at you on every wall in the house.
questions: Why does he hang examples of his work on the wall?
What do you have to do if you pose for a picture?
How long does the process take?

vocab.ex: Learning a new language is a slow but rewarding process.

vocab.ex: The sculptor wanted to immortalize the hero in a bronze statue.

3.translation: 在當地的一個市場裡，有一位藝術家設立一間工作室。牆上掛著許多他作品的樣本供你欣賞。如果你願意的話，他也可以為你畫一張畫像。 擺姿勢畫像時，你必須端坐不動。如果你動得太厲害，畫家很難為你畫一張素描。當你坐著時，他會仔細地觀察你臉上的線條及輪廓，然後將這些特點描繪在紙上。畫家會試著加強你的優點，也許也會忽略一些缺點，盡可能讓你看起來美麗動人。然而，別期望奇蹟的出現，因為他能掩飾的就只有這麼多。 整個素描的過程需要花費約二十分鐘左右。當他完成時，你將得以永恆不朽地活在藝術品中，而且可以把畫像掛在家中的牆上供大家欣賞。然而，千萬不要買太多自己的畫像，若是每一面牆都會看到你的畫像，你的家人也許會感到很厭煩。
answers: He hangs them on the wall for people to admire and for advertising purposes.
You have to sit very still.
It takes about 20 minutes or so.
paras_zh: 在當地的一個市集裡，有位畫家擺了一個攤位。牆上掛著他的作品範例，供人欣賞。如果你願意，他也可以幫你畫一幅肖像。
擺姿勢讓人畫像時，你必須坐得非常靜止不動。如果你動來動去，畫家就很難把你的樣子畫出來。當你坐著的時候，他會仔細端詳你臉部的線條與輪廓，然後把它們畫在紙上。他會盡量凸顯你美好的特徵，也許還會略過一些不太好看的部分，好讓你看起來盡可能美麗。不過別期望會出現奇蹟，因為能修飾掩蓋的畢竟有限。
整個過程大約需要20分鐘左右。畫完之後，你就把自己永遠留存在一幅藝術作品裡，可以掛在家裡牆上讓大家欣賞。不過別買太多自己的畫像，免得家人看膩了家裡每面牆上都是你的臉。

quiz.q: What does the artist do before drawing your picture?
options: He takes a photograph of you first

He studies the lines and shape of your face

quiz.q: According to the passage, why should you not expect a 'miracle' from the artist?
options: Because he only draws landscapes
