9.title: Cherry Tomatoes
paras: This lady is washing tomatoes. The tomatoes are in an aluminum bowl. She puts them under running water to rinse them off. She does this to remove any dirt or chemicals. Once rinsed, she will drain off the excess water into the sink. Now the tomatoes are ready to be eaten or cooked.

These children look very happy eating the little tomatoes. The tomatoes are served on a small decorative platter. Tomatoes can be eaten by themselves, like an apple, or in a salad with other fruits and vegetables. They are very good for your health and contain many different vitamins.

Tomatoes grow on vines and are green before they ripen. While they ripen, they slowly turn red and become softer and sweeter just like other fruits. Tomatoes that are ripened “on the vine” are thought to be the best for eating. But sometimes they have to be picked when they are green so that they can be shipped to far away stores. Very ripe tomatoes turn a dark red, are mushy and hard to cut.
To make a tomato ripen faster, try setting it next to a banana! Bananas release a chemical as they ripen that helps the tomato to ripen faster. It may sound strange, but it’s a scientific fact.
questions: Why does the lady rinse the tomatoes?
What happens to tomatoes as they ripen?
What can help a tomato ripen faster?

vocab.ex: She put a handful of cherry tomatoes into the salad.
vocab.exzh: 她把一把小番茄放進沙拉裡。

vocab.ex: The soup was stored in a large aluminum pot.

vocab.ex: Please rinse the grapes before you eat them.

vocab.ex: She poured off the excess oil from the pan.

vocab.ex: Grapes grow on a long, twisting vine.

vocab.ex: The overripe banana had become soft and mushy.

vocab.ex: Oranges are rich in vitamin C.

vocab.ex: Some fruits produce a natural chemical that helps them ripen.
vocab.exzh: 有些水果會產生一種天然的化學物質，幫助它們成熟。

vocab.ex: After boiling the noodles, drain off the hot water.

vocab.ex: The company will ship the fresh fruit to stores across the country.

vocab.ex: It is a scientific fact that plants need sunlight to grow.
vocab.exzh: 植物需要陽光才能生長是一項科學事實。

9.translation: 這位女士正在洗番茄。番茄放在鋁盆裡，她把它們放到流動的水下沖洗，去除泥土或化學物質。沖洗後，她會把多餘的水倒進水槽。現在番茄可以吃，也可以拿去烹調了。這些孩子看起來很開心地吃著小番茄。番茄放在一個小裝飾盤上。番茄可以像蘋果一樣單獨吃，也可以和其他水果蔬菜一起做成沙拉。它們對健康很好，含有許多不同的維生素。番茄長在藤上，成熟前是綠色的。成熟時會慢慢變紅，並像其他水果一樣變得更軟更甜。人們認為在藤上成熟的番茄最適合食用。不過有時它們必須在還是綠色時採收，才能運送到很遠的商店。非常熟的番茄會變成深紅色，軟爛而且很難切。想讓番茄更快成熟，可以試著把它放在香蕉旁邊。香蕉成熟時會釋放一種化學物質，幫助番茄更快成熟。這聽起來很奇怪，但這是科學事實。
answers: She rinses them to remove dirt or chemicals.
They turn red and become softer and sweeter.
Setting it next to a banana can help it ripen faster.
paras_zh: 這位女士正在洗番茄。番茄放在一個鋁製的碗裡。她把番茄放在水龍頭下沖洗，把它們洗乾淨。她這麼做是為了去除任何灰塵或化學物質。沖洗過後，她會把多餘的水瀝到水槽裡。現在這些番茄已經可以拿來吃或烹煮了。
這些孩子吃著小番茄，看起來非常開心。番茄被裝在一個小小的裝飾性淺盤上。番茄可以像蘋果一樣單獨吃，也可以和其他水果、蔬菜一起做成沙拉。番茄對健康非常有益，含有許多不同的維生素。
番茄長在藤蔓上，成熟之前是綠色的。在成熟的過程中，它們會慢慢變紅，也會像其他水果一樣變得更軟、更甜。在「藤蔓上」自然成熟的番茄，被認為是最好吃的。但有時候番茄必須在還是綠色的時候就採收，這樣才能運送到遙遠的商店。非常熟的番茄會變成深紅色，變得軟爛又難以切開。
想讓番茄更快成熟，可以試著把它放在香蕉旁邊！香蕉在成熟的過程中會釋放出一種化學物質，能幫助番茄更快成熟。這聽起來可能很奇怪，但這是一項科學事實。

options: In an aluminum bowl
In a salad bowl
On a small decorative platter
On a vine

quiz.q: Why are tomatoes sometimes picked while they are still green?
options: Because green tomatoes taste sweeter
So they can be shipped to stores far away
Because they are easier to rinse
So children will enjoy eating them more

quiz.q: According to the passage, what happens to a banana as it ripens that can help a tomato ripen too?
options: It releases a chemical that speeds up ripening
It turns the tomato a darker red immediately
It becomes mushy and soft like the tomato
It absorbs excess water from the tomato

9.title: Fire Safety
paras: The local fire department came to our school last week. They were promoting fire safety. Besides putting up posters about fire prevention, they also gave demonstrations on what to do in the case of a fire. We practiced our fire drill for them, so that they could see we knew how to do it. Then they showed us how to put out a fire.

They taught us how to use a big fire hose, too. It was really cool. The hose could shoot water really far and could even hurt someone. The fireman helped me to use it because I am not strong enough to hold it by myself. Other students lined up behind me and waited for their turn. Our teacher was there, too, so that we didn't goof off in front of our guests.

Next, the firemen, who were dressed in red, took us over by the fire truck where a game was set up for us. Several fire extinguishers filled with water were set up in a row. A couple meters away, there were see-saw like things for us to spray the extinguishers at. The idea was to spray the water into the container on the see-saw until it weighed enough to raise the ball on the other end. Whoever did it first was the winner. I didn't win, but it was still loads of fun.
questions: Why did the fire department come to the school?
Why did a fireman help the student use the hose?
What was the goal of the extinguisher game?

vocab.ex: The teacher gave a talk on fire safety so students would know what to do in an emergency.
vocab.exzh: 老師講解了消防安全，讓學生知道緊急時該怎麼做。

vocab.ex: We called the fire department as soon as we saw smoke coming from the window.
vocab.exzh: 我們一看到窗戶冒煙就馬上打電話給消防隊。

vocab.ex: The posters on the wall taught us simple rules of fire prevention.
vocab.exzh: 牆上的海報教我們一些簡單的防火規則。

vocab.ex: The fireman gave a demonstration on how to use a fire extinguisher.
vocab.exzh: 消防員示範了如何使用滅火器。

vocab.ex: Every student walked calmly outside during the fire drill.
vocab.exzh: 在消防演練期間，每位學生都冷靜地走到外面。

vocab.ex: It took two people to hold the heavy fire hose steady.
vocab.exzh: 需要兩個人才能把沉重的消防水帶握穩。

vocab.ex: There is a fire extinguisher hanging on the wall of every classroom.
vocab.exzh: 每間教室的牆上都掛著一個滅火器。

vocab.ex: The students tried not to goof off while the guests were watching.
vocab.exzh: 客人在看的時候，學生們盡量不胡鬧。

vocab.ex: The visitors came to promote fire safety among young students.
vocab.exzh: 訪客來向年輕學生宣導消防安全。

vocab.ex: The firemen showed us the fastest way to put out a small fire.
vocab.exzh: 消防員教我們最快撲滅小火的方法。

vocab.ex: All the students had to line up to take a turn with the hose.
vocab.exzh: 所有學生都得排隊輪流拿水帶。

vocab.ex: The powerful hose could shoot water more than ten meters.
vocab.exzh: 這條強力水帶能把水噴射超過十公尺遠。

vocab.ex: The fire extinguishers were placed in a row for the game.
vocab.exzh: 滅火器排成一排，準備用於遊戲。

vocab.ex: We had to spray water into the container until it was heavy enough.
vocab.exzh: 我們必須把水噴進容器裡，直到它夠重為止。

vocab.ex: Even though I lost the game, I still had loads of fun.
vocab.exzh: 雖然我輸了比賽，但我還是玩得很開心。

9.translation: 上週當地消防隊來到我們學校。他們正在宣導消防安全。除了張貼防火海報，他們也示範遇到火災時該怎麼做。我們為他們演練了消防疏散，讓他們看看我們知道怎麼做。接著他們教我們如何滅火。他們也教我們如何使用大型消防水帶，真的很酷。水帶可以把水噴得很遠，甚至可能傷到人。消防員幫我操作，因為我力氣不夠，無法自己拿住。其他學生排在我後面等著輪流。我們老師也在旁邊，確保我們不會在客人面前胡鬧。接著，穿著紅衣的消防員帶我們到消防車旁，那裡為我們設置了一個遊戲。好幾個裝水的滅火器排成一列，幾公尺外有像蹺蹺板一樣的裝置讓我們噴。遊戲的目標是把水噴進蹺蹺板上的容器，直到重量足以讓另一端的球升起。誰先做到誰就是贏家。我沒有贏，但仍然非常好玩。
answers: They came to promote fire safety.
The student was not strong enough to hold it alone.
The goal was to spray water into a container until the ball rose.
paras_zh: 上星期，當地的消防隊來到我們學校，宣導消防安全。除了張貼防火海報之外，他們還示範了發生火災時該怎麼辦。我們為他們演練了一次消防演習，讓他們看看我們是不是都學會了。接著，他們教我們如何撲滅火災。
他們也教我們如何使用大型消防水帶，真的很酷。水帶可以把水噴得很遠，甚至可能傷到人。因為我力氣不夠大，沒辦法自己拿穩水帶，所以消防員幫我一起拿。其他同學排在我後面，等著輪到自己。我們的老師也在場，好讓我們在客人面前不會胡鬧。
接著，穿著紅色制服的消防員帶我們到消防車旁邊，那裡為我們設置了一個遊戲。好幾個裝滿水的滅火器排成一排。幾公尺外，有像蹺蹺板一樣的裝置讓我們對著噴水。遊戲規則是要把水噴進蹺蹺板上的容器裡，直到它重到能把另一端的球舉起來為止。誰先做到誰就贏。我雖然沒有贏，但還是玩得非常開心。

9.title: Hand Warmers
paras: "My hands are so cold," Jason said to himself. "They feel like ice."
He rubbed his hands together trying to keep them warm, but it only worked for a short while before they became cold again.
"Maybe I'll try one of those new heating packets that mom bought for the family," he said as if talking to someone in the empty room.
He searched the room intently for some time. Then inside a drawer in the cupboard he found what he was looking for: the little hand-warmers his mother bought during the last cold spell.

He brought them all to the table and set them down. Then he took one out of the wrapper and rubbed it between his hands. It began to get warmer and warmer. When it was nice and toasty, he put it in his pocket.
"Ah! That feels much better," he remarked, still talking to himself like a crazy man.

Next he tried a strange looking one that was reusable. He pressed the metal strip inside the sealed rubber-like bag. The gel inside magically turned into crystals that created heat. He placed that one in the other pocket.
"Wow! That is neat," he said in amazement as he wondered how it worked.

Now that he had a heater in both pockets he was ready to go outside for a walk. If his hands got cold, he could simply slip them into his pockets to warm up. What a smart boy, even if he does talk to himself!
questions: What was Jason looking for in the room?
How did the reusable hand warmer create heat?
Why was Jason ready to go outside?

vocab.ex: Jason put a hand warmer in his pocket before going outside.
vocab.exzh: 傑森出門前把一個暖暖包放進口袋裡。

vocab.ex: Mom bought a box of heating packet for the cold winter days.
vocab.exzh: 媽媽為了寒冷的冬日買了一盒發熱包。

vocab.ex: The whole city shivered during the sudden cold spell.
vocab.exzh: 整座城市在突如其來的寒流中瑟瑟發抖。

vocab.ex: He tore open the wrapper to get the hand warmer inside.
vocab.exzh: 他撕開包裝紙拿出裡面的暖暖包。

vocab.ex: The blanket felt nice and toasty on a cold night.
vocab.exzh: 在寒冷的夜裡，那條毯子感覺暖烘烘的很舒服。

vocab.ex: She prefers a reusable hand warmer over a disposable one.
vocab.exzh: 她比較喜歡可重複使用的暖暖包，而不是用完就丟的那種。

vocab.ex: The clear liquid slowly turned into a solid crystal.
vocab.exzh: 那清澈的液體慢慢變成了固態的晶體。

vocab.ex: The children stared in amazement as the packet grew warm.
vocab.exzh: 孩子們驚訝地盯著那個包裝逐漸變暖。

vocab.ex: He began to rub his hands together to stay warm.
vocab.exzh: 他開始搓手取暖。

vocab.ex: She searched the drawer intently, looking for her keys.
vocab.exzh: 她專注地在抽屜裡翻找鑰匙。

vocab.ex: The extra blankets were kept in the cupboard by the door.

vocab.ex: Make sure to seal the bag tightly so no air gets in.

vocab.ex: The clear gel inside the packet changes when you press the metal strip.
vocab.exzh: 包裝裡的透明凝膠在你按壓金屬片時會發生變化。

vocab.ex: The liquid magically turned solid within seconds.
vocab.exzh: 那液體在幾秒內神奇地變成了固體。

vocab.ex: She likes to slip into her pockets when her hands get cold.

9.translation: 「我的手好冷，」傑森自言自語地說。「感覺像冰一樣。」他搓著雙手想讓它們暖和，但只維持了一小段時間，手又冷了起來。「也許我可以試試媽媽買給家人的新暖暖包，」他像在對空房間裡的某個人說話。他仔細在房間裡找了一段時間。然後他在櫥櫃的一個抽屜裡找到了他要的東西：媽媽在上次寒流時買的小暖暖包。他把它們都拿到桌上放好。接著他拆開一個包裝，在雙手之間搓揉。它開始越來越暖。當它變得溫暖舒服時，他把它放進口袋。「啊！感覺好多了，」他說，仍像個怪人一樣自言自語。接著他試了一個看起來很奇怪、可以重複使用的暖暖包。他按下密封橡膠袋裡的金屬片，裡面的凝膠神奇地變成晶體並產生熱。他把那個放進另一個口袋。「哇！真有趣，」他驚訝地說，一邊想著它是怎麼運作的。現在兩個口袋裡都有暖爐，他準備好出去散步了。如果手冷了，只要伸進口袋就能暖起來。真是個聰明的男孩，即使他會自言自語。
answers: He was looking for hand warmers.
He pressed the metal strip and the gel turned into crystals.
He had a warmer in each pocket.
paras_zh: 「我的手好冷，」傑森自言自語道。「感覺像冰一樣。」
他搓著雙手想保持溫暖，但這只管用一下子，沒多久手又變冷了。
「也許我該試試媽媽買給全家用的那種新發熱包，」他說著，彷彿是在對空盪盪房間裡的某個人說話。
他仔細地在房間裡找了好一陣子。接著在櫥櫃的一個抽屜裡，他找到了要找的東西：媽媽在上次寒流時買的那些小暖暖包。
他把所有的暖暖包都拿到桌上放好。然後他從包裝紙裡拿出一個，在雙手間搓揉。它漸漸變得越來越暖。等到暖烘烘的時候，他就把它放進口袋裡。
「啊！這樣感覺好多了，」他說道，還是像個怪人一樣自言自語。
接著他試了一個造型奇特、可重複使用的暖暖包。他按壓密封的橡膠狀袋子裡的金屬片。裡面的凝膠神奇地變成了晶體，並產生了熱量。他把這個放進另一個口袋。
「哇！這真酷，」他驚訝地說道，心裡納悶著這東西是怎麼運作的。
現在兩個口袋裡都有了暖爐，他準備好要出門散步了。如果他的手變冷，只要把手伸進口袋就能取暖。真是個聰明的孩子，就算他會自言自語！

9.title: Wear a Helmet!
paras: It is time to take a ride on the scooter, but first we must think about safety. Whenever you ride a scooter, you need to wear a helmet. Helmets can prevent head injuries in case of an accident or a fall. Without a helmet, the risk of injury due to an accident is greatly increased. Helmets protect you against concussions, skull fractures and lacerations. They are a necessity and not just something to make you look pretty. Some think that helmets make them look stupid or un-cool, when in fact not wearing a helmet shows a lack of intelligence.

This little girl has already climbed up on the scooter. Her mother is now going to take the scooter off the kickstand. It is common for children in Taiwan to ride on the front of the scooter holding onto the handlebars. It is also common for young children to ride without a helmet. Children are not old enough to know what is safe and what isn't. They can only assume that their parents will not put them into dangerous situations, but this isn't always the case. Many parents allow their children to ride helmet-less and put their children at great risk of injury or even death by doing so. Don't wear a helmet because it's the law; wear a helmet because it can save your life.
questions: Why should people wear helmets on scooters?
Where is it common for children in Taiwan to ride on a scooter?
Why does the writer say people should wear helmets?

vocab.ex: Every rider on the scooter should wear a helmet for safety.
vocab.exzh: 每個騎機車的人都應該戴安全帽以確保安全。

vocab.ex: My father rides his scooter to work every morning.
vocab.exzh: 我爸爸每天早上騎機車去上班。

vocab.ex: A head injury can be very serious if left untreated.
vocab.exzh: 頭部受傷若不加以治療可能會非常嚴重。

vocab.ex: The boy suffered a concussion after falling off his bike.
vocab.exzh: 那男孩從腳踏車上摔下來後得了腦震盪。

vocab.ex: Doctors confirmed that the patient had a skull fracture from the crash.
vocab.exzh: 醫生證實病患因車禍而顱骨骨折。

vocab.ex: The nurse cleaned the deep laceration on his arm.
vocab.exzh: 護理師清理了他手臂上很深的割傷。

vocab.ex: She lowered the kickstand before getting off the scooter.
vocab.exzh: 她下機車前先把腳架放下來。

vocab.ex: Riding helmet-less greatly increases the risk of a serious injury.
vocab.exzh: 不戴安全帽騎車會大幅增加嚴重受傷的風險。

vocab.ex: Not wearing a helmet increases the risk of injury during an accident.
vocab.exzh: 不戴安全帽會增加事故中受傷的風險。

vocab.ex: Wearing a seatbelt in a car is a necessity, not a choice.
vocab.exzh: 在車上繫安全帶是必要的，而不是可以選擇的。

vocab.ex: Ignoring safety rules on the road shows a lack of intelligence.
vocab.exzh: 在路上忽視安全規則顯示出缺乏智慧。

vocab.ex: The little girl held tightly onto the handlebars as her mother drove.
vocab.exzh: 小女孩在媽媽騎車時緊緊抓住把手。

vocab.ex: Children often assume that their parents will always keep them safe.
vocab.exzh: 孩子們常常認為父母總是會保護他們的安全。

vocab.ex: Riding without a helmet can put a child at risk of serious injury.
vocab.exzh: 不戴安全帽騎車會讓孩子處於嚴重受傷的風險之中。

vocab.ex: Wearing a helmet every time you ride can save your life.
vocab.exzh: 每次騎車都戴安全帽可以救你一命。

9.translation: 現在要騎機車了，但首先必須想到安全。每當你騎機車時，都需要戴安全帽。安全帽可以在意外或摔倒時避免頭部受傷。沒有安全帽時，因事故受傷的風險會大幅增加。安全帽能保護你免於腦震盪、顱骨骨折和割傷。它是必需品，不只是讓你看起來漂亮的東西。有些人覺得安全帽讓他們看起來笨或不酷，但事實上，不戴安全帽才顯示缺乏判斷力。這個小女孩已經爬上機車，她媽媽現在要把機車從腳架上放下來。在台灣，小孩坐在機車前方握著把手很常見；年幼兒童不戴安全帽也很常見。孩子還不夠大，無法知道什麼安全、什麼不安全。他們只能相信父母不會讓他們陷入危險，但事實並不總是如此。許多父母讓孩子不戴安全帽騎車，這樣做使孩子面臨受傷甚至死亡的重大風險。不要因為法律規定才戴安全帽；要因為它能救你的命而戴。
answers: Helmets can prevent head injuries.
They often ride on the front holding the handlebars.
Because a helmet can save your life.
paras_zh: 現在是騎機車兜風的時候了，但我們必須先想想安全問題。無論何時騎機車，你都需要戴安全帽。安全帽可以在發生意外或摔車時預防頭部受傷。如果不戴安全帽，發生意外時受傷的風險會大大增加。安全帽可以保護你免於腦震盪、顱骨骨折和割傷。安全帽是必需品，而不只是用來讓你看起來好看的東西。有些人認為戴安全帽會讓自己看起來很蠢或不酷，但事實上，不戴安全帽才顯示出缺乏智慧。
這個小女孩已經爬上機車了。她的媽媽現在要把機車的腳架收起來。在台灣，孩子坐在機車前座、抓著把手，是很常見的事。年幼的孩子不戴安全帽騎車也很常見。孩子還不夠大，無法判斷什麼是安全的、什麼不是。他們只能假設父母不會讓他們處於危險之中，但事實並非總是如此。許多父母讓孩子不戴安全帽騎車，這樣做讓孩子承受了受傷甚至死亡的極大風險。不要因為法律規定才戴安全帽；要戴安全帽，因為它能救你一命。

quiz.q: According to the passage, what is one effect of NOT wearing a helmet?
options: It makes the scooter ride smoother
It greatly increases the risk of injury in an accident
It helps children balance better
It has no real effect at all

quiz.q: Why do some people choose not to wear a helmet, according to the passage?
options: They believe helmets make them look stupid or un-cool
They think helmets are too expensive
They think helmets are hard to find
They believe helmets are only for adults

quiz.q: What does the passage suggest about children riding on the front of a scooter without a helmet?
options: It is against the law everywhere in Taiwan
It is only dangerous for adults, not children
It is the safest way for children to ride
It puts children at great risk of injury or even death

9.title: Home Time
paras: It's time to go home. The little yellow vans are lined up with their sliding doors open. They are waiting for the kindergarten children to board. This is a daily routine outside all kindergartens.

The vans are very brightly colored. This is to make them more visible to other cars and trucks on the road. If people recognize the vans as school vans, then they are more likely to drive carefully near them because they would know that there are children inside. It’s all about getting the kids home safely.

Supervisors watch out for the children so that they are safe. It is their job to make sure that everyone gets in the right van, too. They also need to make sure no one is “in the way” when the vans take off.

Supervisors also help out with backpacks, jackets and anything else that the small students need help with. Carrying big book bags and other essentials can be difficult for small children and they often forget things. It isn’t a wonder they need someone around to help them out.

The lady next to the first bus is helping young students board the bus safely. She occasionally has to assist really small children to get in as well because some of them are just too small to get in without assistance.
questions: What are the yellow vans waiting for?
Why are the vans brightly colored?
What do supervisors help children with?

vocab.ex: Every morning, the little boy walks to kindergarten with his older sister.
vocab.exzh: 每天早上，這個小男孩都和姐姐一起走路去幼稚園。

vocab.ex: The children lined up quietly before they began to board the van.
vocab.exzh: 孩子們在開始上車前安靜地排隊。

vocab.ex: Brushing her teeth before bed is part of her nightly routine.
vocab.exzh: 睡前刷牙是她每晚例行事項的一部分。

vocab.ex: The bright orange jacket made him clearly visible in the dark.
vocab.exzh: 那件亮橘色的外套讓他在黑暗中清楚可見。

vocab.ex: The supervisor checked that every child had a seat before the bus left.
vocab.exzh: 督導人員在公車出發前確認每個孩子都有座位。

vocab.ex: He carried his heavy backpack all the way to the classroom.
vocab.exzh: 他一路把沉重的背包背到教室。

vocab.ex: She packed only the essentials for the short school trip.
vocab.exzh: 她只為這趟短程校外教學打包了必需品。

vocab.ex: The small child needed some assistance to climb the steps of the van.
vocab.exzh: 這個小孩需要一些協助才能爬上車廂的階梯。

vocab.ex: The vans were lined up outside the gate, waiting for the children.
vocab.exzh: 這些小巴排成一列停在門外，等著孩子們上車。

vocab.ex: The driver opened the sliding door so the students could get on quickly.
vocab.exzh: 司機打開滑門，讓學生可以快速上車。

vocab.ex: The bus will take off as soon as everyone is seated and buckled in.
vocab.exzh: 只要大家都坐好並繫好安全帶，公車就會出發。

vocab.ex: The supervisor asked the parents to move so they would not be in the way of the vans.
vocab.exzh: 督導人員請家長移動，以免擋住這些小巴的去路。

vocab.ex: Drivers can easily recognize the yellow vans from far away.
vocab.exzh: 駕駛人可以從遠處輕易認出這些黃色小巴。

vocab.ex: The supervisor made sure every child arrived home safely.
vocab.exzh: 督導人員確保每個孩子都安全到家。

vocab.ex: The bag was so heavy that it was no wonder the boy needed help carrying it.
vocab.exzh: 這個包包太重了，難怪這個男孩需要人幫忙提。

9.translation: 該回家了。小黃車排成一列，滑門打開，等著幼稚園孩子上車。這是所有幼稚園外每天都會出現的例行景象。這些車顏色非常鮮明，是為了讓路上的其他汽車和卡車更容易看見。如果人們認出這些是娃娃車，就更可能在附近小心駕駛，因為他們知道車裡有孩子。一切都是為了讓孩子安全回家。督導人員照看孩子，確保他們安全。他們的工作也包括確認每個人都上對車，並確定車子出發時沒有人擋在路上。督導人員也會幫忙背包、外套和小學生需要協助的其他物品。對小孩來說，拿大書包和其他必需品可能很困難，而且他們常常忘東忘西。難怪他們需要有人在旁邊幫忙。第一輛車旁邊的女士正在幫年幼學生安全上車。她偶爾也得協助特別小的孩子上車，因為有些孩子太小，沒有幫助就上不去。
answers: They are waiting for kindergarten children to board.
The bright colors make them more visible to other drivers.
They help with safety, boarding, backpacks, jackets, and other needs.
paras_zh: 該回家了。黃色的小巴一輛輛排成一列，滑門敞開著，等著幼稚園的小朋友上車。這是每所幼稚園門外每天都會上演的例行景象。
這些小巴的顏色都非常鮮豔，這是為了讓路上其他汽車和卡車能更容易看見它們。如果人們認出這些是校車，就比較會在它們附近小心駕駛，因為他們知道車上載著孩子。這一切都是為了讓孩子們平安回家。
督導人員會留意孩子，確保他們的安全。確認每個人都上對車也是他們的工作之一。他們還必須留意，確保小巴出發時沒有人擋在路中間。
督導人員也會幫忙拿背包、外套，以及任何這些小學生需要幫忙的東西。對小朋友來說，背著大書包和其他必需品可能不太容易，他們也常常忘東忘西。難怪他們需要有人在旁邊幫忙。
第一輛公車旁的那位女士正協助小朋友安全上車。她有時還得幫忙一些特別矮小的孩子上車，因為有些孩子實在太小了，沒有人幫忙就上不去。

quiz.q: Why are the vans painted in bright colors?
options: To match the school's uniform colors
Because bright colors are cheaper to paint
To make them more visible so other drivers are careful near them
To help children recognize their own backpacks

quiz.q: According to the passage, why do small children often need a supervisor's help with their belongings?
options: They are not allowed to touch their own bags
Carrying big bags is hard for them and they often forget things
The school requires supervisors to carry everything
Their parents ask the supervisors to do it every day

quiz.q: Why does the lady next to the first bus sometimes physically help students get in?
options: Because some children are too small to get in without assistance
Because the bus door is broken
Because the driver told her to
Because it is faster than letting them climb in alone

9.title: A Mother’s Day Celebration
paras: Mother's Day only comes once a year, so it is important to show your appreciation for your mother on this day. You can do this in many ways. You can give her flowers, candy, a card, or just tell her how much you love her. It’s the thought that counts and she’ll love whatever you give her or do for her.

This year my school decided to throw a party for the students’ mothers. It was held in the student activity center at our school. We spent all morning setting up and decorating tables and chairs for the event. When we had finished, the room looked fit for a King—or, in this case, a Queen.

The mothers, as well as a few grandmothers, started showing up at around one o'clock. They sat at tables spread out around the room and chatted while waiting for the festivities to start. After everyone was seated, our class came in.

We marched into the room in a line. Each of us carried a small tray. On each tray were a flower, a hand-made card and a cup of hot tea. The trays were presented to our respective mothers and they were tickled pink to receive our tokens of appreciation.

After the presentation, we sat with our mothers and talked while they drank their tea. My mother thanked me for being so thoughtful, but I told her that no thank you was needed. After all, it was her day.

Once everyone had said goodbye and our guests had left, we cleaned up the activity room. In a way, it was fitting to do the cleaning for a change because it is usually our mothers that do it for us day after day, year after year. The difference being, they rarely, if ever, receive thanks for doing it, while our teachers thoughtfully thanked us for the hard work that we did.
questions: Where was the Mother’s Day party held?
What did each student carry on a tray?
Why was cleaning the activity room fitting?

vocab.ex: She wrote a card to show her appreciation for her mother.
vocab.exzh: 她寫了一張卡片，表達對母親的感謝。

vocab.ex: The party was held in the school activity center.
vocab.exzh: 這場派對是在學校的活動中心舉行的。

vocab.ex: We spent the morning helping to decorate the room for the party.
vocab.exzh: 我們花了一個早上幫忙布置房間，準備派對。

vocab.ex: Everyone waited eagerly for the festivity to begin.
vocab.exzh: 大家都殷切地等待慶祝活動開始。

vocab.ex: Each student carried a small tray with a flower and a card on it.
vocab.exzh: 每個學生都端著一個小托盤，上面放著一朵花和一張卡片。

vocab.ex: The homemade card was a small token of the child's love.
vocab.exzh: 那張手作卡片是孩子表達愛意的小小心意。

vocab.ex: It was very thoughtful of the students to prepare tea for their mothers.
vocab.exzh: 學生們為媽媽們準備茶水，真是十分貼心。

vocab.ex: Mothers rarely receive thanks for the housework they do every day.
vocab.exzh: 媽媽們每天做家事，卻很少得到感謝。

vocab.zh: 極為豪華、堪比國王的排場
vocab.ex: After we finished decorating, the room looked fit for a King.
vocab.exzh: 布置完成後，這個房間看起來豪華得堪比國王的排場。

vocab.ex: The tables were spread out around the room so everyone had space to sit.
vocab.exzh: 桌子分散擺放在房間各處，讓每個人都有地方坐。

vocab.ex: The mothers sat down to chat while waiting for the program to start.
vocab.exzh: 媽媽們坐下來聊天，等待節目開始。

vocab.ex: The students began to march into the room in a straight line.
vocab.exzh: 學生們開始整齊列隊走進房間。

vocab.ex: The mothers were tickled pink to receive gifts from their children.
vocab.exzh: 媽媽們收到孩子的禮物，樂不可支。

vocab.ex: Each tray was carefully carried forward to present it to a mother.
vocab.exzh: 每個托盤都被小心地端上前，呈給一位媽媽。

vocab.ex: It felt fitting for the children to do the cleaning for a change.
vocab.exzh: 這次換孩子們來打掃，感覺格外合適。

9.translation: 母親節一年只有一次，所以在這一天向母親表達感謝很重要。你可以用許多方式做到。你可以送花、糖果、卡片，或只是告訴她你有多愛她。重要的是心意，不管你送什麼或為她做什麼，她都會喜歡。今年我們學校決定為學生的母親舉辦一場派對。活動在學校的學生活動中心舉行。我們花了一整個早上布置桌椅，為活動做準備。完成後，房間看起來非常華麗，適合國王，或者在這個情況下，適合皇后。母親們以及幾位祖母大約一點鐘開始到場。她們坐在分散於房間各處的桌邊聊天，等待慶祝活動開始。大家都就座後，我們班進場。我們排成一列走進房間，每個人都端著一個小托盤。每個托盤上有一朵花、一張手工卡片和一杯熱茶。托盤被呈給各自的母親，她們收到這些感謝的小禮物時都非常開心。呈獻後，我們和母親坐在一起聊天，她們則喝著茶。媽媽感謝我這麼貼心，但我告訴她不需要道謝，畢竟這是屬於她的日子。等大家道別、客人離開後，我們清理活動室。某種程度上，換我們來打掃很合適，因為通常都是母親日復一日、年復一年替我們做這件事。不同的是，她們很少，甚至幾乎從未因此得到感謝，而我們的老師卻貼心地感謝我們的辛苦。
answers: It was held in the student activity center.
Each tray had a flower, a handmade card, and a cup of hot tea.
It was fitting because mothers usually do cleaning for their children.
paras_zh: 母親節一年只有一次，所以在這一天向媽媽表達感謝格外重要。你可以用很多方式做到這一點：可以送她花、糖果、卡片，或只是告訴她你有多愛她。重要的是心意，不論你送什麼、為她做什麼，她都會喜歡。
今年我們學校決定為學生的媽媽們舉辦一場派對，地點就在學校的學生活動中心。我們花了一整個早上布置桌椅、準備會場。等我們忙完之後，整個房間看起來豪華得堪比國王的排場——不過這次是為了「皇后」而準備的。
媽媽們，還有幾位奶奶、外婆，大約在下午一點開始陸續抵達。她們分散坐在會場各處的桌子旁，一邊聊天一邊等待活動開始。等大家都坐定後，我們班就進場了。
我們排成一列走進會場，每個人都端著一個小托盤，上面放著一朵花、一張手作卡片和一杯熱茶。我們把托盤呈給各自的媽媽，她們收到我們這份小小的心意，都樂不可支。
獻上禮物之後，我們就坐在媽媽身邊，陪她們一邊喝茶一邊聊天。媽媽謝謝我這麼貼心，但我告訴她，其實不需要道謝，畢竟這是屬於她的節日。
等大家互道再見、客人都離開後，我們便開始整理活動室。換我們來打掃，感覺特別合適，因為平常都是媽媽們日復一日、年復一年地為我們做這些事，而且她們幾乎從未因此得到感謝；相較之下，老師們卻很貼心地為我們的辛勞道謝。

quiz.q: Why did the room look, in the writer's words, fit for a King (or Queen)?
options: Because the school hired a professional decorator
Because the mothers decorated it themselves before arriving
Because it was originally built for royal ceremonies
Because the students spent the morning setting up and decorating it

quiz.q: What was the narrator's response when her mother thanked her for being thoughtful?
options: She said her teacher deserved the thanks instead
She asked her mother to thank the whole class
She said no thanks was needed since it was her mother's day
She said she would do it again next year

quiz.q: According to the last paragraph, what is different between the mothers' usual work and the students' cleaning that day?
options: The students cleaned faster than their mothers usually do
The mothers rarely get thanked for their daily work, but the students were thanked by their teachers
The mothers' work is harder and takes longer
The students had to clean without any help, unlike their mothers

9.title: Making Papaya Milk
paras: Two boys are trying to make papaya milk. It is a good drink for growing children because it is sweetened naturally and contains a lot of vitamins. In addition, milk is high in calcium which is great for strong bones and teeth. But good for them or not, the boys like to drink it because it tastes great.
Picture 1 Tom is slowly pouring milk into a blender. His friend John carefully spoons in some papaya that has already been cut into small pieces.

Picture 2 John pushes the start button to blend the papaya and milk together. The blender makes a horribly loud noise as the blades rotate very quickly, pulverizing everything inside. It only takes a few seconds before it is finished, and he then hits the stop button.

Picture 3 John is trying to pour the thick sludge into the tiny paper cup without making too much of a mess. Tom is holding the cup steady, so that it doesn't tip over. The little girls next to them can't wait to taste the yummy drink.

In the end, the boys wound up with a goopy sludge. They used too much papaya and not enough milk. But for their first attempt, they did a pretty good job. Even if it is too thick to drink, they can still eat it with a spoon. Maybe next time they’ll remember to use more milk.
questions: Why is papaya milk good for growing children?
What did John do in Picture 2?
Why did the drink become too thick?

vocab.ex: Tom and John decided to make fresh papaya milk for the little girls.
vocab.exzh: 湯姆和約翰決定為小女孩們做新鮮的木瓜牛奶。

vocab.ex: The fruit drink is naturally sweet, so there is no need to add sugar.
vocab.exzh: 這種水果飲料本身自然帶有甜味，所以不需要額外加糖。

vocab.ex: Milk is a great source of calcium, which helps build strong bones.
vocab.exzh: 牛奶是鈣質的良好來源，有助於強化骨骼。

vocab.ex: They poured the milk and papaya into the blender together.
vocab.exzh: 他們把牛奶和木瓜一起倒進果汁機裡。

vocab.ex: The sharp blade inside the blender spins very fast to chop the fruit.
vocab.exzh: 果汁機裡的鋒利刀片會快速旋轉，把水果切碎。

vocab.ex: The blender can pulverize chunks of papaya in just a few seconds.
vocab.exzh: 果汁機只要幾秒鐘就能把木瓜塊粉碎。

vocab.ex: Because they added too much papaya, the drink turned into a thick sludge.
vocab.exzh: 因為木瓜加太多，這杯飲料變成了濃稠的糊狀物。

vocab.ex: For their first attempt at making papaya milk, the boys did a good job.
vocab.exzh: 作為第一次嘗試做木瓜牛奶，這兩個男孩做得還不錯。

vocab.ex: This drink is sweetened naturally by the ripe papaya, not by sugar.
vocab.exzh: 這杯飲料是靠成熟的木瓜自然增添甜味，而不是靠糖。

vocab.ex: Tom began to pour the milk slowly into the blender.
vocab.exzh: 湯姆開始把牛奶慢慢倒進果汁機裡。

vocab.ex: The blades rotate very quickly once the start button is pressed.
vocab.exzh: 一按下啟動鈕，刀片就會快速旋轉。

vocab.ex: Tom held the cup steady so the thick drink would not spill.
vocab.exzh: 湯姆把杯子握穩，這樣濃稠的飲料才不會灑出來。

vocab.ex: John poured carefully so that he would not make a mess on the table.
vocab.exzh: 約翰小心地倒，才不會把桌子弄得一團亂。

vocab.ex: The little girls can't wait to taste the papaya milk.
vocab.exzh: 小女孩們迫不及待想要嚐嚐木瓜牛奶。

vocab.ex: Because they used too much papaya, the boys wound up with a thick sludge instead of a drink.
vocab.exzh: 因為木瓜放太多，兩個男孩最後得到的是濃稠糊狀物，而不是飲料。

9.translation: 兩個男孩正在嘗試做木瓜牛奶。這對成長中的孩子來說是很好的飲料，因為它自然帶甜味，且含有很多維生素。此外，牛奶富含鈣質，對強壯骨骼和牙齒很有幫助。不管對身體好不好，男孩們喜歡喝它，因為味道很好。圖一中，湯姆正慢慢把牛奶倒進果汁機。他的朋友約翰小心地把已切成小塊的木瓜舀進去。圖二中，約翰按下啟動鈕，把木瓜和牛奶混合。果汁機發出可怕的巨大聲響，刀片快速旋轉，把裡面的東西全部打碎。幾秒鐘後就完成了，他接著按下停止鈕。圖三中，約翰試著把濃稠的糊狀飲料倒進小紙杯裡，盡量不要弄得一團糟。湯姆扶穩杯子，避免它翻倒。旁邊的小女孩們迫不及待想嚐這杯美味飲料。最後，男孩們做出了一杯黏稠的糊狀物。他們用了太多木瓜、牛奶不夠。但以第一次嘗試來說，他們做得相當不錯。即使太濃不能喝，他們還是可以用湯匙吃。也許下次他們會記得多加一點牛奶。
answers: It has vitamins and milk contains calcium.
He pushed the start button to blend the papaya and milk.
They used too much papaya and not enough milk.
paras_zh: 兩個男孩正試著做木瓜牛奶。這對正在成長的孩子來說是很好的飲料，因為它天然帶有甜味，還含有大量維生素。此外，牛奶富含鈣質，對強化骨骼和牙齒非常有幫助。但不管對身體好不好，男孩們喜歡喝它，是因為它真的很好喝。
圖片1：湯姆正慢慢地把牛奶倒進果汁機裡。他的朋友約翰小心翼翼地用湯匙舀入已經切成小塊的木瓜。
圖片2：約翰按下啟動鈕，把木瓜和牛奶攪打在一起。果汁機發出可怕的巨大聲響，因為刀片快速旋轉，把裡面的東西全部打碎。只需要幾秒鐘就完成了，接著他按下停止鈕。
圖片3：約翰正試著把濃稠的糊狀物倒進小紙杯裡，盡量不要弄得一團亂。湯姆把杯子扶穩，好讓它不會翻倒。旁邊的小女孩們已經迫不及待想嚐嚐這美味的飲料。
最後，兩個男孩得到的是一團濃稠的糊狀物。他們木瓜放太多，牛奶放太少。不過對他們第一次嘗試來說，已經做得相當不錯了。就算它濃稠得沒辦法用喝的，他們還是可以用湯匙來吃。也許下次他們會記得要多加一點牛奶。

quiz.q: What is John doing while Tom pours the milk into the blender?
options: He is spooning cut papaya into the blender
He is washing the papaya
He is drinking the milk
He is cleaning the cups

quiz.q: Why does the blender make a horribly loud noise?
options: It is broken
The boys are shouting
The cup is too small
The blades are rotating very quickly and pulverizing everything inside

quiz.q: What can the boys still do with the drink even though it is too thick to drink?
options: Throw it away
Freeze it into ice cream
Eat it with a spoon
Pour it back into the blender

9.title: Soccer Practice
paras: Twice a week the girls' soccer team meets with their coach to practice. Rain or shine they are required to show up and at least check in. If the field is too wet, they might be given a break but otherwise, they have to be there.

Their coach is a very kind, yet strict man. He teaches them many of the skills they will need to become good soccer players. He instructs them on the rules of the game and how to work together as a team. As well, he teaches them something many coaches ignore…sportsmanship.

Practicing their footwork is an important part of every practice. Often, he has the team stand around as he instructs them on the fundamentals of controlling the ball. He also teaches them such things as how to kick and pass the ball properly. He shows them that by holding out their arms, it will help to them maintain balance when they strike the ball.

One of the drills that the coach likes to use is to set up a ball in front of the goal and let each of the girls take a turn at kicking it into the net. As they run the drill, he will stand by to watch their form. By doing this he is able to give each girl a few pointers that will help her to hone her skills. He is also there to offer encouragement and praise when needed.

While the drill is being run, students wait their turn behind the net and watch their fellow teammates. They can learn a lot by watching others who are better than them and also from others' mistakes.

With diligent practice and repeatedly doing simple drills, they are able to master basic soccer skills. The more time they spend kicking and passing the ball, the more power and accuracy they will develop. With enough practice, handling the ball will become like second-nature and make them much better players. They will be able to dribble past the opposition as if they weren’t even there. Or, as I like to say, turn the opponent into a human pylon.
questions: How often does the girls’ soccer team practice?
What important value does the coach teach besides skills?
How can students learn while waiting behind the net?

vocab.ex: The girls never skip soccer practice, even when it rains.
vocab.exzh: 即使下雨，女孩們也從不缺席足球練習。

vocab.ex: Their coach arrives early to set up the cones before every session.
vocab.exzh: 他們的教練每次練習前都會提早到場擺放標誌錐。

vocab.ex: He is strict about attendance, but the players respect him for it.
vocab.exzh: 他對出席要求很嚴格，但球員們也因此敬重他。

vocab.ex: Shaking hands with the other team after a loss shows good sportsmanship.
vocab.exzh: 輸球後還與對方球隊握手，展現了良好的運動家精神。

vocab.ex: Good footwork helps a player control the ball under pressure.
vocab.exzh: 良好的腳法能幫助球員在壓力下控制好球。

vocab.ex: The passing drill lasted almost twenty minutes.
vocab.exzh: 這項傳球訓練持續了將近二十分鐘。

vocab.ex: She practices every day to hone her shooting skills.
vocab.exzh: 她每天練習，磨練自己的射門技巧。

vocab.ex: Repeated passing drills improve a player’s accuracy.
vocab.exzh: 反覆的傳球訓練能提升球員的準確度。

vocab.ex: All players must check in with the coach before practice begins.
vocab.exzh: 所有球員在練習開始前都必須向教練報到。

vocab.ex: The coach spends the first ten minutes reviewing the fundamentals of ball control.
vocab.exzh: 教練會花前十分鐘複習控球的基本功。

vocab.ex: Holding out your arms helps you maintain balance while kicking the ball.
vocab.exzh: 把手臂張開有助於踢球時保持平衡。

vocab.ex: Each girl will take a turn kicking the ball into the net.
vocab.exzh: 每個女孩都會輪流把球踢進球網。

vocab.ex: The coach offers encouragement to every player, even after a mistake.
vocab.exzh: 即使球員犯了錯，教練也會給予鼓勵。

vocab.ex: With diligent practice, the team improved quickly this season.
vocab.exzh: 憑著勤勉的練習，這支隊伍這一季進步得很快。

vocab.ex: After months of drills, controlling the ball became second nature to her.
vocab.exzh: 經過幾個月的訓練，控球對她來說已經變得習以為常。

9.translation: 每週兩次，女子足球隊會和教練集合練習。不論晴雨，她們都必須到場，至少要報到。如果場地太濕，她們可能會被放一天假；否則就必須在場。她們的教練非常親切，但也很嚴格。他教她們許多成為好足球員所需的技巧，也指導她們比賽規則和如何團隊合作。此外，他還教她們許多教練會忽略的東西：運動家精神。練習腳法是每次訓練的重要部分。教練常讓隊員站在一旁，講解控球的基本原理。他也教她們如何正確踢球和傳球。他示範伸出雙臂能在踢球時幫助保持平衡。教練喜歡用的一項訓練，是把球放在球門前，讓每個女孩輪流把球踢進網裡。進行訓練時，他會站在旁邊觀察她們的姿勢。這樣他就能給每個女孩一些建議，幫助她精進技巧。需要時，他也會給予鼓勵和稱讚。訓練進行時，學生在球網後方等待輪到自己，並觀看隊友。她們可以從比自己更好的人身上學到很多，也能從別人的錯誤中學習。透過勤奮練習和反覆做簡單訓練，她們能掌握基本足球技巧。花越多時間踢球和傳球，就越能培養力量與準確度。練習足夠後，控球會變得像第二天性，使她們成為更好的球員。她們將能運球越過對手，彷彿對手不存在。或者用我喜歡的說法，把對手變成人形路標。
answers: They practice twice a week.
He teaches sportsmanship.
They can learn by watching better players and others’ mistakes.
paras_zh: 女子足球隊每週和教練練習兩次。無論晴雨，她們都必須到場，至少要去報到。如果場地太濕，她們或許可以休息，但除此之外，她們都得出席。
她們的教練是一位既親切又嚴格的人。他教導她們許多成為優秀足球員所需的技巧，指導她們比賽規則以及如何團隊合作。此外，他也教導許多教練會忽略的一件事——運動家精神。
練習腳法是每次訓練中重要的一環。他常讓隊員們圍站一旁，指導她們控球的基本功。他也教她們如何正確地踢球和傳球等技巧。他向她們示範，張開雙臂有助於在踢球時保持平衡。
教練喜歡使用的訓練之一，是在球門前擺一顆球，讓每個女孩輪流嘗試將球踢進球網。在進行這項訓練時，他會站在一旁觀察她們的動作。透過這樣的方式，他能給每個女孩一些提點，幫助她們磨練技巧。他也在一旁適時給予鼓勵與讚美。
在進行這項訓練時，其他學生會在球門後排隊等候，同時觀看隊友練習。她們可以從觀察比自己厲害的隊友身上學到很多，也能從別人的錯誤中學習。
透過勤勉的練習，並反覆進行簡單的訓練，她們得以精通基本的足球技巧。花越多時間練習踢球和傳球，她們的力道與準確度就會越好。練習得夠多之後，控球就會變得像本能一樣自然，讓她們成為更出色的球員。她們將能夠靈活運球繞過對手，彷彿對方根本不存在。或者，就像我喜歡說的那樣，把對手變成一根人肉柱子。

quiz.q: What happens if the field is too wet for practice?
options: The team practices indoors instead
The girls might be given a break
The coach cancels the season
They practice twice as long the next day

quiz.q: Why does the coach have the girls hold out their arms while kicking?
options: To help them maintain balance
To signal to their teammates
To block the opposing team
To make the drill more difficult

quiz.q: What does the author mean by turning an opponent into a 'human pylon'?
options: The opponent becomes injured and cannot play
The opponent is used to mark the practice field
The opponent joins the team as a coach
The opponent is easily dribbled past, as if standing still like an obstacle

10.title: Bumper Cars
paras: Hi, my name is Jefferson. Yesterday, I went to the carnival with my brother, Billy Joe. There were lots of people there and all the rides had long lineups to get on them. We waited for almost thirty minutes just to get on the Ferris wheel. We even had to line up just to get something to eat or drink.

Our favorite ride was the bumper cars. It was a great place to practice our ‘Taiwanese’ driving skills. The cars are surrounded by big rubber bumpers, so that they can hit each other without damaging anything. People raced around the floor madly trying to ram each other. It was so much fun I couldn't stop smiling.

One time, I rear-ended Billy. He wasn't expecting it and let out a big scream. It was so funny that I almost peed myself laughing. He spent the rest of the time angrily chasing me and trying to crash into my car, but I was too fast. It was hilarious.
I hope the carnival comes back to town soon. I can hardly wait to ride the bumper cars again. It was a blast. Billy can't wait, either. He wants revenge.
questions: Where did Jefferson and Billy Joe go?
Why can bumper cars hit each other safely?
Why does Billy want the carnival to come back?
10.translation: 嗨，我叫傑佛森。昨天我和哥哥比利喬去了嘉年華。那裡有很多人，所有遊樂設施都排了長長的隊。我們光是為了坐摩天輪就等了將近三十分鐘，甚至連買吃的或喝的也要排隊。我們最喜歡的遊樂設施是碰碰車。那是練習我們「台灣式」駕駛技巧的好地方。車子四周有大型橡膠保險桿，所以可以互相撞擊而不會造成損壞。大家在場地上瘋狂地開來開去，想撞上彼此。實在太好玩了，我笑得停不下來。有一次，我從後面撞上比利。他沒料到，發出一聲尖叫。那太好笑了，我幾乎笑到尿出來。剩下的時間他都生氣地追著我，想撞我的車，但我太快了。真是好笑極了。我希望嘉年華很快再回到鎮上。我迫不及待想再坐碰碰車。那真是太過癮了。比利也等不及了，因為他想報仇。
answers: They went to the carnival.
They have big rubber bumpers around them.
He wants revenge after Jefferson rear-ended him.

10.title: First Day of School
paras: It is the first day of class. All the new first graders are excited. They sit quietly in their desks listening to their new teacher, Wendy. It is a big day for them.

The students' parents are also excited and stand at the door curiously looking on. Their little babies are growing up and now need to attend class every day. Some of the moms want to make sure that everything will be all right before they leave their kids alone.

Wendy is a bit nervous being watched as she teaches. But most parents are just watching their children and aren’t too concerned about her. Besides, she knows they will only stick around for a couple of periods anyway and then they will leave.

The new students don't yet know how to read Chinese characters. To help them out, Wendy has written the phonetic symbols on the chalkboard beside each character. They must first learn this phonetic system, called Be Pe Me Fe, to be able to pronounce words. When they have mastered this, they can then begin to memorize the Chinese characters. It will take them many years to learn to read and write Chinese, but, with Wendy's help, they will learn some very important lessons in the first grade.
questions: Why are the first graders excited?
Why is Wendy nervous?
What does Be Pe Me Fe help students do?
10.translation: 這是上課第一天。所有新來的一年級學生都很興奮。他們安靜地坐在座位上，聽新老師溫蒂說話。這對他們來說是重要的一天。學生的父母也很興奮，站在門口好奇地看著。他們的小寶貝長大了，現在每天都需要上課。有些媽媽想在離開孩子前確認一切都會沒問題。溫蒂被人看著教學，覺得有點緊張。不過大多數父母只是看著自己的孩子，並不太在意她。再說，她知道他們反正只會待幾節課，然後就會離開。新學生還不會讀中文字。為了幫助他們，溫蒂在黑板上每個字旁寫上注音符號。他們必須先學會這套稱為ㄅㄆㄇㄈ的注音系統，才能發音。掌握之後，他們才能開始記中文字。學會讀寫中文會花他們許多年，但在溫蒂的幫助下，他們會在一年級學到一些非常重要的課程。
answers: It is their first day of class.
Parents are watching her teach.
It helps them pronounce Chinese characters.

10.title: Paper Cutter
paras: Paper cutters are very handy to make precise, clean cuts on paper or thin cardboard. They are very powerful cutters and can cut many sheets at a time. The back edge of the cutter has a ruler attached. This helps to line up the paper, making it square, and also for more accurate measurements. The rest of the board is covered with a grid, which also aids in alignment. The cutter is a long steel blade with a handle on the end.

To use the paper cutter, lift up the cutter blade and insert some paper. Push the paper snuggly against the ruler on the back to keep it square to the blade. Use your left hand to hold the paper in place and then slowly lower the blade. Be sure to keep your fingers away from the cutting edge, as this device could easily cut one right off.
When you are finished, make sure that you store the paper cutter with the blade down. This will prevent any unfortunate accidents. Also, make sure that it is out of the reach of curious little hands.
questions: Why are paper cutters useful?
What helps line up the paper?
How should the paper cutter be stored?
10.translation: 裁紙機很方便，可以在紙張或薄紙板上做出精準、乾淨的切割。它們是很有力的切割工具，一次可以切很多張紙。裁紙機後緣附有尺，這有助於把紙對齊，使它保持方正，也能提供更準確的測量。板面其餘部分覆蓋著格線，也有助於對齊。切刀是一片長長的鋼刀，末端有把手。使用裁紙機時，先把刀片抬起並放入紙張。把紙緊貼後方尺邊，讓它與刀片保持方正。用左手固定紙張，再慢慢放下刀片。務必讓手指遠離切割邊，因為這個工具很容易把手指切掉。使用完畢後，務必把刀片放下來收納，這可以避免不幸意外。此外，也要確保它放在好奇的小手碰不到的地方。
answers: They make precise, clean cuts on paper or thin cardboard.
The ruler and grid help line up the paper.
It should be stored with the blade down and away from children.

10.title: Exit
paras: Exit signs point the way to the outside door. They are required by law in most public buildings such as schools and apartment complexes, but even where it isn’t mandatory to have them they are still a good idea to have around.

Their primary function is safety. They help occupants of buildings to quickly locate the nearest door in the case of an emergency. The signs should have bold writing on them or some type of symbol to make them more visible as well as an arrow to point the way to safety.

Exit lights are best because power failures often accompany disasters like fires and earthquakes when you need to escape quickly. They can easily be read in the dark because the lights are connected to an emergency power source to keep them lit.

Although safety is their main job, they also help you out of large buildings that you may not be familiar with. Big shopping malls and department stores are good examples because they are often difficult to find your way out of. This is not by coincidence either, but by design to keep customers inside longer. However, much to their dismay, it is mandatory that they have exit signs. So look for the signs when you are finished shopping before you are tempted into spending more money at the till than you had initially anticipated.
questions: What is the primary function of exit signs?
Why are exit lights useful during disasters?
Why might shopping malls be hard to leave?
10.translation: 出口標誌指引通往外門的方向。在學校、公寓大樓等多數公共建築中，法律都要求設置出口標誌；即使某些地方不是強制規定，設置它們仍是個好主意。它們的主要功能是安全。遇到緊急情況時，它們幫助建築物內的人快速找到最近的門。標誌上應有醒目的文字，或某種符號讓它更容易看見，也應有箭頭指出通往安全的方向。出口燈最好，因為火災和地震等災害常伴隨停電，而那正是你需要快速逃生的時候。它們連接緊急電源，所以在黑暗中也能清楚讀取。雖然安全是它們的主要工作，但它們也能幫你走出不熟悉的大型建築。大型購物中心和百貨公司就是好例子，因為它們常常很難找到出口。這也不是巧合，而是設計使然，目的是讓顧客待得更久。不過令商家失望的是，設置出口標誌是強制的。所以當你逛完街時，在被誘惑到結帳台花掉比原本預期更多錢之前，記得尋找出口標誌。
answers: Their primary function is safety.
They can stay lit during power failures.
They are designed to keep customers inside longer.

10.title: Food for the Health un-conscience
paras: This is the McDonald's located in Huatan. The infamous golden arches mounted on a pole next to the road make it very easy to find. Sitting on a bench outside of the main entrance is a life-sized statue of Ronald McDonald. He is McDonald's mascot. Feel free to sit and have lunch with him, he won't mind a bit.

The cashiers stand behind the counter. Each one of them has their own cash register. Above and behind the front counter is the menu board. It lists meals you can order and individual items, too, along with the prices. When you're ready to order, you can tell one of the clerks what you want or point to it on the menu board. They will then key it into the register.

Once you have paid, they will get your order for you. If you want it to go, then they will put your food in a paper bag. If you plan to eat in, they will put it on a tray with a paper liner. And once they have filled your order, you can take the tray to an empty table and sit down. Make sure to grab a straw, some ketchup and napkins.

Here is a look at someone's McChicken (chicken burger) meal. A meal usually consists of a burger, french-fries and a drink. If you want, you can super-size it, too, but a regular-sized meal contains enough unhealthy calories for one person anyway.

For those in a big hurry, this McDonald's also has a drive-thru window. You can order your food directly from the outside menu board via a microphone and speaker. By the time you get to the drive-thru window, your order should be ready. You give the person wearing a headset and a phony smile some money, they give you some food, and then you are on your way. All this without ever having to get out of your car. Life can’t get any lazier than that.
questions: Where is this McDonald’s located?
What does a meal usually include?
How can people order from the drive-thru?
10.translation: 這是位於花壇的麥當勞。路邊柱子上的知名金色拱門讓它非常容易找到。主入口外的長椅上坐著一尊真人大小的麥當勞叔叔雕像，他是麥當勞的吉祥物。你可以放心坐下來和他一起吃午餐，他一點也不介意。收銀員站在櫃台後方，每個人都有自己的收銀機。櫃台上方後面是菜單板，列出可點的套餐、單點品項以及價格。準備點餐時，你可以告訴店員想要什麼，或指著菜單板。店員接著會把它輸入收銀機。付款後，他們會替你準備餐點。如果你要外帶，他們會把食物放進紙袋。如果你打算內用，他們會把食物放在鋪著紙墊的托盤上。餐點準備好後，你可以把托盤拿到空桌坐下。記得拿吸管、番茄醬和餐巾紙。這是一份麥香雞餐的樣子。套餐通常包含漢堡、薯條和飲料。如果你想，也可以加大，但普通大小的套餐其實已經含有一個人足夠多的不健康熱量。對非常趕時間的人來說，這家麥當勞也有得來速窗口。你可以透過外面的菜單板，用麥克風和喇叭直接點餐。等你到達得來速窗口時，餐點應該已經準備好了。你把錢交給戴著耳機、掛著假笑的人，他們給你食物，然後你就可以上路。這一切都不用下車。人生實在不能再更懶了。
answers: It is located in Huatan.
A meal usually includes a burger, french fries, and a drink.
They order through an outside menu board with a microphone and speaker.

10.title: Plastic Blocks
paras: Playing with interlocking blocks is a lot of fun. You can use your imagination to build all sorts of things. You can build a tower, a spaceship, a house or anything else you can dream up; the sky is the limit.

The colorful blocks are helping these boys to explore and expand their creativity. The girl on the left has stacked the blocks on top of one another and made a long narrow column, like a tower. The other boy has invented some strange device that only he understands.

As a child, my brother and I played with similar blocks called Lego’s. They kept us busy for hours on end. My favorite things to build were spaceships and trucks. My brother liked to build houses. Whenever we had extra allowance money to spend, we would quite often buy more Lego’s. We had boxes and boxes of the things.
Now, when my nephews are playing with them, I like to join in as I still enjoy building things with them. And they enjoy the time we play together because adults don’t often play with toys.
questions: What can children build with interlocking blocks?
What did the girl on the left build?
Why does the writer still join his nephews?
10.translation: 玩可互相嵌合的積木很有趣。你可以運用想像力建造各式各樣的東西。你可以蓋塔、太空船、房子，或任何你想像得到的東西；可能性無限。這些彩色積木正在幫助男孩們探索並擴展創造力。左邊的女孩把積木一個疊一個，做出一根又長又窄、像塔一樣的柱子。另一個男孩發明了某種只有他自己懂的奇怪裝置。我小時候，哥哥和我玩過類似的積木，叫做樂高。它們讓我們一玩就是好幾個小時。我最喜歡做的是太空船和卡車，哥哥則喜歡蓋房子。每當我們有多餘零用錢可以花時，常常會買更多樂高。我們有一箱又一箱的積木。現在，當我的姪子們玩它們時，我也喜歡加入，因為我仍然喜歡用積木建造東西。他們也喜歡我們一起玩的時間，因為大人不常玩玩具。
answers: They can build towers, spaceships, houses, or anything they imagine.
She built a long narrow column like a tower.
He still enjoys building things with the blocks.

10.title: On Top of the Podium
paras: After two months of hard training, the race is finally over. What had taken so much time and preparation was finished in less than 20 seconds. But for at least one of the students, it was all worthwhile.

The crowd watched as the third place competitor stepped onto the podium. She waved to everyone and quietly nodded, thanking them for the applause without saying a word. She was then congratulated with a handshake by the principal and presented with a bronze medal.

Everyone cheered a bit louder as the second place runner stepped up. She did not wave or make any gesture of thanks. In fact, she seemed upset at being second. The principal patted her on the shoulder and said she had done well, but she felt he was being patronizing. Regardless, she accepted the silver medal.

As the last of the three took her place on the top of the podium, the crowd gave her a standing ovation. The shy girl tried being modest, but couldn’t help but to smile uncontrollably amidst the cheers and whistles. She bowed several times to show her gratitude. The principal shook her hand, just as he had the others, and congratulated her on behalf of the school.

She bowed once more and as she did, the head of the school placed a gold medal around her neck. When the young girl rose again, this time with the award around her neck, the audience went wild. She couldn't believe the reception she was getting, but it made her feel so good inside.

Before stepping down, the polite girl gave her competitors a big hug. The other girls had done their best, too and were great athletes. Beating them was quite an accomplishment and will perhaps inspire her to train even harder in the future. With lots of practice and training, she may stand on top of the podium once again.
questions: How long had the students trained before the race?
What did the winner receive?
What did the winner do before stepping down?
10.translation: 經過兩個月的辛苦訓練，比賽終於結束了。花了那麼多時間和準備的事情，不到二十秒就完成了。但至少對其中一名學生來說，一切都值得。觀眾看著第三名選手走上頒獎台。她向大家揮手，安靜地點頭，不發一語地感謝掌聲。接著校長和她握手祝賀，並頒給她一面銅牌。第二名跑者走上台時，大家歡呼得更大聲。她沒有揮手，也沒有做任何感謝動作。事實上，她似乎對得到第二名感到不高興。校長拍拍她的肩膀，說她表現得很好，但她覺得他是在用居高臨下的語氣安慰她。無論如何，她還是收下了銀牌。最後一位選手站上頒獎台最高處時，觀眾起立鼓掌。害羞的女孩試著保持謙虛，但在歡呼和口哨聲中忍不住笑了起來。她鞠躬好幾次以表達感謝。校長像對其他人一樣和她握手，代表學校向她道賀。她又鞠了一躬，這時校長把金牌掛到她脖子上。年輕女孩再次抬起身來，這次脖子上掛著獎牌，觀眾都沸騰了。她不敢相信自己受到這樣的歡迎，但這讓她心裡非常開心。下台前，這位有禮貌的女孩給了競爭對手一個大大的擁抱。其他女孩也都盡力了，而且都是很棒的運動員。打敗她們是相當了不起的成就，也許會激勵她未來更加努力訓練。透過大量練習和訓練，她也許能再次站上頒獎台最高處。
answers: They trained for two months.
She received a gold medal.
She gave her competitors a big hug.

10.title: Shooting Gallery
paras: When I walk into the night-market, the owners of this booth shudder. They know that if I decide to pick up one of their guns, prizes will start to disappear faster than Oprah's lunch. Lucky for them, the prizes are worth less than the cost of playing the game, which is about 30NT for 10 shots.

Sometimes I use the AK-47 look-alike, but not often as this makes the targets that much closer and easier to hit. This is because rifles are far more accurate than handguns and make shooting the balloons like shooting fish in a barrel. It isn't much fun if the game is too easy, right?

Even though the fake 9mm only shoots plastic bee-bees, holding it in my hands still makes me feel like a tough guy. There is something about staring down the barrel of a semi-automatic weapon that you just can't explain; kind of like the feeling you get when you sit behind the wheel of a '64 Mustang—it just makes the beast inside you come alive.

I usually aim for the rotating wheel with the balloons stuck in it. A moving target is much more fun to shoot at than a stationary one. The first couple of shots usually go to waste trying to figure out where the gun is aimed, because the sights don't necessarily point in the same direction as the bee-bee is going to fly. I guess that's the price you have to pay when you use cheap toy guns.
questions: Why do booth owners shudder when the writer arrives?
Why does the writer not often use the rifle?
Why do the first shots often go to waste?
10.translation: 當我走進夜市時，這個攤位的老闆們都會發抖。他們知道，如果我決定拿起他們的一把槍，獎品就會比歐普拉的午餐消失得更快。幸好對他們來說，獎品價值低於玩遊戲的成本，大約三十元可以射十發。有時我會用看起來像 AK-47 的槍，但不常用，因為這樣目標會更近、更容易打中。這是因為步槍比手槍準確得多，打氣球就像甕中捉鱉。遊戲太容易就不好玩了，對吧？即使假 9mm 手槍只射塑膠 BB 彈，拿在手上仍讓我覺得自己像個硬漢。盯著半自動武器槍管的感覺有種難以解釋的魅力，有點像坐在 1964 年野馬車方向盤後的感覺，會讓你內心的野獸甦醒。我通常瞄準插著氣球的旋轉輪。移動目標比固定目標有趣得多。前幾發通常都浪費在摸清槍瞄準哪裡，因為準星不一定指向 BB 彈實際飛去的方向。我想這就是使用便宜玩具槍必須付出的代價。
answers: They know he may win prizes quickly.
The rifle makes the targets too easy to hit.
He is figuring out where the toy gun is aimed.

10.title: Shooting Baskets
paras: Shooting baskets is so popular that someone has even made an arcade game out of it. It costs approximately 20NT to play. Put your coins in and push the start button to get it going. The balls will roll down to the holding area in front of you and the clock will begin to count down. The idea is to score at least 50 points before the clock runs out. If you reach this goal in the given time, you will move on to the next stage. Each stage is progressively more difficult, requiring more and more points to be scored in order to continue. During some of the stages, the basket will even move from side to side and sometimes it even goes up and down—as if it wasn't difficult enough already. It seems easy, but when you are racing to shoot baskets, sometimes they just don't go in. If you get too flustered, just slow down and carefully line up your shot to get back on track. You can also link two or more machines together so that you can play against your friends. If you repeatedly lose to your friends, you can buy a machine of your own and keep it at home. This way you will be able to practice whenever you have spare time and ensure that you will be the "King of the Hoops" for a long time to come.
questions: How much does the basketball arcade game cost?
What must players do before the clock runs out?
How can players compete against friends?
10.translation: 投籃非常受歡迎，甚至有人把它做成了街機遊戲。玩一次大約二十元。投入硬幣並按下開始鈕，遊戲就會啟動。球會滾到你面前的置球區，時鐘也會開始倒數。目標是在時間結束前至少得到五十分。如果你在規定時間內達到目標，就能進入下一關。每一關都會逐漸變難，需要得到越來越多分才能繼續。有些關卡中，籃框甚至會左右移動，有時還會上下移動，好像原本還不夠難似的。看起來很簡單，但當你趕著投籃時，有時球就是不進。如果你太慌張，就放慢速度，仔細瞄準，讓自己回到正軌。你也可以把兩台或更多機器連在一起，和朋友對戰。如果你一直輸給朋友，可以買一台自己的機器放在家裡。這樣你就能在有空時練習，確保自己能長久當「投籃之王」。
answers: It costs about 20NT.
They must score at least 50 points.
They can link two or more machines together.

10.title: Sale on Aisle Five
paras: Chuck is going shopping at Carrefour. Near the entrance he sees four rows of shopping carts for customer use. He tries to separate one from the bunch, but it is attached by a chain. He pulls on the pin several times but it won't budge. Suddenly he realizes that the carts have small coin-operated locks attached to them.
Chuck digs a 10 dollar coin out of his pocket and sticks it into the slot. As he pushes on the coin, the pin that is attached to the chain pops out. He is now able to pull the cart out and use it.
Inside the big store, Chuck pushes the shopping cart down the aisles. He is in search of a bargain. Soon he finds a sale on homeless children and decides to stock up.

His first buy is a young girl in pink pajamas. He carefully sits her in the child's seat on the cart. She will be perfect for doing his laundry and dishes. She can also make his bed and tidy up around the house.
Next, he picks up a small boy. For now he isn't much good, but as he grows older and becomes stronger he will be able to do yard work and house repairs. Besides, at 500NT, it’s a steal.

The last little girl he finds near the checkout counter. He doesn't have much use for her, but he figures maybe his uncle Larry could use her to clean the bathroom. In any case, at these prices, who can say no?

After paying he pushes his cart full of kids back to the car and loads them into the trunk. Then he returns the cart and slides it back into line. As he pushes the pin back into the lock, his coin comes back out of the front. He pulls it out of the slot and places it back in his pocket. He happily turns to go home, knowing that by tomorrow his house will be nice and clean.
questions: Why could Chuck not pull out a shopping cart at first?
What did he put into the slot?
What happened when he returned the cart?
10.translation: 查克要去家樂福購物。靠近入口處，他看到四排供顧客使用的購物車。他試著把一台車從一排車中拉出來，但它被鏈條鎖住。他拉了幾次插栓，但它一動也不動。突然他意識到購物車上有小型投幣鎖。查克從口袋裡挖出一枚十元硬幣，塞進投幣孔。當他推入硬幣時，連著鏈條的插栓彈了出來。現在他可以把推車拉出來使用了。在大賣場裡，查克推著購物車走過走道。他正在尋找便宜貨。很快他發現「流浪兒童特價」，決定囤貨。他第一個買的是一個穿粉紅睡衣的小女孩。他小心地把她坐在購物車的兒童座上。她會很適合替他洗衣服和洗碗，也可以幫他鋪床、整理家裡。接著，他挑了一個小男孩。現在他還沒什麼用，但等他長大變強壯後，就能做庭院工作和房屋修繕。此外，五百元真是划算。最後一個小女孩是在結帳櫃台附近找到的。他其實不太用得上她，但想也許叔叔賴瑞可以讓她打掃浴室。無論如何，以這種價格，誰能說不呢？付款後，他推著裝滿孩子的購物車回到車旁，把他們裝進後車廂。接著他把推車送回去，滑回隊伍裡。當他把插栓推回鎖中時，硬幣從前方退了出來。他把硬幣從投幣孔取出放回口袋，開心地轉身回家，知道到了明天他的房子就會又整齊又乾淨。
answers: The cart was attached by a chain and coin-operated lock.
He put in a 10 dollar coin.
His coin came back out.

11.title: The Clumsy Dancer
paras: Today Lewis fell in ballet class and hit his head. His teacher sent him to the infirmary. She had Leo go along just to look after him.

Leo made an icepack for his friend's head. He hoped that the icepack would reduce the swelling and prevent any further brain damage. He wrapped the icepack in a towel so that it wouldn’t be too cold on Lewis’ skin. The towel would also absorb any condensation.
When Leo returned to the infirmary, he found Lewis fast asleep, or possibly unconscious! He gently slipped the ice pack under poor friend’s head, trying not to disturb the clumsy dancer.
When Lewis and if awakes, he might have a throbbing headache, but thanks to Leo's first-aid, the tiny dancer will be back doing pirouettes in no time. Hopefully, he will be more careful next time.
questions: Why was Lewis sent to the infirmary?
Why did Leo wrap the icepack in a towel?
What might Lewis have when he wakes up?
11.translation: 今天路易斯在芭蕾課上跌倒並撞到頭。老師把他送到保健室，還讓里奧陪他去照看他。里奧為朋友的頭做了一個冰袋，希望冰袋能減少腫脹，並防止進一步的腦部傷害。他把冰袋包在毛巾裡，這樣放在路易斯皮膚上就不會太冷。毛巾也會吸收凝結的水氣。當里奧回到保健室時，他發現路易斯睡得很熟，或者可能是昏迷了！他輕輕把冰袋滑到可憐朋友的頭下，盡量不要打擾這位笨手笨腳的舞者。如果路易斯醒來，他可能會頭痛得一跳一跳的，但多虧里奧的急救，這位小舞者很快就能回去跳芭蕾旋轉了。希望下次他會更小心。
answers: He fell in ballet class and hit his head.
He wrapped it so it would not be too cold and to absorb condensation.
He might have a throbbing headache.

11.title: Vending Machines
paras: Picture: When you are thirsty and don't have time to run to a tea stand or a convenience store, you can probably find a vending machine nearby. Vending machines can hold many different things, like drinks, snacks or even CD's. They are very handy. In the vending machine pictured here you have a choice between water, water and water. Surprisingly, the thirsty boy decides on water. He puts his change into the coin slot and pushes the button below his selection.

Picture 2: This machine holds many different drinks unlike the first one that just holds water. The assorted drinks are displayed behind glass. When you put your money in and push a button, the drink will drop down into the bottom of the machine. You simply push back the flap and reach in to grab your beverage. Then you can quench your thirst.

Picture 3: When the machine is empty, someone has to refill it, right? Of course! And today this lady is that someone. After opening the door of the refrigerated machine, she can refill all the compartments with bottled drinks. In this particular machine there is only water so the job is quite easy. In machines with more than one choice, she must make sure she puts the right drink into the right compartment. Otherwise someone may hit the Coke button and end up with tomato juice. That would be a nasty surprise, wouldn’t it?
questions: What can vending machines hold?
How do you get a drink from the second machine?
What could happen if drinks are placed in the wrong compartment?
11.translation: 圖一：當你口渴又沒時間跑去茶攤或便利商店時，附近大概可以找到自動販賣機。自動販賣機可以裝許多不同的東西，例如飲料、點心，甚至 CD。它們非常方便。照片中的自動販賣機提供的選擇是水、水和水。令人意外的是，這位口渴的男孩選了水。他把零錢投入投幣孔，並按下所選項目下方的按鈕。圖二：這台機器裝有許多不同飲料，不像第一台只裝水。各式飲料陳列在玻璃後方。當你投入錢並按下按鈕，飲料就會掉到機器底部。你只要推開擋板，把手伸進去拿飲料，就能解渴。圖三：當機器空了，總得有人補貨，對吧？當然！今天這位女士就是那個人。打開冷藏販賣機的門後，她可以把瓶裝飲料補進所有隔層。這台機器只有水，所以工作很簡單。如果機器有不只一種選擇，她必須確定把正確飲料放進正確隔層。否則有人按可樂按鈕，最後卻拿到番茄汁。那會是很糟糕的驚喜，不是嗎？
answers: They can hold drinks, snacks, or even CDs.
You put in money, push a button, and reach into the flap to grab it.
Someone might push one button and receive the wrong drink.

11.title: Video Conferencing
paras: Video conferencing is a relatively new technology to many people. Although it has been around quite a while, only within the last few years has it really taken off. Now it is hard to find a computer or a laptop without a camera hidden somewhere.

So what exactly is video conferencing? It is when you use a web cam to broadcast your image and a microphone for talking through to one or more people. Using this feature allows you to keep in contact with family or friends on a much more personal level than just chatting with idle words on a screen. It is also a great tool for international businesses whose clients may be on the other side of the world. It is far more personal than just e-mailing or even phoning, allowing businesses and their clientele to see one another when conducting business.

A lot of computers now have the capability to video conference right out of the box. This means that no expensive add-ons are required as the software is already there and the hardware is often integrated. Surprisingly, no one has started billing for this yet, but once everyone is hooked on the technology, additional costs are sure to follow.
questions: What do you use for video conferencing?
Why is video conferencing more personal than email?
Why might extra costs appear later?
11.translation: 視訊會議對許多人來說是相對新的科技。雖然它已存在一段時間，但直到近幾年才真正普及。現在很難找到沒有某處藏著攝影機的電腦或筆記型電腦。那麼視訊會議到底是什麼？就是你用網路攝影機傳送自己的影像，並用麥克風和一個或多個人交談。使用這項功能可以讓你與家人或朋友保持更個人化的聯繫，比只在螢幕上用閒聊文字溝通更親近。它也是國際企業的好工具，因為客戶可能在世界另一端。它比只寄電子郵件或甚至打電話更有個人感，讓企業和客戶在洽談業務時能看見彼此。許多電腦現在一開箱就具備視訊會議能力。這表示不需要昂貴的附加設備，因為軟體已經在裡面，硬體也常常是內建的。令人意外的是，目前還沒有人開始為此收費，但一旦大家都迷上這項科技，額外費用一定會接著出現。
answers: You use a web cam and a microphone.
People can see one another while talking.
Once everyone is hooked on the technology, companies may start charging.

11.title: A Wheelbarrow Race
paras: These girls are taking part in a wheelbarrow race. But where is the wheelbarrow? Well… that is the fun part: you have to pretend that one person is a wheelbarrow. The girl in the back holds the other girl by the legs just like the handles on a wheelbarrow. The girl in the front then walks on her hands as quickly as she can.

At first, they move quite fast toward the finish line. Then both of them begin to get tired. The back girl has a hard time trying to hold the other girls legs up and they keep slipping out of her hands. The front girl’s arms are getting very tired and she is having trouble putting one hand if front of the other.

When they are almost at the finish line, the wheelbarrow girl’s arms finally give out. She isn’t strong enough to keep going. Her body drops to the ground. Then the girl in the back trips over her fallen friend and lands on top of her. The two girls just lie there giggling happily. They didn’t win, but they had a lot of fun trying!
questions: How does the back girl hold her friend?
How does the front girl move?
Why does the wheelbarrow girl fall?
11.translation: 這些女孩正在參加推車賽跑。但推車在哪裡？嗯，這就是有趣的地方：你必須假裝其中一個人是推車。後面的女孩抓住另一個女孩的腿，就像抓著推車把手一樣。前面的女孩則用雙手盡快往前走。一開始，她們很快朝終點線前進。接著兩人都開始累了。後面的女孩很吃力地想把前面女孩的腿抬起來，但腿一直從她手中滑掉。前面女孩的手臂也非常累，連一手接一手往前放都有困難。當她們快到終點時，當推車的女孩手臂終於撐不住了。她不夠強壯，無法繼續前進。她的身體倒在地上。接著後面的女孩被倒下的朋友絆倒，摔在她身上。兩個女孩只是躺在那裡開心地咯咯笑。她們沒有贏，但嘗試的過程很有趣。
answers: She holds her friend by the legs like wheelbarrow handles.
She walks on her hands.
Her arms get tired and give out.

11.title: An Addicting Puzzle
paras: My mother bought me a Rubic’s Cube for my last birthday. At the time it seemed like a good idea, but in retrospect, I think she was punishing me for something.

If you have never played with a Rubic’s Cube before, don’t do it! It’s a trap! Once you have picked it up you will never be able to put it down again. And it’s not like it is fun: it’s just addictive. It’s almost like some kind of torture.
My last birthday was almost 6 months ago and you would think that I would have solved the stupid thing by now, but I haven’t. No, not even close. The most I’ve managed to do is three sides.

Friends have come to visit and made the mistake of trying to solve the evil puzzle-cube, too. A couple of them thought it was really cool and went out and bought their own. I haven’t seen either of them since.

There has only been one person that I know of that has been able to put it down without any problem. That was my friend Cookie. But honestly, I don’t think she is smart enough to be puzzled by anything; she is a cheerleader after all.

I guess I am destined to twist and turn this damned colored cube in my callused hands until the day I die. There is no turning back now. The best that I can hope for is that it breaks or that I get hit by a large, fast-moving truck. Damn you Rubic, damn you to hell!
questions: Who bought the writer a Rubic’s Cube?
What is the most the writer has solved?
Why does the writer call the cube addictive?
11.translation: 我媽媽在我上次生日時買了一個魔術方塊給我。當時看起來是個好主意，但回想起來，我覺得她可能是在懲罰我什麼。如果你從沒玩過魔術方塊，千萬別玩！那是個陷阱！一旦拿起來，你就再也放不下。它也不是好玩，而是會上癮。幾乎像某種折磨。我的上次生日已經快六個月前了，你可能會以為我現在應該已經解開那個愚蠢的東西，但我沒有。不，甚至差得遠。我最多只完成過三面。朋友來訪時也犯了同樣錯誤，試著解開這個邪惡的方塊。有幾個人覺得它很酷，還跑去買了自己的。從那以後我就沒再見過他們。我認識的人中只有一個能毫無問題地放下它，那就是我的朋友 Cookie。不過說真的，我不覺得她聰明到會被任何東西困住；畢竟她是啦啦隊員。我想我命中注定要用長繭的手不停扭轉這個該死的彩色方塊，直到死去。現在已經回不了頭。我最多只能希望它壞掉，或我被一輛又大又快的卡車撞上。該死的 Rubic，願你下地獄！
answers: His mother bought it for his birthday.
He has managed to solve three sides.
Once he picks it up, he cannot put it down.

11.title: Celebrating the Winter Solstice
paras: Picture 1: Along time ago, farmers started celebrating the shortest day of the year. This was because it was, and still is, a time of rest and relaxation after finishing the fall harvest. In English, this day is known as the winter solstice, but in Chinese it is referred to as Dongzhi which, roughly translated, means “extreme of winter” or “winter is coming.”

Picture 2: As with many Taiwanese events, Dongzhi is a time for families to come together and, of course, food plays an important role in the festivities. The traditional food of this holiday is tangyuan, which are rice balls. They are made with glutinous rice flour that is rolled into balls. Eating them at the winter solstice signifies family unity and prosperity.

Picture 3: Children love to eat tangyuan. Not surprising since most youngsters love to eat anything sweet. Parents will sometimes say to their kids that after eating tangyuan they will be one year older. The winter solstice is not actually the beginning of the New Year, but it is close and for many it is a prelude to the most auspicious Chinese holiday on the calendar, the Chinese New Year.
questions: Why did farmers celebrate the winter solstice?
What food is traditionally eaten at Dongzhi?
What does eating tangyuan signify?
11.translation: 圖一：很久以前，農民開始慶祝一年中白天最短的日子。這是因為它過去和現在都是秋收結束後休息放鬆的時候。英文稱這一天為 winter solstice，中文則稱為冬至，大致意思是「冬天極致」或「冬天來了」。圖二：和許多台灣節慶一樣，冬至是家人團聚的時刻，當然食物在慶祝活動中扮演重要角色。這個節日的傳統食物是湯圓，也就是米糰。它們由糯米粉搓成球。冬至吃湯圓象徵家庭團圓和興旺。圖三：孩子們喜歡吃湯圓。這並不令人意外，因為大多數小孩都喜歡吃甜食。父母有時會告訴孩子，吃了湯圓就會長大一歲。冬至其實不是新年的開始，但它很接近，而且對許多人來說，它是農曆新年這個最吉祥節日的序曲。
answers: It was a time to rest after the fall harvest.
Tangyuan, or rice balls, are traditionally eaten.
It signifies family unity and prosperity.

11.title: Grandma's Birthday Cake
paras: Yesterday was Grandma's sixty-seventh birthday, so mom and I decided to make a birthday cake for her. It took us all afternoon, but it was lots of fun. I especially liked decorating the cake. The party was held at my Auntie's house. All my relatives were there. It was good to see all of them again. We hadn't been together to celebrate her birthday since she turned sixty. Everyone in the family bought Grandma a gift. Uncle Bob's family brought flowers, Aunt Linda bought her some new clothes, and crazy cousin Luke bought her a hairless cat that looked like something out of a science-fiction movie. She appreciated all the gifts, even the cat! After opening the gifts, I took the cake out of the refrigerator and placed it on the dining room table. Candles, which looked like a six and a seven, were stuck in the center of the cake and lit. While they burned, everyone sang Happy Birthday.

As soon as we finished singing, Grandma made a wish and blew out the candles. I think she secretly wished that the ugly cat would disappear, but she didn’t say anything. Anyway, you can’t tell anyone what your wish is or it won’t come true. So, I guess we’ll never know. Next, the cake was served. Uncle Bob's wife cut it, my cousin Susan put each piece on a plate and I served it to the hungry guests. Everyone said that it was delicious and it was gone in no time. Even fussy old Grandpa Joe had a piece and he usually hates everything, so it must have been good. With the presents opened and the cake finished there wasn’t much left to do except relax. The children played in the back yard while the adults did what adults do: they chatted about work, the weather and other stuff that kids could care less about. Then it was time to go home. The party was over and it was time to go home and let Grandma recover from all the excitement. We all said our goodbyes and hugged Grandma. As we drove away, I thought about how she was going to cope with her new pet: the weird, bald cat. Then I dozed off in the backseat. Zzz.
questions: How old was Grandma on her birthday?
What unusual gift did cousin Luke buy?
Who served the cake to the guests?
11.translation: 昨天是奶奶六十七歲生日，所以媽媽和我決定為她做一個生日蛋糕。這花了我們整個下午，但非常有趣。我尤其喜歡裝飾蛋糕。派對在阿姨家舉行。所有親戚都在場。再次見到大家真好。我們自從奶奶六十歲生日後，就沒有聚在一起為她慶生了。家裡每個人都買了禮物給奶奶。鮑伯叔叔一家帶了花，琳達阿姨買了新衣服給她，瘋狂的表哥路克買了一隻看起來像科幻電影裡東西的無毛貓。她很感謝所有禮物，甚至包括那隻貓。打開禮物後，我把蛋糕從冰箱拿出來，放在餐廳桌上。像六和七形狀的蠟燭插在蛋糕中央並被點燃。蠟燭燃燒時，大家唱生日快樂歌。歌唱完後，奶奶許了願並吹熄蠟燭。我想她偷偷希望那隻醜貓會消失，但她什麼也沒說。反正願望不能告訴任何人，否則不會實現。所以我想我們永遠不會知道。接著開始分蛋糕。鮑伯叔叔的妻子切蛋糕，我表妹蘇珊把每一塊放在盤子上，而我端給飢餓的客人。大家都說蛋糕很好吃，很快就吃光了。連挑剔的老爺爺喬都吃了一塊，而他平常什麼都討厭，所以蛋糕一定真的不錯。禮物打開、蛋糕吃完後，剩下也沒什麼事可做，只能放鬆。孩子們在後院玩，大人們做大人會做的事：聊工作、天氣和其他小孩一點也不在乎的事情。然後就到了回家的時間。派對結束了，該回家讓奶奶從所有興奮中恢復過來。我們都說再見並擁抱奶奶。開車離開時，我想著她要如何應付她的新寵物，那隻奇怪的禿貓。接著我就在後座睡著了。呼。
answers: She was sixty-seven years old.
He bought her a hairless cat.
The narrator served the cake to the guests.

11.title: Persistence Pays Off
paras: Every afternoon Jenny meets with other neighborhood mothers in the park. They bring their kids along so that they can run, play together and burn off some energy before going home for their afternoon nap. Meanwhile, the mothers have a chance to relax and catch up on the latest gossip. Jenny watched as her daughter tried to climb one of the slippery poles near the trees on the east side of the playground. Placing one hand over the other, she tried to shimmy up the pole. Her legs wrapped tightly around it and she seemed to be making progress, but then down she would come. She made several attempts before her mother thought that she was finally going to give up, but then up she went again in what seemed to be a lost cause. Jenny could only shake her head in disapproval. “She never knows when to give up,” Jenny laughed, trying to conceal her embarrassment. “My son would have given up right away,” one of the other mothers replied. “I think he gives up too easily.” “Mine, too,” another mother said. “You should be proud that your daughter is so persistent.” “I suppose you're right,” Jenny agreed. “It is a good quality to have, I guess.” Just as she finished speaking, she turned to see her daughter nearing the top of the bar. Jenny felt so proud of her little girl. None of the other children could do it, but hers could and all because she wouldn't give up.
questions: Why do mothers bring their kids to the park?
What was Jenny’s daughter trying to climb?
Why did Jenny feel proud?
11.translation: 每天下午，珍妮都會和其他鄰居媽媽在公園碰面。她們帶孩子一起來，讓孩子跑跑、一起玩，在回家午睡前消耗一些精力。與此同時，媽媽們也有機會放鬆並聊聊最新八卦。珍妮看著女兒試著爬上遊樂場東側樹旁的一根滑桿。她一手接一手，試著沿著桿子往上爬。雙腿緊緊纏住桿子，看起來似乎有進展，但接著又滑下來。她試了好幾次，媽媽以為她終於要放棄了，但她又再次往上爬，雖然看起來像是徒勞無功。珍妮只能不以為然地搖頭。「她從來不知道什麼時候該放棄，」珍妮笑著說，試圖掩飾尷尬。「我兒子一定馬上就放棄了，」另一位媽媽回答。「我覺得他太容易放棄了。」「我家的也是，」另一位媽媽說。「妳應該為妳女兒這麼堅持感到驕傲。」「我想妳說得對，」珍妮同意。「這算是一個好品質吧。」她剛說完，轉頭就看見女兒快要爬到桿子頂端。珍妮為小女兒感到非常驕傲。其他孩子都做不到，但她的孩子做到了，全因為她不肯放棄。
answers: They let the kids play and burn off energy before nap time.
She was trying to climb a slippery pole.
Her daughter reached near the top because she would not give up.

11.title: Janet's Good Study Habits
paras: As soon as Janet gets home from school each day, she likes to finish her homework. If she gets it out of the way as soon as possible, she knows that she can enjoy the rest of the day without having to worry about it.

Today Janet is lucky, as she only has a little homework to do. She pulls her notebook and textbook out of her schoolbag. Then she grabs a dictionary from the bookshelf and sets it on the table. Next, she leafs through the textbook and finds the page she needs to work on for English class. There are a few new words and phrases in today’s homework that she is not sure of. She flips through the dictionary and finds the first new vocabulary word and writes the definition in her notebook. She then tries to use the new word in the appropriate context by forming a sentence with it.

Because she is a very diligent student, she repeats this process with each of the vocabulary words she has to learn: writing each one down and then using it in a new sentence. After she has written down all the definitions for the new words, she spends a few minutes reading them over. She wants to make sure she understands everything completely. By doing her English homework like this everyday, Janet is able to increase her vocabulary dramatically. And by doing it as soon as she gets home, she can put her books away and forget about them until tomorrow. She is now free to watch TV, go outside and play, or do whatever she likes because her homework is finished.
questions: When does Janet like to finish her homework?
What does Janet do with each new vocabulary word?
Why can Janet enjoy the rest of the day?
11.translation: 珍妮特每天放學回家後，都喜歡先完成作業。如果她能盡快把作業處理掉，就知道自己可以享受一天剩下的時間，不必再擔心。今天珍妮特很幸運，因為她只有一點作業要做。她從書包裡拿出筆記本和課本。接著她從書架上拿了一本字典，放在桌上。然後她翻課本，找到英文課需要完成的頁面。今天的作業裡有幾個新單字和片語她不太確定。她翻字典找到第一個新單字，並把定義寫在筆記本裡。接著她試著用這個新字造一個符合語境的句子。因為她是非常勤奮的學生，她對每個要學的單字都重複這個過程：寫下每個單字，然後用它造新句子。寫完所有新字定義後，她花幾分鐘再讀一遍。她想確定自己完全理解。透過每天這樣做英文作業，珍妮特能大幅增加字彙量。而且因為她一回家就做完，她可以把書收起來，直到明天都不用再想它們。現在她可以自由看電視、到外面玩，或做任何她喜歡的事，因為作業已經完成了。
answers: She likes to finish it as soon as she gets home.
She writes the definition and makes a sentence with it.
Her homework is already finished.

11.title: Christmas Concert
paras: My class had been practicing for the Christmas concert for about a month or so. My teacher chose the song "I Have a Dream" for us to sing. It wasn't too difficult to learn the words and it only took me a couple of days before I had memorized it. I think most of the students learned the song quickly, but the dance steps were another matter all together, especially with my two left feet. During practice we kept bumping into one another and stepping on each other's toes. For a while, I didn't think we would ever be able to learn the entire routine, but we kept practicing and practicing and practicing some more. Practice makes perfect, right? On the night of the concert, everyone was very nervous. No one was confident that we would finally be able to do the dance without messing it up. I was so anxious that I even forgot to bring my red shirt that my teacher had asked me to wear; it was the last thing on my mind. When we finally got up on stage, I thought I was going to faint. My palms were sweaty, my stomach was queasy, and I was shaking like a leaf. The music started and my body slowly began to move with the song. Then I started to sing. I couldn't believe it, I was actually doing it and so was everyone else. We stepped in time and did all our moves like professional dancers. It was so cool. When the music ended, we all raised our arms in victory. We felt very proud of what we had accomplished and everyone in the crowd gave us a big applause. I guess it's true: practice does make perfect!
questions: How long had the class practiced for the concert?
What happened while they practiced the dance steps?
How did they know they did well?
11.translation: 我們班為聖誕音樂會練習了大約一個月左右。老師選了〈I Have a Dream〉這首歌讓我們唱。歌詞不太難學，我只花了幾天就背起來。我想大多數學生很快就學會了這首歌，但舞步完全是另一回事，尤其是我手腳笨拙。練習時，我們一直撞到彼此、踩到彼此的腳。有一陣子，我不覺得我們能學會整套動作，但我們不斷練習、練習，再更多練習。熟能生巧，對吧？音樂會當晚，大家都很緊張。沒有人有信心我們終於能跳完舞而不出錯。我焦慮到甚至忘了帶老師要我穿的紅襯衫；那是我最後會想到的事。當我們終於上台時，我以為自己要昏倒了。我的手心冒汗，胃不舒服，整個人抖得像葉子一樣。音樂開始後，我的身體慢慢跟著歌曲移動。接著我開始唱。我不敢相信，我真的做到了，而且其他人也做到了。我們跟著拍子踏步，像專業舞者一樣完成所有動作。太酷了。音樂結束時，我們全都舉起手臂慶祝勝利。我們為自己完成的事感到非常驕傲，台下觀眾也給了我們熱烈掌聲。我想這是真的：熟能生巧！
answers: They had practiced for about a month.
They bumped into one another and stepped on each other’s toes.
The crowd gave them big applause.

12.title: Pretending
paras: It is a rainy day in October. Joe and Steven are terribly bored. They need a way to keep occupied without getting into too much trouble.
Joe has an idea. He is going to use his father's new digital camera to take pictures of Steven while Steven pretends to be a motorcycle racer.

Joe's little brother's bicycle makes a perfect make-believe motorbike. Steven hops on. He burns around the living room pretending that he is in a race. Joe acts like a photo journalist and begins snapping pictures.
Soon Steven approaches the finish line and raises his hand in victory. Joe tries to capture this glorious moment on his camera for the front page of tomorrow's sports section.

Steven and Joe continue pretending like this for a few more minutes, but soon they grow tired of this game and boredom sets in again. They move on, looking for something more exciting to do. Hopefully, they don’t find any mischief on their way.
questions: Why are Joe and Steven bored?
What does Steven pretend to be?
What does Joe try to capture with the camera?
12.translation: 十月的一個雨天，喬和史蒂文非常無聊。他們需要找個方法讓自己有事可做，又不要惹上太多麻煩。喬想到一個主意。他要用爸爸的新數位相機，拍史蒂文假裝成摩托車賽車手的照片。喬弟弟的腳踏車正好可以當作想像中的摩托車。史蒂文跳上車，在客廳裡到處衝，假裝自己正在比賽。喬則裝成攝影記者，開始拍照。很快地，史蒂文接近終點線，並舉手慶祝勝利。喬想用相機捕捉這個光榮時刻，放到明天體育版的頭版。史蒂文和喬又這樣假裝玩了幾分鐘，但很快就玩膩了，無聊又回來了。他們繼續尋找更刺激的事來做。希望他們一路上不要找到什麼惡作劇。
answers: Because it is a rainy day and they need something to do.
He pretends to be a motorcycle racer.
He tries to capture Steven raising his hand in victory near the finish line.

12.title: Cutting the Lawn
paras: The lawn. It seems like you just finish cutting it and it is time to do it again. It grows so quickly that it is a never ending struggle to keep it short.

Here my mother is pushing the lawn mower alongside a green fence. She is sheltered briefly by the shade of the bushes behind the fence, but soon she will have to cut the grass in the middle of the yard out in the hot sun. She will have to be careful that she doesn't hit her head on the bird feeder that hangs from the lilacs. She wears gloves so that she doesn’t get blisters on her hands. It is also a good idea to wear shoes because sometimes rocks and other hard things fly out from under the mower and hit your feet.

Now my father is helping my mother to empty the mower bag. The mower bag collects the grass clippings so that you don’t have to spend extra time raking. If the lawn is really long, the bag will fill up quickly, but usually you can mow for quite a while before the bag needs emptying. The grass clippings are put into a garbage bag and then they can be composted or used as mulch for the garden.
questions: Why is keeping the lawn short a never ending struggle?
Why does the mother wear gloves?
What can people do with the grass clippings?
12.translation: 草坪。感覺才剛割完，又到了要再割的時候。草長得非常快，所以要讓它保持短短的，像是一場永無止境的奮戰。這裡，我媽媽正沿著綠色圍籬推著割草機。她暫時受到圍籬後方灌木陰影的遮蔽，但很快就必須到院子中央，在炎熱的太陽下割草。她還得小心不要撞到掛在紫丁香上的鳥類餵食器。她戴著手套，這樣手上才不會起水泡。穿鞋也是個好主意，因為有時石頭和其他堅硬的東西會從割草機底下飛出來，打到你的腳。現在我爸爸正在幫媽媽清空割草機的集草袋。集草袋會收集草屑，這樣你就不用另外花時間耙草。如果草坪很長，袋子很快就會裝滿；但通常可以割上一段時間才需要清空。草屑會被放進垃圾袋，之後可以做成堆肥，或當作花園的護根物。
answers: Because the grass grows very quickly.
She wears gloves so she does not get blisters on her hands.
They can compost them or use them as mulch for the garden.

12.title: Digging a Drainage Ditch
paras: Putting in a drainage ditch by hand is a lot of work, but not if you know someone with a backhoe. With a backhoe, it is relatively easy to dig large holes as its long powerful arm is able to dig into the ground with ease.
