At the foot of these steps, this hoe is digging a long, narrow ditch. However, before the hoe operator was able to begin digging, someone had to break up the concrete with a jackhammer. After that, the backhoe could start.

The hoe operator sits on the large machine and uses the controls to move the machine around. He is very careful while working so that he doesn't do any more damage than is necessary. He also has to watch carefully for underground lines and pipes.

To dig a hole, the operator extends the boom and opens the bucket. When the bucket touches the ground, he slowly closes it and moves the arm toward the hoe. This process fills the bucket with dirt. The arm is then raised as the machine turns. When the bucket is over the place where he wants to make a pile, he opens it again and empties the dirt. All this is done without actually moving anywhere, because the body of the machine rotates on its tracks. This process is repeated until you have a big hole or, in this case, a ditch.
questions: What machine makes digging a drainage ditch easier?
What had to be done before the backhoe could start?
Why must the operator watch carefully while working?
12.translation: 用手挖排水溝是非常辛苦的工作，但如果你認識有反鏟挖土機的人，就不一樣了。有了反鏟挖土機，挖大洞相對容易，因為它又長又有力的手臂能輕鬆挖入地面。在這些台階底下，這台挖土機正在挖一條又長又窄的溝。不過，在操作員開始挖之前，必須先有人用破碎機把水泥打碎。之後，反鏟挖土機才能開始工作。操作員坐在大型機器上，用控制桿移動機器。他工作時非常小心，以免造成不必要的損壞。他也必須仔細注意地下的線路和管線。要挖洞時，操作員伸出吊臂並打開挖斗。當挖斗碰到地面時，他慢慢關起挖斗，並把手臂往機器方向移動。這個過程會讓挖斗裝滿泥土。接著手臂升起，機器轉動。當挖斗到達他想堆土的地方上方時，他再次打開挖斗，把泥土倒出來。這一切都可以在機器本身不移動位置的情況下完成，因為機身會在履帶上旋轉。這個過程會一直重複，直到挖出一個大洞，或像這次一樣，挖出一條溝。
answers: A backhoe makes the job easier.
Someone had to break up the concrete with a jackhammer.
He must watch for underground lines and pipes and avoid unnecessary damage.

12.title: Oral Presentations
paras: In today’s working world, we often need to communicate ideas to large audiences. This is usually done through oral presentations. Thus, mastering the art of the oral presentation is an important skill that can help one be successful in his or her profession.

Audio and visual aids are two things that can be incorporated into an oral presentation. They are both great ways to enhance your presentation and make sure the audience clearly understands your main idea. For example, PC projectors can be used to show simple visual aids like graphs, charts or pictures on your topic. Try and keep it simple, though. Inundate your audience with too much information and you might confuse them.

As the speaker, your words and ideas are what everyone comes to hear about. The presentation should only aid you and not be the primary focus; it shouldn’t be ALL about flashy computer images, and super detailed charts, even though they may help. If people leave your presentation remembering nothing but a bunch of graphs or leave blinded from staring at a white projector screen, chances are you didn't give a successful oral presentation.
questions: Why is mastering oral presentations important?
How can audio and visual aids help a presentation?
What should be the main focus of a presentation?
12.translation: 在今日的職場中，我們常常需要向大量聽眾傳達想法。這通常是透過口頭報告來完成。因此，掌握口頭報告的技巧是一項重要能力，能幫助人在自己的職業中成功。音訊和視覺輔助是可以加入口頭報告的兩種工具。它們都能提升你的報告，並確保聽眾清楚理解你的主要想法。例如，電腦投影機可以用來展示簡單的視覺輔助，如與主題相關的圖表、統計圖或圖片。不過，要盡量保持簡單。若向聽眾灌輸太多資訊，可能會讓他們困惑。身為講者，大家是來聽你的話語和想法。簡報應該只是輔助你，而不是主要焦點；即使華麗的電腦影像和超詳細圖表可能有幫助，報告也不應該全都圍繞著它們。如果人們離開你的報告時，只記得一堆圖表，或因盯著白色投影幕而眼花，那你很可能沒有做出成功的口頭報告。
answers: Because it can help someone be successful in his or her profession.
They can enhance the presentation and help the audience understand the main idea.
The speaker’s words and ideas should be the main focus.

12.title: Sheet Protectors
paras: Whether you are an elementary school student or a doctor, you must take good care of some of your important papers. Keeping such papers in sheet protectors is a great way to both organize and protect them. Without them, your materials may become torn (ripped), wrinkled or dirty.
Look at Colby. He is preparing a presentation for his social studies class.

He has a binder, in which he has organized all the papers that he needs. Each paper has been carefully placed into a sheet protector. He leafs through his work to make sure everything is in order. It is a good thing that he has done this because a couple of the pages weren’t in order. He removes them from the sheet protector and reinserts them in their correct places.

Now Colby is ready for his presentation. He is confident that he will do well as he has learned that using sheet protectors will not only give his project a professional look, but more importantly, he knows that his papers will not become unorganized or get damaged.
So learn from Colby: If you want to keep your school work neat and tidy, go out and stock up on sheet protectors today.
questions: Why are sheet protectors useful?
What is Colby preparing for?
Why is Colby confident about his presentation?
12.translation: 無論你是小學生還是醫生，你都必須好好照顧一些重要文件。把這些文件放在文件保護套裡，是整理並保護它們的好方法。沒有保護套，你的資料可能會被撕破、起皺或弄髒。看看科爾比。他正在為社會課準備報告。他有一本活頁夾，裡面整理了所有需要的文件。每一張紙都被仔細放進文件保護套中。他翻閱自己的資料，確認一切都按順序排列。他這樣做真是好事，因為有幾頁順序不對。他把它們從保護套中取出，再重新放回正確的位置。現在科爾比已經準備好報告了。他很有信心自己會表現得很好，因為他學到使用文件保護套不只會讓他的作業看起來專業，更重要的是，他知道自己的文件不會變得亂七八糟或受到損壞。所以向科爾比學習吧：如果你想讓學校作業保持整潔，今天就去多買一些文件保護套吧。
answers: They help organize and protect important papers.
He is preparing a presentation for his social studies class.
He knows his papers are organized, protected, and look professional.

12.title: Staring Death in the Face
paras: Somewhere in Taiwan a daring, and very handsome man taunts death by forcing open the mouth of a ferocious lion. Moments earlier, the man had been waiting for a call. Not knowing the beast was still alive, he got a little too close. When his cell-phone rang the lion mistook it for a tasty bird and swallowed it whole.

Apparently, the call the man was expecting was very important; important enough to risk his life for. He wrestled the lion’s jaws apart and carefully moved his arms past the razor-sharp teeth, over the slimy tongue and into the lion's throat. A small crowd that had gathered cheered as he removed his arm and held aloft his saliva and slime covered phone.
Surely, such a display of bravery and phenomenal strength has never been seen by mortal eyes before. Nor will it probably ever be seen again.
questions: Why did the lion swallow the cell phone?
Why did the man risk his life?
What did the crowd do when he got the phone back?

vocab.zh: 把 A 誤認為 B

12.translation: 在台灣某處，一位大膽而且非常英俊的男子，強行打開一隻凶猛獅子的嘴，挑戰死亡。就在不久前，這名男子一直在等一通電話。他不知道這隻野獸還活著，所以靠得太近。當他的手機響起時，獅子把它誤認為一隻美味的小鳥，整個吞了下去。顯然，男子等待的那通電話非常重要，重要到值得冒生命危險。他用力扳開獅子的下顎，小心地把手臂伸過鋒利如剃刀的牙齒、黏滑的舌頭，進入獅子的喉嚨。一小群聚集起來的人，在他抽出手臂並高高舉起沾滿口水和黏液的手機時歡呼。毫無疑問，凡人的眼睛從未見過如此勇敢與驚人力量的展現，恐怕以後也不會再見到了。
answers: It mistook the ringing phone for a tasty bird.
The call he was expecting was very important.
They cheered when he held up the phone.

12.title: You scratch my back and I’ll eat whatever is crawling on yours
paras: Away from the troop and sitting high above the forest floor, these macaques take turns grooming one another. They search through each other's fur, trying to find ticks, lice, and anything crawly that shouldn't be there.

Grooming has two very important benefits. Firstly, it is quality social time, not unlike the time people spend together talking over coffee. The grooming helps to strengthen bonds between individuals and in turn this helps these very social animals maintain a sense of community.

Secondly, grooming is very important for the health of these macaques. A friend can remove nasty ticks and biting insects which they wouldn't normally be able to reach by themselves. Too many parasites on one monkey can lower the immune system and even cause disease and infection.

But what does the groomer get out of all of this hard work? What’s in it for him? Well, it seems, this habitual grooming does come with a reward of sorts after all; A big juicy one, in fact. You see, each parasite removed becomes a tasty snack for the grooming macaque to enjoy!
questions: What do the macaques look for while grooming?
How does grooming help macaques socially?
What reward does the groomer get?
12.translation: 遠離猴群、坐在森林地面上方高處的這些獼猴，輪流為彼此理毛。牠們在彼此的毛髮中搜尋，試著找出蜱蟲、蝨子，以及任何不該在那裡爬來爬去的東西。理毛有兩個非常重要的好處。第一，這是有品質的社交時間，就像人們一起喝咖啡聊天一樣。理毛能幫助個體之間加強連結，進而幫助這些非常社會化的動物維持群體意識。第二，理毛對這些獼猴的健康非常重要。朋友可以幫忙除去牠們自己平常碰不到的討厭蜱蟲和會咬人的昆蟲。一隻猴子身上的寄生蟲太多，可能會降低免疫系統，甚至造成疾病和感染。不過，替別人理毛的猴子從這些辛苦工作中得到什麼呢？牠有什麼好處？嗯，看來這種習慣性的理毛終究還是有某種獎賞，而且其實是又大又多汁的獎賞。你看，每一隻被除下來的寄生蟲，都會變成理毛獼猴可以享用的美味點心！
answers: They look for ticks, lice, and other crawly things.
It strengthens bonds and helps them maintain a sense of community.
Each parasite removed becomes a tasty snack.

12.title: Sculpting
paras: What may appear to be an ordinary block of wood to you, is much, much more to these men. They are sculptors and, to them, a block of wood is a work of art waiting to be revealed to the world.

In order to make their visions a reality, these sculptors will use a variety of tools. In the picture on the left, the sculptor uses a mallet and chisel to carve a beautiful fish dancing on the water's surface out of a block of wood. With these tools, he gradually chips away at the wood, slowly revealing the hidden beauty within.

In the picture on the right, the sculptor is working on a depiction of a big-horned sheep. His detailed work shows a ram climbing up a mountainous, rocky cliff. For some of the more intricate details he uses a pneumatic (air) rotary-tool, which makes working in hard to reach areas much easier. Notice the orange airline that feeds air to the tool.

Starting out as nothing more than plain looking chunks of wood, these sculptors have removed the outer layers, revealing truly amazing works of art within. Perhaps you will you never look at a block a wood in the same way again! Maybe you, too, will see the hidden beauty that beckons to be feed.
questions: What do sculptors see in a block of wood?
What tools does the sculptor use to carve the fish?
Why does the sculptor use a pneumatic rotary-tool?
12.translation: 對你來說看起來只是普通木塊的東西，對這些人而言卻有更多更多的意義。他們是雕刻家，對他們來說，一塊木頭就是等待向世界展現的藝術品。為了讓他們的想像成真，這些雕刻家會使用各式各樣的工具。在左邊的圖片中，雕刻家用木槌和鑿子，從一塊木頭中雕出一條美麗的魚，像是在水面上舞動。透過這些工具，他逐漸鑿掉木頭，一點一點顯露其中隱藏的美。在右邊的圖片中，雕刻家正在製作一件大角羊的作品。他精細的作品呈現一隻公羊攀爬在多山、多岩石的峭壁上。為了處理一些更複雜精細的細節，他使用氣動旋轉工具，這讓在難以觸及的地方工作容易許多。注意那條橘色的空氣管，它把空氣送到工具中。這些作品一開始不過是看起來平凡的木塊，但雕刻家去除了外層，展現出其中真正令人驚嘆的藝術品。也許你再也不會用同樣的眼光看待一塊木頭了！也許你也會看見那呼喚著要被釋放的隱藏之美。
answers: They see a work of art waiting to be revealed.
He uses a mallet and chisel.
He uses it for intricate details and hard-to-reach areas.

12.title: Gondolas
paras: Trucks, cars and buses are not always the most practical way to transport people from point A to point B. This is particularly true when point B is hundreds of meters up the side of a steep mountain. In such cases gondolas are much better.
Gondolas use heavy cables strung between poles to carry cars up and down mountains. Usually they run high above the tree line, giving you a bird's-eye-view of the surroundings.

In the picture on the left, we can see passengers who have purchased tickets and are waiting to board the gondola running between Taipei Zoo and Maokong. After a long wait in line, the passengers are excited as they are finally preparing to board the gondola. The doors of the car open automatically. Once on, the doors close again and the car swings into motion.

As you exit the terminal, the floor quickly drops away as does your stomach. Now all that is below you are trees and plenty of air as you climb steadily, onward and upward. It is very picturesque. You can also see other cars suspended in front of you.

From this viewpoint, the world seems quite amazing. It is a rare opportunity to experience things dangling from such heights. In reality, our world is very two dimensional (other than climbing a flight of stares). The vertical is something that maybe only pilots and birds really navigate. If only for a moment, taking a ride on a gondola can give us a new perspective on the world around us.
questions: When are gondolas especially useful?
What view do gondolas usually give passengers?
How can riding a gondola change our perspective?
12.translation: 卡車、汽車和公車並不總是把人從 A 點運送到 B 點最實用的方法。當 B 點位在陡峭山坡上數百公尺高的地方時，這一點尤其明顯。在這種情況下，纜車就好得多。纜車使用架在柱子之間的粗重纜線，載著車廂上下山。通常它們會行駛在樹梢線上方，讓你能鳥瞰周遭景色。在左邊的圖片中，我們可以看到已買票、正在等待搭乘台北動物園到貓空之間纜車的乘客。排了很久的隊後，乘客終於準備上纜車，感到很興奮。車廂門會自動打開。一上車後，門又關上，車廂開始擺動前進。當你離開車站時，腳下的地面迅速遠離，你的胃也像跟著往下掉。現在你下方只剩樹木和大量空氣，而你穩定地向前、向上攀升。景色非常如畫。你也可以看見其他車廂懸掛在前方。從這個視角看，世界似乎相當奇妙。從這麼高的地方懸在空中體驗事物，是難得的機會。事實上，我們的世界非常平面化（除了爬一段樓梯之外）。垂直方向或許只有飛行員和鳥類真正能自在穿梭。即使只有片刻，搭乘纜車也能讓我們用新的視角看待周遭的世界。
answers: They are useful when the destination is high up a steep mountain.
They give passengers a bird’s-eye-view of the surroundings.
It lets us see the world from high above and gives us a new perspective.

12.title: Jack-o'-lanterns
paras: Look at all the scary faces on the pumpkins. It must be Halloween. Making jack-o'-lanterns is a traditional activity this time of year. People use them to decorate their windows and front porches so that their house looks spooky.

Making a jack-o'-lantern is quite simple and fun. All that is needed is a knife, a spoon and a candle. Start by cutting a lid out of the top. Cut it at an angle so that the lid will sit on the top and not fall in. Next, use your hands or a spoon to scoop out the seeds and guts of the pumpkin. When the inside is clean, you are ready to carve the face.
Before cutting the face, first draw it on with a marker. This makes it easier to cut as you can just follow the lines. Be careful with the knife so that you don't cut yourself.

The final step is to place a candle inside the hollowed out pumpkin. When the candle is lit, the eerie face will glow in the dark, casting an evil light on everything around. Place the jack-o'-lantern in a safe place where nothing will catch on fire. Also, blow the candle out before you go to bed or leave the house.
When Halloween is over, don't forget to throw the pumpkin away or it will rot, stink and attract flies. Or you could make pumpkin soup for your family and friends.
questions: Why do people make jack-o'-lanterns?
Why should the lid be cut at an angle?
What should you do before going to bed or leaving the house?
12.translation: 看看南瓜上那些嚇人的臉。一定是萬聖節到了。製作南瓜燈是每年這個時候的傳統活動。人們用它們裝飾窗戶和前門廊，讓房子看起來陰森可怕。製作南瓜燈其實很簡單，也很有趣。只需要一把刀、一支湯匙和一根蠟燭。先從頂端切出一個蓋子。切的時候要有角度，這樣蓋子才會坐在上方，不會掉進去。接著，用手或湯匙把南瓜籽和裡面的東西挖出來。當內部清乾淨後，就可以開始雕刻臉了。在切臉之前，先用麥克筆畫上去。這樣會比較容易切，因為你只要沿著線切就好。使用刀子時要小心，免得割傷自己。最後一步是把蠟燭放進挖空的南瓜裡。蠟燭點燃時，詭異的臉會在黑暗中發光，把邪惡的光投射到周圍的一切。把南瓜燈放在安全的地方，確保沒有東西會著火。另外，睡覺或離開家之前要把蠟燭吹熄。萬聖節結束後，別忘了把南瓜丟掉，否則它會腐爛、發臭並吸引蒼蠅。或者你也可以為家人和朋友做南瓜湯。
answers: They make them to decorate windows and porches so the house looks spooky.
So the lid will sit on top and not fall in.
You should blow the candle out.

13.title: Parent-Teacher Association
paras: The Sihu Senior High School Parent Teacher Association recently elected a new president. An inauguration ceremony was held to officially swear in the new president. The Chinese name for this ceremony is 交接典禮, which literally translated means passing-it-on. In other words, the old president passes the torch to the new one.

Members of the PTA signed an attendance sheet at the door. This is a formality that takes place before all PTA gatherings so that there is documentation of who is present. The secretary of the group, who is most likely a volunteer, usually looks after these records.

Once signed in, members were then asked to pin red ribbons to their shirts or jackets. Mr.Wu had difficulty attaching the pin to his lapel, so he asked Mary for assistance. She opened the lapel, carefully stuck the pin through it and then put the clasp on.

When the new president was formally sworn in, a handshake from the former president made it official. He was then presented with flowers and gifts from the PTA to congratulate him. To finish everything off, he gave a short speech to thank everyone for their support.
questions: What was the ceremony held for?
Why do members have to sign in?
Why did the PTA give him flowers?
13.translation: 四湖高中家長教師會最近選出了一位新會長。學校舉行就職典禮，正式讓新會長宣誓上任。這個典禮的中文名稱是「交接典禮」，字面意思就是把事情傳下去；換句話說，就是舊會長把責任交棒給新會長。家長教師會成員在門口簽到。這是所有會議前都會進行的正式程序，目的是留下出席紀錄。團體的秘書，通常可能是志工，會負責保管這些紀錄。簽到後，成員被要求把紅絲帶別在襯衫或外套上。吳先生不太會把別針別在翻領上，所以請瑪麗幫忙。她拉開翻領，小心地把別針穿過去，再扣上扣環。新會長正式宣誓就職時，前任會長與他握手，使交接正式完成。接著家長教師會送上花和禮物祝賀他。最後，他發表簡短演說，感謝大家的支持。
answers: It was held to officially swear in the PTA president.
They have to sign in so that there is documentation of who is present.
They gave him flowers to congratulate him.

13.title: Bending and Stretching
paras: Look at the children stretching. They bend and stretch to make themselves more flexible. Stretching before any exercise is a good idea. It reduces the chances of injury.

In dance class, flexibility is very important, so stretching is a must. The more flexible you are, the more dance moves you can do. The more dance moves you can do, the better a dancer you are. But it is also important to stretching exercises properly, or you could hurt yourself.

First, their teacher shows them how to do each stretching exercise. Then the students try it. She watches to see that they do it right. If a student is doing something wrong, the teacher can help him or her out.

In the stretching exercise in the picture, the students lie on their stomachs and try to lean back as far as they can while bending their legs at the knees. Some of the girls are so flexible that they can touch the back of their heads with their toes. The teacher helps one boy to stretch way back. Hopefully she doesn’t break the boy in half!
questions: Why do the children do stretches in dance class?
Why is it important to stretch properly?
What can the girls who are very flexible do?
13.translation: 看看孩子們正在伸展。他們彎曲並伸展身體，讓自己變得更柔軟。任何運動前伸展都是好主意，因為可以降低受傷的機率。在舞蹈課中，柔軟度非常重要，所以伸展是必要的。你越柔軟，就能做越多舞步；能做越多舞步，就越能成為好的舞者。不過，正確做伸展也很重要，否則可能會受傷。首先，老師示範每一個伸展動作，然後學生試著做。老師會觀察他們是否做對；如果有學生做錯，老師就能幫忙。照片中的伸展動作是學生趴著，膝蓋彎曲，並盡可能往後仰。有些女孩柔軟到可以用腳趾碰到後腦勺。老師正在幫一個男孩往後伸展。希望她不要把那個男孩折成兩半！
answers: They do it to make themselves more flexible.
It is important so that you don’t hurt yourself.
They can touch the back of their heads with their toes.

13.title: Buying Tree Decorations
paras: I get very excited every year when it is time to set up and decorate the Christmas tree. This year was even more special because Mom decided to throw out the old tree and buy a new one. We had been using that old tree for more than five years and it was starting to look a little worn-out.

My parents don't believe in using real trees. They think it is very wasteful to chop down a healthy tree that is just going to be used for a few days and then be thrown out. That is why we have an artificial one. If you ask me, they look just as good anyway.

The store we went to had lots of Christmas decorations. I swear that they had enough tinsel and garland to make Jay Chou jealous. There were hundreds of tree ornaments there, as well. Some of them were the pretty glass ones that I like, but Mom opted for the cheap plastic kind because my cat would probably just break anything that was too fragile anyway.

Most of the ornaments came in packs of six or eight. I wanted to get blue and yellow ones, but my mom wanted green and red. Because we couldn’t agree on the colors for the tree, we picked up 4 variety packs.

We didn't have to buy new lights this year, but we still looked at them anyway. I wished that we would buy new ones so that I wouldn’t have to untangle the old strands like I do every year. And ours don't blink either, like some of the new fancier ones that make the tree look like it is sparkling. Oh well, maybe next year.
questions: Why did they throw out the old tree?
What couldn’t the girl and her mother agree on?
Why did the girl want new strands of lights?
13.translation: 每年到了架設和裝飾聖誕樹的時候，我都非常興奮。今年更特別，因為媽媽決定丟掉舊樹，買一棵新的。我們那棵舊樹已經用了五年以上，開始看起來有點破舊。我的父母不贊成使用真正的樹。他們覺得砍下一棵健康的樹，只用幾天就丟掉，非常浪費。所以我們用的是人造聖誕樹。要我說，它們看起來也一樣好。那家店有很多聖誕裝飾。我敢說那裡的金銀絲和彩帶多到會讓周杰倫嫉妒。店裡也有上百種聖誕樹裝飾品。有些是我喜歡的漂亮玻璃裝飾，但媽媽選了便宜的塑膠款，因為我的貓大概會把太易碎的東西打破。大多數裝飾品是六個或八個一包。我想買藍色和黃色的，但媽媽想買綠色和紅色。因為我們無法決定聖誕樹的顏色，所以買了四包綜合包。今年我們不必買新的燈串，但還是看了一下。我希望我們會買新的，這樣我就不用像每年一樣解開舊燈串。舊燈串也不會閃爍，不像一些新的漂亮燈串，會讓樹看起來閃閃發光。算了，也許明年吧。
answers: They threw it out because it was worn out.
They couldn’t agree on what color of ornaments to buy.
She wanted new strands of lights so that she wouldn’t have to untangle the old ones.

13.title: Opening Ceremony
paras: Yesterday there was a track meet at my school. I didn't take part in any of the events or the opening ceremonies, but I still enjoyed watching everything. Even though I am not really into track and field, at least I didn’t have to go to class for the day.

The opening ceremony seemed quite official. It was almost like watching a real Olympic ceremony. A flag bearer led each class’s athletes onto the field. The procession marched once around the track, smiling and waving to the crowd.

After one loop around the track, each class lined up behind their respective leaders and flag bearers. It was all done in a very militant style. I was surprised to see that even some of my unruly classmates were capable of standing so still and being so quiet for more than ten seconds.

Once everyone was standing in place, six cheerleaders ushered out the school flag. They marched once around the track in their knee-high boots and short skirts. They must have done their job because the whole crowd cheered as they passed.

All in all, it was pretty impressive that a bunch of my schoolmates could put together such a wonderful opening ceremony. Maybe they are more organized than they seem. They certainly did a better job than I would have believed.
questions: Who led each class onto the field?
Why was it surprising that some of the students were able to stand so still?
Who ushered out the school flag?
13.translation: 昨天我的學校舉行田徑運動會。我沒有參加任何比賽或開幕典禮，但我仍然很享受觀看一切。雖然我不是很喜歡田徑，至少那天不用上課。開幕典禮看起來相當正式，幾乎像在看真正的奧運典禮。每班的掌旗手帶領運動員進入場地。隊伍繞著跑道走一圈，微笑並向觀眾揮手。繞完一圈後，每個班級都排在各自的領隊和掌旗手後方。整個過程非常有軍事化風格。我很驚訝看到連一些平常難管教的同學，也能站得這麼直、安靜超過十秒。大家站定後，六位啦啦隊員引領校旗出場。她們穿著及膝長靴和短裙，繞跑道走了一圈。她們一定做得很好，因為經過時全場都在歡呼。總的來說，一群我的同學能合力完成如此精彩的開幕典禮，真的令人印象深刻。也許他們比看起來更有組織。他們的表現確實比我原本相信的還要好。
answers: A flag bearer led each class onto the field.
It was surprising because some of them are usually unruly.
Cheerleaders ushered out the school flag.

13.title: Pumps in the Park
paras: Jack and Jill are playing with the pumps in the park. Not so long ago, everyone had to use pumps to get water out of the ground. This was before electric pumps.

I can remember filling the water trough for Grandma's cows by hand. It took a long time to fill the very large trough. It had to be done two times a day because the cows drank a lot. If it was really hot out, then it had to be done even more often because the cows would drink even more water. It was hard work, but it made me a strong boy.

When my mother was a girl, her and her family had to pump their own water for everything. If you wanted to take a drink you had to pump. If you wanted to wash your clothes, you had to pump. If you wanted to take a bath, you had to pump. Not to mention all the water they had to pump and haul by hand for the farm animals. Pump, pump, pump, all day long. Imagine having to pump water every time you turn on the tap!

Jack and Jill find it fun to pull on the long handle and to watch the water come out of the spout. It splashes on the ground as they giggle and dance about. They are having the time of their lives, but I am sure that it would not be so much fun if they had to do it every day like my mother’s family did.
questions: What are the kids doing in the park?
How often did the author have to fill the cows’ trough?
What did the author’s mother have to pump water for?

vocab.w: have the time of one’s life

13.translation: 傑克和吉兒正在公園裡玩抽水幫浦。不久以前，每個人都必須用幫浦把地下水抽出來，那是在電動抽水機出現以前。我還記得曾經用手幫奶奶的牛把水槽裝滿。那個很大的水槽要裝滿需要很久。因為牛喝很多水，所以一天必須裝兩次。如果天氣很熱，還得更常裝，因為牛會喝更多水。那是辛苦的工作，但也讓我成為強壯的男孩。當我媽媽還是女孩時，她和家人所有用水都得自己抽。想喝水，要抽水；想洗衣服，要抽水；想洗澡，也要抽水。更不用說他們還得為農場動物抽水，並用手搬運。整天就是抽啊、抽啊、抽啊。想像一下，每次打開水龍頭前都得先抽水！傑克和吉兒覺得拉長把手、看水從出水口流出來很好玩。水濺到地上時，他們咯咯笑著跳來跳去。他們玩得非常開心，但我敢肯定，如果他們必須像我媽媽一家人那樣每天做，這就不會那麼有趣了。
answers: They are playing with the water pumps.
He had to fill it twice a day.
She had to pump water for drinking, bathing, cooking and washing the laundry.

13.title: Skating on the Pond
paras: This is my friend's son. His name is Brendan. Brendan is 3 years old. He has very blonde hair that is almost white. He always smiles and has very good manners. He is a good boy.
Brendan loves to go ice skating. It is his favorite thing to do in the winter. Sometimes he skates at the hockey arena, but usually he skates on a pond near his home.

Before he goes out, his mother always dresses him in winter clothes. They help to keep him warm even when it is cold outside. First, she puts long underwear and an undershirt on him. Then she puts on his pants and a warm sweater. Next she puts his ski pants and a winter jacket over the other clothes. Finally, she wraps a scarf around his neck, puts a toque on his head and pulls on his mittens. He is now ready to go.

The skates are put on when they get to the pond, as is the helmet. Brendan is too young to put his skates on by himself, so his father usually does it. He wears a helmet to protect his head incase he falls down.

Brendan can skate for hours without stopping. His mom always sits and watches him while she drinks hot coffee and talks to the other moms. She also brings a thermos of hot chocolate along for Brendan to drink when he takes a break.

When Brendan is finally cold and tired they head back home. After a day of skating, he always likes to eat a hot bowl of tomato soup to warm up. Then he often falls asleep on the couch watching cartoons. The fresh air and exercise is very good for a growing young boy.
questions: Where does Brendan usually skate?
What does his mother do before he goes out?
How long can Brendan skate for?
13.translation: 這是我朋友的兒子。他叫布蘭登，三歲。他有一頭幾乎像白色的金髮。他總是微笑，也很有禮貌，是個好孩子。布蘭登喜歡溜冰，這是他冬天最愛做的事。有時他在冰球場溜冰，但通常是在家附近的池塘上溜。在出門前，他媽媽總是幫他穿上冬天的衣服。即使外面很冷，這些衣服也能讓他保持溫暖。首先，她幫他穿上保暖內衣和內衣。接著穿上褲子和暖和的毛衣。然後再把滑雪褲和冬季外套穿在其他衣服外面。最後，她把圍巾圍在他的脖子上，幫他戴上毛帽，再套上連指手套。他現在準備好了。到池塘後才穿溜冰鞋，也才戴上安全帽。布蘭登太小，還不能自己穿溜冰鞋，所以通常由爸爸幫他。他戴安全帽是為了跌倒時保護頭部。布蘭登可以不停地溜好幾個小時。媽媽總是坐在旁邊看著他，一邊喝熱咖啡，一邊和其他媽媽聊天。她也會帶一瓶熱巧克力，讓布蘭登休息時喝。當布蘭登終於又冷又累時，他們就回家。溜了一天冰後，他總喜歡吃一碗熱番茄湯取暖。接著他常常在沙發上一邊看卡通一邊睡著。新鮮空氣和運動對成長中的小男孩非常有益。
answers: He usually skates on a pond near his home.
She dresses him in winter clothes.
He can skate for hours.

13.title: The Balance Beam
paras: This PE class is learning how to use the balance beam. A balance beam is nothing more than long square pole that runs horizontally above the ground. Young gymnasts who are just learning use a beam that is only a foot or two off the ground. Competitive gymnasts are required to use a beam that it much higher. Regardless of its height, it still takes a lot of practice to maintain your balance on the narrow apparatus.

In gymnastic competitions, such as the Olympics, gymnasts perform choreographed routines on the balance beam. Scores are calculated by several judges according to degree of difficulty, artistic merit and execution of the routine. This means that the more difficult and appealing the routine, it will receive higher scores than a simple routine as long as there are no mistakes. The judging system encourages gymnasts to push their limits and strive for perfection.

In the above picture, little Susan is practicing on the balance beam. She slowly places one foot in front of the other, keeping her arms spread out to help maintain her balance. If she falls, it is not far to the ground so she doesn't have to worry about getting hurt.

The other girls in the class are waiting patiently in line for their turn. For now, they are just practicing walking back and forth trying not to fall off. Once they have learned to this without falling off and have gained some confidence, their teacher will teach them increasingly more difficult moves. When they have mastered the fundamentals, they may be ready to learn a choreographed routine and take part in a small competition.
questions: Why do beginners use a balance beam that is close to the ground?
What are the three criteria that balance beam gymnasts are judged on?
Why does Susan keep her arms spread out?
13.translation: 這堂體育課正在學習如何使用平衡木。平衡木不過是一根長長的方形木桿，水平地架在地面上方。剛開始學習的年輕體操選手會使用離地只有一兩英尺高的平衡木。競賽體操選手則必須使用高得多的平衡木。無論高度如何，要在狹窄的器械上保持平衡，都需要大量練習。在奧運等體操比賽中，體操選手會在平衡木上表演編排好的動作。分數由幾位裁判依照難度、藝術表現和動作完成度來計算。這表示只要沒有失誤，越困難、越吸引人的動作會比簡單動作得到更高分。評分制度鼓勵體操選手挑戰極限，追求完美。在上方圖片中，小蘇珊正在平衡木上練習。她慢慢地一腳接一腳往前放，雙臂張開幫助保持平衡。如果她跌倒，離地面不遠，所以不用擔心受傷。班上其他女孩正耐心排隊等候輪到自己。現在她們只是練習來回走，努力不要掉下來。等她們學會不掉下來並建立信心後，老師會教更困難的動作。當她們掌握基本功後，也許就可以學習一套編排好的動作，參加小型比賽。
answers: They do this so that they don’t get hurt if they fall off.
They are degree of difficulty, artistic merit and execution.
She does this to help maintain her balance.

13.title: The Wedding Procession
paras: When people get married, there is always a lot of decorating involved. The place where the wedding is held usually gets the most attention, but even the cars that the wedding party travels around in are adorned with colorful bows, ribbons and sometimes flowers. It isn’t hard to spot a wedding procession on the street; just look for the cars that look like they are wrapped up as gifts.

This particular car is for the bride and groom. It has a red bow on each of the door handles, long red ribbons running overtop the hood all the way to the trunk and a heart made from plastic roses on the grill. Other cars in the procession may or may not be decorated quite as extravagantly, but they will usually have at least some decorations to show that they are involved in the wedding.

In some areas of the world, it is customary for the wedding procession to drive through the streets honking the horn to garner attention. In smaller towns, people may run to the window or go outside to wave as the cars pass, but in larger centers they probably will only get an occasional glimpse if anything at all.

Chances are that one day you will ride in a car decorated this, whether you are getting married or maybe just a part of the wedding party. Maybe you will get to help decorate one of the cars, too. Who knows?
questions: What are some common wedding decorations for cars?
What is the group of cars called that the wedding party travel in called?
Why do some wedding processions honk their horns while they drive through the streets?
13.translation: 人們結婚時，總會有很多裝飾。舉行婚禮的地方通常最受注意，但連婚禮隊伍搭乘的車子，也會用彩色蝴蝶結、緞帶，有時還有花來裝飾。在街上要認出婚禮車隊並不難；只要找那些看起來像禮物包裝一樣的車子就可以。這輛特別的車是給新郎和新娘坐的。每個車門把手上都有紅色蝴蝶結，長長的紅緞帶從引擎蓋一路延伸到後車廂，水箱護罩上還有一個用塑膠玫瑰做成的愛心。車隊中的其他車可能不會裝飾得這麼華麗，但通常至少會有一些裝飾，表示它們參與了婚禮。在世界某些地區，婚禮車隊開過街道時按喇叭吸引注意是一種習俗。在小城鎮，人們可能會跑到窗邊或到外面揮手；但在大城市，如果有注意到，也可能只是偶然瞥見而已。也許有一天你也會坐在這樣裝飾的車裡，不管是你自己結婚，或只是婚禮隊伍的一員。也許你還會幫忙裝飾其中一輛車。誰知道呢？
answers: Some common decorations are bows, ribbons, and flowers.
It is called the wedding procession.
They honk their horns to attract attention.

13.title: Valentine’s Day
paras: Valentine's Day is a very special day for those of you who are romantic at heart. It's a time to show that special someone just how much you really care. It can also be a time to admit your feelings to someone for the first time.

Although some men may beg to differ, Valentine's Day is mostly for women and stores. It is the day when women want to be treated like queen or, at the very least, princesses. And remember that your queen or princess WILL be expecting something from you, so don’t forget to mark February 14th on your calendar…unless you enjoy the feeling guilty and being in the dog house. Phil is a very romantic young man. Today on his way home from school, he stopped by the local florist to pick up a bouquet of flowers for his sweetheart. Although his face doesn't show it, he cares deeply for Jill and he tells her this as he hands her the flowers. She blushes and accepts the token of his love.
Surprisingly, Jill actually has feelings for the stone-faced boy. She has even picked out a gift for him, too. It is a heart-shaped box of chocolates.
Phil is shocked that she cares about him. He can’t believe that the girl of his dreams likes him enough to buy him a gift. He is so overcome with joy that he begins to sob uncontrollably.
Jill is quite taken aback with his crying. Perhaps her feelings are not as strong as his overwhelming adoration of her. In a panic, she drops the flowers and rushes to the door.
Poor Phil can only watch as the door closes and his sweetheart exits his life forever. He drops to his knees holding his hands toward the skies. “WHY?!!” he blubbers. “WHY?!!”
Five minutes later Jill is on the next bus out of town.
questions: Why should a man never forget Valentine’s Day?
Why can’t we tell how Phil feels by looking at his face?
Does Jill like Phil as much as he likes her?
13.translation: 情人節對內心浪漫的人來說是非常特別的一天。這是向那個特別的人表達你有多在乎的時候，也可能是第一次向某人承認感情的時候。雖然有些男人可能不同意，但情人節大多是為女人和商店而存在的。這一天，女人希望被當成女王，至少也要像公主一樣被對待。請記得，你的女王或公主一定會期待你有所表示，所以別忘了在日曆上標記二月十四日，除非你喜歡內疚並惹她生氣。菲爾是一位非常浪漫的年輕人。今天放學回家途中，他到當地花店買了一束花給心上人。雖然他的臉看不出來，但他深深在乎吉兒，並在把花交給她時告訴她。她臉紅了，接受了這份愛的信物。令人意外的是，吉兒其實也喜歡這個面無表情的男孩。她也為他挑了一份禮物，是一盒心形巧克力。菲爾很震驚她竟然在乎他。他不敢相信夢中女孩竟然喜歡他到買禮物給他。他高興得無法控制，開始啜泣。吉兒被他的哭泣嚇了一跳。也許她的感情沒有他對她那種強烈愛慕那麼深。她慌張地丟下花，衝向門口。可憐的菲爾只能看著門關上，心上人永遠離開他的生命。他跪倒在地，雙手伸向天空。「為什麼？！」他哭喊著。「為什麼？！」五分鐘後，吉兒已經坐上下一班離開城鎮的公車。
answers: He should never forget because his wife or girlfriend would be very, very angry.
We can’t tell how he feels because his face is emotionless.
No, he likes her a lot more.

13.title: Wedding Photos
paras: It looks like somebody forgot to send out the wedding invitations! There isn't even a priest. And where are their parents? I'm no legal expert, but I think you at least need a justice of the peace and two witnesses to make a marriage legal.

Actually, the bride and groom are having their wedding photos taken. In Taiwan it is common to have professional wedding photos done prior to the wedding, sometimes weeks in advance. It takes some of the stress out of an already pressure packed wedding day when everyone is a little on the tense side. The time saved by having the photos done ahead of time can be better utilized mingling with guests and making the day more memorable rather than standing around waiting for someone to take your picture.

Another advantage of shooting the pictures in advance is that the photos can be developed and printed in time for the wedding. This is a big plus because the bride and groom can hand out the photos at the wedding instead of having to mail them out to everyone.

Scenic outdoor locations, such as this picturesque park, are often chosen for shoots. The natural beauty of areas like this provides a wonderful backdrop for pictures of any kind; though, in my opinion, they could lose the cheesy balloons.
questions: When are wedding photos taken?
What are two advantages of taking the photos in advance?
Why are scenic outdoor locations often chosen for wedding photos?
13.translation: 看起來好像有人忘了寄婚禮邀請函！現場甚至沒有神父。他們的父母在哪裡？我不是法律專家，但我想至少需要一位公證人和兩位見證人，婚姻才算合法。其實，新郎和新娘正在拍婚紗照。在台灣，婚禮前先拍專業婚紗照很常見，有時甚至提前好幾週。這能減少原本就充滿壓力的婚禮當天的負擔，因為那天每個人都會有點緊張。提前拍照省下的時間，可以更好地用來與賓客互動，讓那一天更難忘，而不是站著等人拍照。提前拍照的另一個好處是，照片能趕在婚禮前沖洗和印好。這是一大優點，因為新郎新娘可以在婚禮上把照片送給大家，而不必之後再寄給每個人。風景優美的戶外地點，例如這座美麗的公園，常被選來拍照。這些地方的自然美景能為任何照片提供很棒的背景；不過依我看，他們可以不要那些俗氣的氣球。
answers: They are taken prior to the wedding.
It takes some stress out of the wedding day and the photos can be handed out at the wedding.
They are chosen because the natural beauty provides a wonderful backdrop.

14.title: Sky Lanterns
paras: This man is writing a sort of prayer on a sky lantern. He writes things that he hopes will bring health and prosperity to him and his family. To make it easier for him to write the characters, his friend holds the balloon up. He uses white paint to really make the words stand out on the red balloon.

Making sky lanterns is sometimes done on a deity's birthday. It is thought that messages floating high in the sky will be read by the gods. If the deity is pleased, then good luck will be bestowed on the family.

The lanterns are made of very lightweight materials. Candles heat the air inside the balloons to make them rise into the air when released, similar to a hot-air balloon. The higher the balloons rise, the better because they are more likely to be seen by a deity. This also increases the chance of a wish being granted.

Whether or not you believe in such things, it is still fun to participate. You get to make a cool lantern and watch as it disappears high into the night sky. So the next you hear about an activity like this going on in your community, go out and get involved. You never know…maybe your prayer will be answered.
questions: What do people write on the sides of sky lanterns?
Why are lanterns made of lightweight materials?
What heats the air inside the lantern?
14.translation: 這名男子正在天燈上寫一種祈禱。他寫下希望能為自己和家人帶來健康與興旺的話。為了讓他更容易寫字，他的朋友把天燈撐起來。他用白色顏料，讓文字在紅色天燈上特別醒目。有時人們會在神明生日製作天燈。人們相信，飄到高空中的訊息會被神明讀到。如果神明高興，好運就會降臨到這個家庭。天燈是用非常輕的材料製成的。蠟燭加熱天燈裡的空氣，讓它被放開時升上天空，類似熱氣球。天燈飛得越高越好，因為越可能被神明看見，也提高願望實現的機會。不管你相不相信這些事，參與仍然很有趣。你可以做一盞很酷的天燈，看著它消失在夜空高處。所以下次你聽說社區有這樣的活動，就出去參加吧。誰知道呢，也許你的祈禱會得到回應。
answers: They write prayers or wishes that will hopefully bring health and prosperity.
They are made of lightweight materials so that they will rise into the air when released.
A candle heats the air inside the lantern.

14.title: Ring toss
paras: Ring toss is a popular game at many night markets and fairs in Taiwan. It is a game that is simple enough to play, but difficult to master. Many people at night markets line up just for a chance to win a prize or to challenge a friend to a little competition. The odds of winning some sort cheap prize is pretty good, too.

To play the game, you first need to pay a small fee and get some rings from the attendant. Once you have the rings, you will need to find a place to stand where it isn’t too crowded—good luck with that—so that you have some room to move your arms and aren’t getting constantly bumped into. Now the object of the game is to toss a ring and get it to land on one of the prizes and stay there. If it does land and stay, you win the prize. If it doesn’t, you can only try again. It is probably best to focus on one target instead of just throwing the rings randomly. You can then hone in on it until you win it or until you run out of money…whatever comes first.

The prizes are set up on boards arranged in a step-like fashion. The prizes that are closest to you on the lower boards are usually little trinkets, toys, key chains or even drinks. The prizes seem to become more and more expensive, and desirable, the further away they become. Usually, the best prizes are way in the back and the odds of winning one are nearly impossible. Though most of the prizes are very cheap—you can probably buy them for about the same price as playing the game or less—it is still fun to play. But whether you win or lose, at least you've had a good time.
questions: How do you win at ring-toss?
Where are the more expensive prizes?
Why are the more desirable items further away?
14.translation: 套圈圈是台灣許多夜市和市集裡很受歡迎的遊戲。這個遊戲玩起來很簡單，但要精通卻很難。夜市裡有很多人排隊，只為了有機會贏得獎品，或和朋友來一場小比賽。贏得某種便宜獎品的機率也還不錯。要玩這個遊戲，首先要付一小筆費用，向工作人員拿一些圈圈。拿到圈圈後，你需要找個不太擁擠的地方站好，祝你好運，這樣你才有空間移動手臂，也不會一直被撞到。遊戲目標是把圈圈丟出去，讓它落在某個獎品上並停住。如果真的套住並停住，你就贏得那個獎品。如果沒有，就只能再試一次。最好專注在一個目標上，而不是隨便亂丟。你可以慢慢瞄準它，直到贏到它，或直到錢花光，看哪個先發生。獎品被放在像階梯一樣排列的板子上。離你最近、較低板子上的獎品通常是小飾品、玩具、鑰匙圈，甚至飲料。獎品越遠，看起來越貴也越令人想要。通常最好的獎品都在最後面，幾乎不可能套中。雖然大多數獎品很便宜，可能花跟玩遊戲差不多甚至更少的錢就買得到，但玩起來仍然有趣。無論輸贏，至少你玩得很開心。
answers: You have to land the ring on a prize and have it stay there.
They are located way in the back.
Because it makes them harder to win.

14.title: Sugarcane
paras: The old lady in this picture is peeling a piece of sugarcane. Holding the sugarcane firmly in her left hand, she uses a peeler to carefully remove the outside layer which is tough and inedible like the bark on a tree. She uses long even strokes to take off only a thin layer so that she doesn’t waste too much of the cane underneath. It is kind of like peeling an apple, as you don’t want to peel off a thick layer or it is just wasteful.

Now she is packaging the sugarcane for sale. She puts one end of the sugarcane into a plastic bag and then cuts it off. By cutting it into sections this way, she can be sure that the pieces aren’t too long. The bags are then tied up and ready to be bought. You can see lots of sugarcane behind her, waiting to be peeled. I wonder how long it will take her to peel and package all of them?

These boys are chewing on sugarcane. They can’t actually eat the sugarcane because it is too fibrous. Instead they only chew it and try to suck out the sweet juice. Then spit out the remaining woody pulp.
If you look closely, you can see some rings around the sugarcane. These parts are particularly tough to chew, so most people just bite them off and discard them.
The boys are having fun chewing on the sugarcane and spitting out the pulp on the table. It looks like they are to see who can make the biggest pile of pulp.
The boy on the left is leaning over the table, trying to get all the little slivers of pulp out of his mouth. He is laughs while he does this.
The boy on the right is waiting for his turn to spit out the pulp. He has a spent piece of sugarcane hanging out of his mouth.
questions: Why does the lady have to peel the sugarcane?
Why does she cut the pieces of cane into shorter pieces?
Can you really eat sugarcane?
14.translation: 照片中的老婦人正在削一段甘蔗。她用左手牢牢握住甘蔗，用削皮器小心地去除外層。外層像樹皮一樣堅硬，不能食用。她用長而均勻的動作，只削掉薄薄一層，避免浪費下面太多甘蔗。這有點像削蘋果，因為你不想削掉太厚的一層，那樣很浪費。現在她正在把甘蔗包裝起來販售。她把甘蔗一端放進塑膠袋裡，然後切斷。用這種方式切成段，她可以確保每段不會太長。袋子綁好後就可以販售了。你可以看到她身後有很多甘蔗，等著被削皮。我想知道她要花多久才能全部削完並包裝好？這些男孩正在嚼甘蔗。他們其實不能真正吃下甘蔗，因為它纖維太多。他們只是咀嚼，想吸出甜甜的汁液，然後吐掉剩下的木質纖維渣。如果你仔細看，可以看到甘蔗上有一些節。這些部分特別難嚼，所以大多數人會把它們咬掉丟棄。男孩們一邊嚼甘蔗，一邊把渣吐在桌上，玩得很開心。看起來他們像是在比誰能堆出最大堆的甘蔗渣。左邊的男孩正趴向桌子，試著把嘴裡的小渣屑都弄出來。他一邊做一邊笑。右邊的男孩正等著輪到他吐渣。他嘴裡還掛著一段嚼過的甘蔗。
answers: She has to peel it because the outside layer (skin) is inedible.
She does this so that they will fit in the bag.
No, it is too fibrous so you can only chew on it and suck out the juice.

14.title: A Letter to the Buddh
paras: Dear Buddha,

How are you? I am not so good. It is winter. The temperature has dropped almost 20 degrees in the last month. Everyone is bundling up before going outside. Scarves and toques have become a popular fashion accessory for most. Only teenage boys and the foolish dare to go out without a jacket on.

I don't get out much when the weather turns cold like this. I hate winter. Even with a heavy winter coat on, I still get cold so easily. At night I sleep with my clothes on and four blankets so that I don't get a chill. On really cold days I even wear mitts to bed.

Yesterday was so cold I thought I was going to die. I had the shivers something awful. My teeth chattered together so much that it gave me a headache. I spent the entire day crouched in front of a portable heater. It kept me warm enough to survive, but gave me terrible sunburn from sitting so close to it.

Later that evening, I thought a hot bath might warm me up. I had set the heater on the edge of the tub before getting in. As I got in, I tripped on the cord and fell into the tub. At the same time, the heater fell in with me—electricity plus water equals a new hairdo.

As I write this letter from my hospital bed, I can only hope that you can help me, Buddha. Please turn the earth's thermostat up about 20 degrees or I fear that I might not live long enough to see the flowers of spring. Thank you for any help that you can provide.
Yours truly,
Luke
questions: Who stills goes outside without a jacket?
Why doesn’t Luke like the cold?
What did Luke do to try and warm up?
14.translation: 親愛的佛祖：您好嗎？我不太好。現在是冬天。過去一個月氣溫下降了將近二十度。每個人出門前都把自己裹得很暖。圍巾和毛帽成了大多數人的流行配件。只有青少年男孩和傻瓜才敢不穿外套出門。天氣變得這麼冷時，我不太出門。我討厭冬天。即使穿著厚重冬季外套，我還是很容易覺得冷。晚上我穿著衣服、蓋四條毯子睡覺，免得著涼。在真的很冷的日子，我甚至戴著手套睡覺。昨天冷到我以為自己要死了。我冷得發抖得很厲害，牙齒一直打顫，打到頭痛。我整天蹲在可攜式暖爐前。它讓我暖到足以活下來，但因為坐得太近，也讓我嚴重曬傷。那天晚上晚些時候，我想洗個熱水澡也許能讓我暖起來。進浴缸前，我把暖爐放在浴缸邊緣。當我進去時，被電線絆倒，跌進浴缸。同時，暖爐也和我一起掉了進去，電加水等於一個新髮型。現在我在病床上寫這封信，只能希望佛祖您能幫助我。請把地球的恆溫器調高大約二十度，否則我怕自己活不到看見春天花開。感謝您能提供的任何幫助。敬上，路克。
answers: Teenage boys and the foolish still go outside without a jacket on.
He doesn’t like it because he gets cold easily.
He sat in front of an electric heater to try and warm up.

14.title: A Funny Story about Chopstics
paras: It was my first day in Taiwan. My stomach was making grumbling sounds as I walked down the street. I was so hungry that I would've eaten anything, but I couldn’t find anything that looked like a restaurant to me. Everything was so different. What would I do?

A man saw me holding my stomach and walking around like I was lost. He stopped me and asked if there was a problem. I was so happy that he spoke English that I didn't respond for a minute. I could only shake his hand and thank him for stopping.

I told him that I was from another country and didn't know where or what to eat. He pointed to a street down the road and told me that I would find many places to eat there. Then on a scrap of paper that he pulled from his pocket, he scribbled a couple of Chinese characters and handed it to me. Just show this to the cook he told me and I would be fine. I repeatedly thanked him and then moved along.
On the street he had pointed out to me were many places to eat, just like he said there would be. I wasn't in a mood to be picky and sat down at the first one I found.

A lady wearing an apron approached me and said something that I didn't understand. I smiled shyly and handed her the paper. She read it and nodded. Disappearing into the back I could only hope that she was going to return with some food.

While I waited, I looked around. There were some foreign condiments, napkins and a container of chopsticks on the table. I had always liked using chopsticks, but I had never actually used them in a country where they were the norm.

I took a set of chopsticks out of the container and removed the paper covering. The chopsticks were stuck together and I thought they were defective. I set them on the table and took out another set. How odd, they were stuck together too, as was the next pair, and the next, and the next. I emptied the entire container, but there wasn't a good pair in the lot, so I moved to the next table. Again, they were all deformed, so I moved to another table. I couldn’t find a good pair in the entire place. I laughed to myself; they must be made in Taiwan.

The lady finally returned from the kitchen and walked toward my table. She suddenly stopped and with wide eyes looked around. The happy face she had worn now turned to a frown and she began to yell. I looked behind me to see who she was yelling at, but there was no one there. People here sure are strange, I thought.

As she continued to yell it became apparent that it was directed at me, but why? Then she stopped directly across from me and picked up one of the pairs of useless chopsticks. Grasping each of the connected sticks from the bottom, she pulled. With a snap they separated and she handed them to me.

My face must have been pretty red when I realized what I had done. What an idiot, I thought. When I left I made sure to leave a huge tip that I hoped would cover the cost of the wasted chopsticks. If it didn't, at least she would have a good story to tell her friends. And I had learned my first lesson about eating out.
questions: Why was the author’s stomach making grumbling sounds?
What did the man on the street do for the author?
What did the author think was wrong with the chopsticks?
14.translation: 那是我到台灣的第一天。我走在街上，肚子咕嚕咕嚕叫。我餓到什麼都吃得下，但找不到任何看起來像餐廳的地方。一切都如此不同。我該怎麼辦？一名男子看見我捧著肚子、像迷路一樣走來走去。他停下來問我是不是有問題。我很高興他會說英文，一時之間甚至沒有回答，只能握著他的手，感謝他停下來。我告訴他我來自外國，不知道去哪裡吃、也不知道吃什麼。他指著前方一條街，告訴我那裡有很多吃東西的地方。接著他從口袋裡拿出一張小紙片，潦草地寫了幾個中文字交給我。他告訴我，只要把這個拿給廚師看，我就沒問題了。我一再感謝他，然後繼續往前走。在他指給我的那條街上，果然有很多吃東西的地方。我沒有心情挑剔，就在找到的第一家坐下。一位穿圍裙的女士走近我，說了一些我聽不懂的話。我害羞地笑了笑，把紙交給她。她看了紙後點點頭，消失到後面。我只能希望她回來時會帶著食物。等待時，我四處張望。桌上有一些陌生的調味料、餐巾紙和一個筷子筒。我一直喜歡用筷子，但從未真正在筷子是日常工具的國家使用過。我從筒子裡拿出一雙筷子，拆掉紙套。筷子黏在一起，我以為它們壞了。我把它放在桌上，又拿出另一雙。真奇怪，這雙也黏在一起，下一雙、再下一雙也一樣。我把整個筒子都清空了，卻沒有一雙好的，所以移到下一張桌子。結果又全都變形。我又換到另一張桌子。整間店都找不到一雙好的筷子。我心裡笑著想：它們一定是台灣製造的。那位女士終於從廚房回來，走向我的桌子。她突然停下來，睜大眼睛四處看。原本開心的表情變成皺眉，並開始大叫。我往身後看她在罵誰，但後面沒有人。我想，這裡的人真奇怪。她繼續叫時，很明顯是對著我，但為什麼？接著她停在我正對面，拿起一雙沒用的筷子。她抓住連在一起的兩支筷子底部，用力一拉。啪的一聲，它們分開了，然後她把筷子交給我。當我明白自己做了什麼時，我的臉一定很紅。我心想，真是個笨蛋。離開時，我特地留下很多小費，希望足以補償被浪費的筷子費用。如果不夠，至少她會有一個好故事可以告訴朋友。而我也學到了在外吃飯的第一課。
answers: It was making grumbling sounds because he was hungry.
He told him where to find some restaurants and wrote something on a piece of paper for the author to give to the waitress.
He thought they were all defective because they were connected on the end.

14.title: Washing the Car
paras: "Why do I always have to wash the car?" I screamed.
"Because you do such a good job," my father replied, as he sat at his desk fumbling through papers.
"Billy never has to do anything," I whined. "It's just not fair."
"Life isn't fair, Jill," he calmly stated. "And the sooner you learn that, the sooner my car will get washed."
Out to the garage I stomped thinking about how badly he treats me sometimes. Maybe he isn't my real father, I thought.
I took a bucket off the shelf and filled it with water and a little soap.
"Where is the darned sponge?" I shouted out loud thinking that I was by myself.
"Next to the toolbox," a voice answered.
Surprised, I turned to see who had answered me. It was my older brother, Billy.
I spun back around and found the sponge, right where he said it was.
"Why don't you take a hike, Billy?" I rudely asked hoping that he would just leave me alone.
"What's your problem? Who peed in your cornflakes?" he asked while his smile disappeared.
"I don't get it. Why doesn't that old fart ever ask you to do anything?" I asked looking for sympathy.
"Are you nuts?" he snapped. "He never lets me have a second to myself. He always has me running some stupid errand."
My question seemed to have hit a nerve with him.
"Who do you think takes out the garbage?” he continued. “Who do you think sweeps the garage? Who do you think mows the lawn? Who do you think does just about everything around this stinking place?"
"Ok. Ok. Take a deep breath and calm down," I said trying to cool him off, but it wasn't working.
His face grew more and more red. I could feel he was about to blow his top. Now I wished that I had kept my mouth shut.

"Listen, you poor spoiled little brat!" he yelled while shaking his finger at me in obvious anger. "If you ever want to change all my jobs for your once-a-week car washing, you just let me know. Until then, you better wake up and realize just how easy you have it around here!"
And then he was gone.
"See ya, wouldn't wanna be ya," I whispered as he left.
I picked up my bucket and sponge and slowly began wiping the windshield of the car. I also thanked God that Psycho Boy was gone. Maybe washing the car once a week wasn’t so bad after all.
questions: Why was Jill upset?
What made Billy snap?
Who actually did more chores around the house?
14.translation: 「為什麼總是我得洗車？」我尖叫道。「因為你洗得這麼好，」爸爸坐在書桌前翻著文件回答。「比利從來不用做任何事，」我抱怨。「這根本不公平。」「人生本來就不公平，吉兒，」他平靜地說。「而你越早學會這點，我的車就越早洗好。」我跺著腳走向車庫，心裡想著他有時對我多麼不好。也許他不是我的親生爸爸，我想。我從架子上拿下水桶，裝滿水和一點肥皂。「那該死的海綿在哪裡？」我以為只有自己一個人，便大聲喊道。「在工具箱旁邊，」一個聲音回答。我嚇了一跳，轉頭看看是誰回答我。原來是哥哥比利。我又轉回去，發現海綿就在他說的地方。「你幹嘛不走開，比利？」我粗魯地問，希望他別來煩我。「你有什麼毛病？誰惹到你了？」他問，臉上的笑容消失了。「我不懂。那個老傢伙為什麼從來不叫你做事？」我想尋求同情地問。「你瘋了嗎？」他突然發火。「他從來不讓我有一秒自己的時間。他總是叫我跑一些愚蠢的腿。」我的問題似乎戳到他的痛處。「你以為是誰倒垃圾？」他繼續說。「你以為是誰掃車庫？你以為是誰割草？你以為這個臭地方幾乎所有事都是誰做的？」「好，好。深呼吸，冷靜一點，」我試著讓他消氣，但沒有用。他的臉越來越紅。我能感覺到他快要爆炸了。現在我真希望自己剛才閉嘴。「聽著，你這個被寵壞的小鬼！」他明顯生氣地一邊搖手指一邊吼。「如果你想把我所有工作拿來換你一週一次的洗車，隨時告訴我。在那之前，你最好清醒一點，知道你在這裡過得有多輕鬆！」然後他就走了。「再見啦，還好我不是你，」我在他離開時小聲說。我拿起水桶和海綿，慢慢開始擦車子的擋風玻璃。我也感謝上帝，那個瘋狂男孩離開了。也許一週洗一次車其實沒那麼糟。
answers: She was upset because she thought she had to do everything around the house and her brother didn’t have to do anything.
He snapped when Jill asked why he was never asked to do anything.
Billy actually did more of the chores around the house.

14.title: My Zipper is STUCK!
paras: It's a cool winter day outside, but still the classroom is quite warm. Brett sits in his classroom with his winter coat zipped all the way to the top. His teacher, Mr. White, enters the room, glances at him and then continues on to his desk. Halfway there, he stops dead in his tracks, raises a brow and turns back to look at Brett who now has sweat dripping off the end of his nose.
"Is everything okay?" Brett’s teacher asks.
Brett, not wanting to make a scene, replies, "Sure, never better."
"Do you have the flu or something?" his teacher curiously inquires.
"Nope," he exclaims. "I’m as healthy as a horse."

Unsatisfied, Mr. White goes to his desk, sits down and proceeds to organize the papers on his desk. He stops abruptly, takes a deep breath and looks up at Brett again. His curiosity has got the best of him.
"Okay. Okay. What’s up with the jacket?" Mr. White asks.
"If you really must know, I zipped up my jacket too far and the zipper got stuck," Brett replies reluctantly.
Mr. White walks over to see if he can be of assistance. He gives the zipper a little tug and miraculously it easily unzips.
"That wasn’t so difficult." Mr. White chuckles and then the whole class bursts into laughter.

"My god, I have been trying to get this stupid thing off for an hour!" Brett, now half embarrassed and half relieved, shouts. He quickly rips off his jacket and throws it on his desk, revealing a sweat-covered shirt underneath. Then they sweaty boy sighs deeply, "Ahh! That is so much better."
The laughter eventually dies down and the class begins. Maybe next time Brett will not be so reluctant to ask for help when he needs it.
questions: Why is Mr. White curious about Brett?
Why is Brett’s zipper done up?
How did Mr. White help Brett?
14.translation: 外面是涼爽的冬日，但教室裡仍然相當暖和。布雷特坐在教室裡，冬季外套的拉鍊一路拉到最上面。他的老師懷特先生走進教室，瞥了他一眼，然後繼續走向書桌。走到一半時，他突然停住，挑起眉毛，轉身看著布雷特。此時汗水正從布雷特的鼻尖滴下。「一切還好嗎？」老師問。布雷特不想引人注意，回答：「當然，好得不能再好了。」「你是得了流感還是怎樣？」老師好奇地問。「沒有，」他大聲說。「我健康得很。」懷特先生不太滿意，走到書桌前坐下，開始整理桌上的文件。他突然停下來，深吸一口氣，又抬頭看著布雷特。他的好奇心終於勝過了他。「好吧，好吧。那件外套是怎麼回事？」懷特先生問。「如果你真的非知道不可，我把外套拉鍊拉得太高，然後卡住了，」布雷特不情願地回答。懷特先生走過去看看能不能幫忙。他輕輕拉了一下拉鍊，奇蹟般地很容易就拉開了。「那沒那麼難嘛。」懷特先生輕笑，接著全班爆笑。「我的天啊，我已經試著把這個蠢東西脫下來一個小時了！」布雷特半尷尬半鬆了一口氣地喊。他很快脫下外套，把它丟在桌上，露出底下被汗浸濕的襯衫。這個滿身汗的男孩深深嘆了一口氣：「啊！好多了。」笑聲最後漸漸停下，課也開始了。也許下次布雷特需要幫助時，就不會那麼不願意開口了。
answers: He is curious because it is warm in the classroom and yet Brett is wearing his jacket zipped all the way to the top.
It is done up because he zipped it up too far and it got stuck.
He helped by unzipping Brett’s jacket for him.

14.title: Putting Things Off
paras: It is Sunday in the Liu household and that means it’s time to clean the house. Every Sunday, Randy's mother gives each of her children a couple of chores to do. But once the chores are finished, the children are allowed to do whatever they want.

Paula, Randy’s oldest sister, has been assigned to do the laundry. She doesn't mind the job at all. The job is quite simple. She just has to gather the dirty laundry from all the bedrooms, sort it into piles (darks, colors, whites, etc.) and then load the washing machine. While she is waiting for the clothes to wash, she folds the clean laundry that was washed on Saturday. It is not a difficult job and she happily goes about her work. Within an hour, Paula is finished and can leave the house to play with her friends.
Meanwhile, Randy has found a comfortable bench to sit on. His job is easy, too. He only has to take out the garbage, but it can wait. He wants to relax for a while long before he starts.

In the kitchen, Rhonda, his younger sister, is doing the dishes. Her job is dirty and not so much fun, but she knows the faster she works, the faster she can go outside. She makes the job even easier by listening to her MP3 player and singing along to it. Within half an hour, she, too, is finished.

An hour after his sisters have finished and left the house, Randy is still sitting on the bench. He has his legs crossed and he's twiddling his thumbs in a daze. He hates taking out the garbage and doesn't want to, but he knows he can't leave until it is done. Just a minute more and I'll do it, he thinks.
Three hours later, Randy wakes with someone tapping him on the shoulder. It's his mother and she asks if he is finished, yet. Randy shakes his head no and stands to finally go and take out the trash.

After about 4 hours of putting it off and another hour of reluctantly doing his chores, he is finally finished. Now he can go to the park and play basketball with his friends… if they are still there.

On his way out the door, he runs into his younger sister. She tells him that she had garbage duty last week and it only took her 20 minutes. She tries to make him understand that he would have more time for fun if he wouldn’t put off his chores for so long But Randy doesn’t listen. He just ignores her and continues on out the door.

Now the day is almost gone and it is already starting to get dark. When Randy finally gets to the park, he finds that his friends have already left. He is very disappointed. Maybe next time I should do my chores faster, he thinks.
questions: What do the kids have to do every Sunday before they can play?
Whose job is the easiest? Why?
Does Randy get to play basketball with his friends? Why or why not?
14.translation: 星期天到了劉家，這表示該打掃房子了。每個星期天，藍迪的媽媽都會分配每個孩子幾項家事。只要家事完成，孩子們就可以做任何想做的事。寶拉是藍迪的大姊，被分配去洗衣服。她一點也不介意這份工作。工作很簡單，她只要從所有臥室收集髒衣服，分成幾堆（深色、彩色、白色等），然後放進洗衣機。等衣服洗的時候，她折星期六洗好的乾淨衣服。這不是困難的工作，她很開心地做著。一小時內，寶拉完成了，可以離開家去找朋友玩。與此同時，藍迪找到一張舒服的長椅坐下。他的工作也很簡單，只需要倒垃圾，但那可以等等。他想在開始前先放鬆一會兒。在廚房裡，他的妹妹朗達正在洗碗。她的工作很髒，也不太好玩，但她知道做得越快，就越快能出去。她聽著 MP3 播放器並跟著唱，讓工作變得更容易。半小時內，她也完成了。姊妹們完成並離家一小時後，藍迪仍然坐在長椅上。他翹著腳，發呆地玩著手指。他討厭倒垃圾，也不想做，但知道沒做完就不能離開。再一分鐘我就做，他想。三小時後，藍迪被人拍肩膀叫醒。是他的媽媽，她問他完成了沒有。藍迪搖頭，終於站起來去倒垃圾。拖了大約四小時，又不情願地花了一小時做家事後，他終於完成了。現在他可以去公園和朋友打籃球了，如果他們還在那裡的話。他出門時遇到妹妹。妹妹告訴他，上週她負責倒垃圾，只花了二十分鐘。她試著讓他明白，如果不要把家事拖那麼久，他會有更多時間玩。但藍迪不聽，只是無視她，繼續出門。現在一天快結束了，天也開始黑了。當藍迪終於到公園時，發現朋友們已經離開。他非常失望。也許下次我應該更快做完家事，他想。
answers: They have to do their chores first.
Randy’s job is the easiest because he only has to take out the garbage.
He doesn’t get to because they have already left by the time he finally gets to the park.

14.title: A Bunun Carving
paras: The massive wooden carving shown above was made by a Bunun artist. It is of several Bunun men standing in a circle with their arms interlocked—kind of like a big group hug. This pose is a typical one and is often seen in all kinds of Bunun art.

All the men are depicted in traditional clothing that is mostly white and black with a wide patterned strip wrapping around their wastes and down their backs. They also don headbands made of long scarf-like cloths that they tie in the back. But no shoes or socks for these guys; barefoot was the way to go back then (and still is for some).

I think this sculpture really shows how closely bonded the Bunun people must’ve been and perhaps still are. Their sense of community is great and this piece definitely represents the unity that they have. Maybe we should all learn from them and have a big group hug with our neighbors once in a while. It might not solve all our differences, but it may unite some of us at least.
questions: What is this carving made from?
In what shape do the men stand in?
Why is this pose “typical”?
What kind of clothes are they wearing?
Why should we have a big group hug with our neighbors?
14.translation: 上方展示的巨大木雕是由一位布農族藝術家製作的。作品中有幾位布農族男子站成一圈，手臂互相扣住，有點像一個大型團體擁抱。這種姿勢很典型，常出現在各種布農族藝術中。所有男子都被描繪成穿著傳統服飾，衣服大多是白色和黑色，腰間和背後有一條寬寬的圖案布帶。他們也戴著由長條圍巾狀布料做成、在後方綁起來的頭帶。不過這些人沒有鞋子也沒有襪子；在當時赤腳才是生活方式（對有些人來說現在仍然如此）。我認為這件雕塑真的展現了布農族人曾經，也或許現在仍然擁有的緊密連結。他們的群體意識很強，而這件作品確實代表了他們擁有的團結。也許我們都應該向他們學習，偶爾和鄰居來個大團體擁抱。這也許不能解決所有差異，但至少可能讓我們中的一些人更加團結。
answers: It is made from wood.
They stand in a circle.
It is typical because it is often seen in Bunun art.
They are wearing traditional Bunun clothes.
We should do this to improve our relationships with them and maybe develop a bond.
